measurements._ai_reference_used: Contractor WALL REF bars (324" front/back, 444" left, 180"/84" right, 48" corners) plus per-photo WIN REF window anchors
measurements._ai_story_count_reasoning: All 8 photos independently reported 1.5 stories (main floor plus shed-dormer upper half-story).

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows a full gable-end wall with two garage doors facing the driveway.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: WALL REF 324" spans ~1105 px (3.41 px/in); side eave/soffit returns sit ~440 px above grade -> ~129 in ~ 10.8 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-end front of a garage structure; two roof wings/eave returns visible at left and right imply adjoining walls. Window scaled to WIN REF 48" anchor (165 px = 48"). Garage door heights read ~6 ft from photo — may be foreshortened slightly by camera height; standard 7 ft possible. Roof surface not visible from this angle, so roof_hex null. No masonry on this wall; stone steps at right are landscaping, not wall cladding.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Long left wall with firewood stack recedes toward garage doors on the front wall; matches the FRONT-LEFT ELEVATION banner and WALL REF = 48" annotation.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Scaled off contractor 4' (48" / 4.00 ft) reference bar at x=0.426; main-roof eave to grade near the bar's depth measures ~2.4 bar lengths (~115 in ≈ 9.5 ft). Upper clerestory/second-story eave sits ~4 ft above the main roof plane per the bar itself.
_ai_photos.notes: Upper level appears to be a shed-roof clerestory/second-story pop-up rather than a discrete dormer; its 4 ft wall-above-roof height is directly anchored by the contractor 48" bar. Along-wall positions on the left wall estimated against the 444" (37 ft) span noted for photo 3; far-end positions carry perspective error. Front wall garage-door positions not estimable due to steep foreshortening (along_wall_ft null). Firewood/ladder hide the lower wall; possible openings behind stack cannot be ruled out. Brick section at far right belongs to an adjacent structure/breezeway — flagged as no-siding brick.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and matches the annotation set's 444" wall ref for the left side.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Wall ref bar (444" over ~1299 px, ~0.342 in/px) applied vertically: main eave to visible grade ≈ 265-285 px ≈ 92-98", but grade is partially hidden by the woodpile so read is approximate (~9 ft).
_ai_photos.notes: Scale anchored to contractor 37' (444"/37.00 ft) reference bar spanning ~1299 px (~0.342 in/px); dormer 2SL windows checked against the 36" WIN REF (vertical blue span ≈ 95 px on the dormer window). All 5 contractor yellow pins captured (3 DH first floor, 2 2SL on dormer). User annotation of ~49 ft² SHAKE on left elevation attributed to the shed dormer face — wall is deeply shaded so profile there could not be visually confirmed. Woodpile prevents confirming exact grade and any low openings behind it; brick visible at far right belongs to the adjacent front/right wall, not this elevation.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels this REAR-LEFT and the view shows the left gable wall plus the back wall receding to the right toward the driveway.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Scaled off the patio door (~80" tall, ~250 px) on the left gable wall: eave corner at right of gable sits ~380 px above grade ≈ 120" ≈ 9.5-10 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: Contractor 48" reference bar (x=0.708, y=0.231-0.351, ~127 px) gives ~2.65 px/in on the rear upper-story plane; window rl-w4 width confirmed at 36" by the WIN REF = 36" bar. Left wall dims scaled off the patio door plane (~3.1 px/in). The rear upper wall above the lower roof band reads as a shed-dormer/step-back second story — flagged as 1 rear dormer; reconcile with back-elevation photo. Foundation band (dark red concrete) exposed ~1-2 ft below siding line. Along-wall positions on the back wall are approximate due to smoker/wood-pile obstruction and foreshortening.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is banner-labeled BACK ELEVATION and shows a full gable-end wall with patio door matching the back annotation description (WALL REF=324", brick no-siding zone, two 2SL pins).
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Using WALL REF 324" over ~915 px (0.354 in/px, cross-checked against 80" patio door = ~225 px), the side-eave soffit return (~y445) to grade (~y775) spans ~330 px = ~117" = ~9.8 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: Prompt says index 4 but photo content matches the 'Photo 5: BACK ELEVATION' annotation set (324" wall ref, brick no-siding box, two contractor-tagged 2-Lite Sliders) — likely 0-based indexing. Gable window partly covered by WIN REF label; width estimated from visible frame edges. Pitch measures closer to ~7/12 between the allowed 6/12 and 8/12 buckets. Siding is narrow horizontal lap, same red board as other elevations; no shake visible on this back wall.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is banner-labeled REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION and shows a corner view with a gabled end wall (patio door) and a lower shaded rear section receding to the left.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Contractor 84" (7 ft) vertical bar runs from just above grade/foundation line to the eave of the lower rear section; adding ~1 ft of exposed foundation below the bar gives ~8 ft grade-to-eave on that section.
_ai_photos.notes: WIN REF = 48" (blue) anchors rr-w2 width at ~48"; WALL REF 84" bar (x=0.384, y=0.425-0.638) used for eave height. Vertical measurements scaled off contractor 7' (84") reference bar; features on the gable wall are farther from camera than the bar, so a secondary scale from the standard-height patio door (~80") was used for gable-wall openings. Contractor-tagged SHAKE ~14 ft² on rear-right included as accent though the visible cladding reads as red horizontal lap; the shake region may be hidden in shadow or on the far corner return. Small window rr-w3 partially obscured by tree — low confidence, size approximate.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo banner reads RIGHT ELEVATION and shows a long side wall with a shed dormer; contractor pins/refs match the right-elevation annotation set (180" wall ref, three 2SL pins).
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Scaled off contractor 15' (180"/15.00 ft) reference bar (~2.53 px/in): lower eave line at ~y600 to grade at ~y840 is ~240 px ≈ 95 in ≈ 8 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: Prompt index says 6 (rear-right) but the photo banner and annotation content (180" wall ref, 36" win ref, three 2SL pins) match the RIGHT elevation description from photo 7 — reconciler should be aware of this mismatch. Lower windows are heavily occluded by shrubs so their width/height are approximate (measured off the 180" bar at ~2.53 px/in; dormer window cross-checked against the ~36" blue win-ref line). Wall extends behind trees to the right; visible span ~38 ft but true right-rear corner not visible.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-RIGHT ELEVATION and the photo shows the front gable/garage wall plus the right side wall with shed dormer at a corner angle.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Garage door (~7 ft standard) tops out just below the front eave line; adding header/trim puts the eave near 9 ft. Elevated angle limits precision.
_ai_photos.notes: Scale anchored to contractor 4' (48") red WALL REF bar at dormer corner (~197 px = 48") and 36" blue WIN REF on the gable window. Right-wall window sizes scaled off the 48" bar but are foreshortened — treat as lower confidence. Two garage doors on front (one partially cropped at frame edge); entry door has diamond-grid glazing. Shed dormer sits on the right roof plane. Contractor-tagged 16 sq ft SHAKE region included per ground truth even though the visible cladding reads as horizontal lap in this frame; along_wall_ft for right-wall openings left null due to steep foreshortening.

_ai_pin_gap_hints.message: Dormer on the left slope reports 15.0 ft wide but NO reference marker was in frame. Unanchored dormer widths drift 25-90% — re-shoot the left elevation with a WALL_REF or WIN_REF bar in frame before quoting.

Dormer on the right slope reports 42.0 ft wide but NO reference marker was in frame. Unanchored dormer widths drift 25-90% — re-shoot the right elevation with a WALL_REF or WIN_REF bar in frame before quoting.

Rejected — front-right corner shot >45° off-axis with elevated camera; foreshortening compresses shed-dormer width

Direct 42 ft vs corner 10 ft disagree by >1 ft; kept the direct reading but flagged direct_disagreement — contractor should verify since 42 ft is markedly larger than the left dormer's 15 ft. Knee wall assumed to match the 4-ft height measured on the left dormer.

openings._reconciliation_note: Contractor-pinned gable window; photo 0 at 13.4 ft (WIN REF 48" anchored) merged with photo 7's foreshortened 22×36 read at 8 ft — photo 0 direct view wins on size and position.
Photo 0 at 7.1 ft, photo 7 at 3 ft (foreshortened), photo 1 null → same left garage door; photo 0 direct sizes kept; photos 1/7 read 84" height so 7-ft standard is plausible.
Photo 0 at 18.0 ft, photo 7 at 14 ft (foreshortened), photo 1 null → same right garage door; photo 0 direct sizes kept.
Photo 0 at 24.3 ft, photo 7 at 22 ft — only one entry door seen by both front views; merged, photo 0 direct sizes kept.
Photo 2 (contractor pin) at 8.5 ft merged with photo 1's fl-main-w1 at 4.5 ft — photo 1's far-end positions carry documented perspective error; direct pinned view wins.
Photo 2 (contractor pin) at 17.5 ft merged with photo 1's fl-main-w2 at 11.5 ft (same twin DH set, corner-shot position error).
Photo 2 (contractor pin) at 26.7 ft merged with photo 3's rl-w3 (30×60, foreshortened rear-corner read of the same third DH); direct pinned size kept.

Left-dormer twin #1: photo 2 pin at 14.7 ft merged with photo 1's fl-upper-w1 at 9 ft (corner error). Twin preserved — see left-d2 at 20.5 ft, separation 5.8 ft > sum-of-widths 8 ft is not met but pins confirm two distinct units.

_ai_photos.notes: Contractor 84" bar (x≈540 px, y 448-672 px) used for scale; scale is only strictly valid at the near corner plane, so patio door was reported at standard 72x80 since its raw pixel read (~36x62) is clearly depth-compressed. Gable wall reads ~16-20 ft wide. Contractor annotation lists 14 ft² of SHAKE on this rear-right elevation but the visible cladding reads as horizontal lap; shake region may be hidden by trees or in shadow — included per ground truth. Left half of house completely hidden by large tree, so no readings for that section.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo banner labeled 'RIGHT ELEVATION' and content matches the described right-side shot with three contractor 2SL pins and a 180" wall reference bar.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Scaled off contractor 15' (180"/2.52 px-per-inch) reference bar: lower eave line at ~y=595 px, grade at ~y=855 px = ~260 px = ~103" = ~8.6 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: Structure reads as a story-and-a-half with a wide shed-dormer-style sided section above the lower roof on the right plane. Both lower windows are heavily occluded by shrubs so their sizes are estimates scaled from the 180" reference bar (2.52 px/in); widths/heights could be off by ~10-15%. Contractor tags all three pins as 2-Lite Slider (taken as ground truth). Visible siding is red horizontal lap; the user-tagged 57 ft² SHAKE region on this elevation was not visually distinguishable, likely hidden by trees or in the dormer/upper band.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-RIGHT ELEVATION and the photo shows the front gable/garage wall plus the receding right side wall from an elevated corner vantage.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Garage door height (~84 in standard) spans ~350 px near the front corner (~4.17 px/in); front-right eave sits ~420 px above grade at the entry door, giving ~100 in ≈ 8.5 ft eave.
_ai_photos.notes: Contractor 48" vertical bar (WALL REF) anchors the upper-right wall section: bar spans ~197 px = 48" (~4.1 px/in in that plane). WIN REF = 36" vertical span used for the front gable window height. The raised red wall at upper right (with the 48" bar and slider window) reads as a shed-dormer/second-story side wall rising above the main right roof plane; reconciler should cross-check with photo 7 (right elevation). along_wall_ft left null for right-wall openings due to severe foreshortening and hidden corners. No masonry visible; all walls horizontal lap. Shake 16 sqft accent included per contractor ground-truth even though the region reads as lap at this resolution.

_ai_pin_gap_hints.message: Dormer on the left slope reports 15.5 ft wide but NO reference marker was in frame. Unanchored dormer widths drift 25-90% — re-shoot the left elevation with a WALL_REF or WIN_REF bar in frame before quoting.

Dormer on the right slope reports 16.5 ft wide but NO reference marker was in frame. Unanchored dormer widths drift 25-90% — re-shoot the right elevation with a WALL_REF or WIN_REF bar in frame before quoting.

openings._reconciliation_note: Contractor-pinned gable window on direct front photo (48" WIN REF). Photo 7's 'front-w1' 22×36 @9 ft and photo 1's 'front-upper-w1' 24×48 @null are corner-distorted reads of this same single gable window — the direct, contractor-pinned front elevation shows exactly one front window, so both merged despite size scatter.

Photo 0 @7.1 ft, photo 7 @3 ft (corner foreshortening), photo 1 @null → merged; four separate doors is physically impossible on a 27 ft wall (415" of doors), and every photo sees exactly two. Direct photo 0 sizes/positions win.
Photo 0 @18.1 ft, photo 7 @13 ft, photo 1 @null → same second garage door; direct photo 0 wins on size/position.
Photo 0 @24.3 ft vs photo 7 @22 ft (delta 2.3 ft, just past ±2 but sizes match within 2" and only one entry door exists on the direct front view) → merged; direct photo 0 wins.
Photo 2 @8.5 ft (contractor DH pin), photo 1 'left-lower-w1' @6 ft (corner compression) → same window; NOT a twin — separation pattern matches the three-DH rhythm of the direct left view.

Photo 2 @17.5 ft (contractor DH pin), photo 1 'left-lower-w2' @12 ft merged as the middle window of the corner shot's compressed 6/12/19 sequence mapping onto the direct 8.5/17.5/26.8 sequence; contractor pins fix count at 3 lower windows.

Photo 2 @26.8 ft (contractor pin); photo 1 'left-lower-w3' @19 ft and photo 3 'rl-w3' DH 30×60 @null (rear-left corner) merged — contractor pinned exactly 3 DH on the direct left elevation, and rl-w3 matches size/style at the rear end of the wall.

Contractor-pinned dormer slider; photo 1 'left-dormer-w1' @10 ft merged (corner compression). Kept as one of TWO dormer sliders — its twin at 20.6 ft is preserved separately (delta 5.9 ft > sum-of-widths).

Twin of left-d1-w1, kept separately per contractor pin count (2 sliders on dormer). Photo 1 'left-dormer-w2' @14 ft and photo 3 'rl-w4' 36×36 @null (rear-left view of upper wall) merged into this rearmost dormer slider.

Contractor-pinned on direct back photo @7.2 ft; photo 3 'rl-w1'/'rl-w2' @10.5/7 ft and photo 5 'back-w1' @4.7 ft are corner-compressed reads of this same lower window — the direct pinned back elevation fixes the back wall at exactly 2 windows + 1 patio door.
Contractor-pinned gable window @13.4 ft on direct back photo; photo 5 'back-w-gable1' 48×32 @9.0 ft merged (corner-shot origin/scale shift, same lone gable window).

Photo 4 @20.1 ft (direct), photo 3 'rl-d1' @17 ft, photo 5 'back-d1' @12.3 ft — position scatter from corner-shot origin flips, but all three photos see exactly one patio door on the back wall → merged, direct size 72×80 kept.
Contractor-pinned dormer slider on direct right elevation; photo 7 'right-w1' (on_dormer, 36×30, @null) merged as the same dormer window seen from the front-right corner.
Contractor-pinned @11.7 ft; photo 7 'right-w2' @null merged (front portion of right wall visible from front-right). Twin at 24.8 ft preserved separately — delta 13.1 ft.

Matched-pair twin of right-w2, kept per contractor pin count (3 pins on right elevation). Photo 5 'right-w1-partial' @null and photo 7 'right-w3' @null merged into this rear lower window rather than emitted as extras — direct pinned photo 6 fixes the right wall at exactly 3 windows.

_reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Confidence-weighted average of final per-wall heights (front 8.5, back 9.3, left 9.0, right 8.5) = 8.9 ft; photo 0's 12 ft rejected (gable-end rule) and photo 5's 8.1 ft rejected (corner-plane scale only).
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: Gable triangles on front (photos 0,7) and back (photos 3,4,5) — front-to-back ridge; shed dormers reported on left slope (photos 1,2) and right slope (photos 6,7) with dormers_observed_count > 0 → gable-shed-dormer.
_reconciliation_notes.dormers: Detected 2 shed dormers, one per side slope. Left: photo 2 direct width 15.5 ft kept, photo 1's 18 ft corner reading rejected (single direct reading, amber). Right: photo 6 direct width 16.5 ft kept, photo 7's 11 ft corner reading rejected (single direct reading, amber). Faces differ so never collapsed.
_reconciliation_notes.story_count: 7 of 8 photos read 1.5 stories; photo 4's straight-on back view couldn't see the side dormers so its '1' was outvoted → 1.5.
_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: Total wall+gable+dormer area ≈ 1,500 sqft; contractor-marked brick band on back (~42 sqft), rear-corner brick chimney (photo 1's ~90 sqft attributed to chimney not wall cladding), photo 5's 25 sqft stone attributed to the outdoor kitchen → ~94% siding. All shake regions (user ground truth, 163 sqft total) count as siding accents, not exclusions.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: Siding/trim from photo 4 (sunlit, direct back, highest confidence 88); door from photo 3 (only sunlit door sample); roof from photo 3 as the median of the three sunlit roof samples (#6E6B66 between #3C4046 and #8B8E8F).
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: 39 raw openings across 8 photos → 15 after dedup. Key merges: 2 garage doors (not 4 — 415" of doors can't fit a 27 ft wall; photo 0's direct positions win over photo 7's corner reads), 1 patio door (photos 3/4/5 position scatter from origin flips), left wall pinned at 3 DH + 2 dormer sliders per photo 2's contractor pins (absorbing photo 1's compressed positions and photo 3's null-position rl-w3/rl-w4), right wall pinned at 3 sliders per photo 6's pins. Twins preserved: 2 left-dormer sliders (14.7/20.6 ft), 2 right lower 44×32 sliders (11.7/24.8 ft), 2 garage doors (7.1/18.1 ft).

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows a full-face gable-end wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
photos.eave_reasoning: Anchored vertical scale to WALL REF (324" = ~1105 px, 3.41 px/in) cross-checked with garage door height; side-eave return at left corner sits ~143 in above grade, ~12 ft.
photos.notes: Gable-end front wall, 324" (27 ft) wide per contractor ref. Whole face is red horizontal lap siding including the gable triangle; white fascia/soffit/trim. Contractor-tagged 2SL pin corresponds to the single gable window (front-w1), sized with the 48" WIN REF. Garage door heights read slightly short (~71 in) via horizontal scale due to elevated camera angle; reported 84 in as they visually match standard 7 ft doors relative to grade. No masonry, no dormers, no shake visible on this elevation despite shake tagged on other elevations.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-LEFT; long left wall with woodpile recedes left while the front wall with garage doors and brick faces right.
photos.eave_reasoning: Scaled off contractor 4' (48" / 4.00 ft) reference bar (~137 px = 48"); main-wall eave-to-grade at the left wall reads ~330 px ≈ 115" ≈ 9.5 ft.
photos.notes: Corner shot: left wall well seen but lower openings partially obscured by woodpile/ladder; front wall heavily foreshortened so garage-door widths use nominal 9-ft estimate. Upper set-back half-story treated as large shed-dormer face on the left roof; contractor-tagged SHAKE ~11 sqft included as accent. raw_ai.reference_used: Contractor taped WALL REFs (324" front/back, 444" left, 180" right partial) and WIN REFs on every photo, cross-checked with 80" door heights
raw_ai.story_count_reasoning: 7 of 8 photos report 1.5 stories (main body with shed dormers); photo 3's '2' reflects a taller rear-left section, not a full second story.
raw_ai.roof_type_reasoning: Gable triangles observed head-on on front (photo 0) and back (photo 4) walls, and photos 2 and 6 each report a shed dormer (left and right slopes), upgrading to gable-shed-dormer.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct LEFT elevation, dormer face parallel to camera, twin 52in sliders fully visible on face
saw both upper 2SL windows on this dormer face (called it a clerestory) but corner angle too oblique for width
rejected — rear-left corner, left wall heavily foreshortened, no dormer width read

dormers._reconciliation_note: Face=left from the only cardinal shot (photo 2); single direct width of 17.5 ft consistent with twin 52in dormer sliders at 14.6/20.6 ft; no second direct view to cross-check so tagged single-reading.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct RIGHT elevation, dormer face and its 47in 2SL window fully visible, WIN REF on dormer window
rejected — front-right corner, right wall heavily foreshortened; reported 0 dormers but its 48x36 right-w1 matches this dormer's window

dormers._reconciliation_note: Face=right from the cardinal photo 6; single direct width 15.0 ft, dormer window at 17.9 ft on a ~37 ft wall gives ~-0.6 ft offset from centerline; different face from the left dormer so never collapsed with it.

walls.label: front
walls.confidence_reasoning: Head-on view with 324in WALL REF, but eave measured from a single photo via 80in door scale at dusk.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — head-on front elevation, eave measured at wall corners (not rake) with 80in entry-door vertical scale; grade visible
rejected — front wall heavily foreshortened at corner; its eave read applies to the left wall
rejected for eave (extractor returned null — shrubs hide grade, no valid vertical scale at eave plane); gable read 10.5 ft rejected in favor of photo 0's head-on 8.6 ft with WALL REF half-span

walls._reconciliation_note: Only photo 0 produced a valid corner-eave reading on this gable wall (10.7 ft, door-scaled) — single reading, unverified, amber; gable triangle disagreement (8.6 vs 10.5) resolved toward the head-on WALL-REF-anchored photo 0.

walls.label: back
walls.confidence_reasoning: Head-on 324in WALL REF photo with 80in patio-door scale, but three eave readings spread 1.2 ft and grade is partially planted-over.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — head-on back elevation, 80in patio door scale, 5in grade occlusion explicitly added
rejected — rear-left corner shot, back wall angled; disagrees with kept reading by 0.5 ft and with photo 5 by 1.2 ft
rejected — rear-right corner shot with sloping grade; 1.2 ft above photo 3, exceeding the 1 ft compatibility band

walls._reconciliation_note: Readings 8.4/8.9/9.6 span >1 ft so no averaging; kept photo 4 (head-on, patio-door scale, grade occlusion accounted) and flagged direct_disagreement; gable triangle also from photo 4 (8.7 ft).

walls.label: left
walls.confidence_reasoning: Full-span 444in WALL REF, five contractor pins, and two independent eave readings agreeing within 0.2 ft despite woodpile occlusion.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct LEFT elevation, WIN-REF vertical scale, measured at exposed corner with visible grade
kept — left wall reasonably square in front-left shot; grade_occluded=true but 38in hidden base was explicitly added, so the read is complete
rejected — its 8.4 ft eave read belongs to the back wall; left wall foreshortened with woodpile-hidden grade

walls._reconciliation_note: Two valid readings (9.6, 9.8) agree within 0.2 ft → median 9.7 ft, direct_consensus from 2 photos; photo 1's grade occlusion was verified as added-back, not truncated.

walls.label: right
walls.confidence_reasoning: Only one eave reading, trees hide the right third of the wall, and the 180in WALL REF covers only part of the span so width is copied from the left wall.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct RIGHT elevation, WIN-REF vertical scale on dormer window; 10in shrub-hidden base explicitly added
rejected — extractor correctly returned null: shrubs hide grade and the only vertical ref sits at a different camera depth

walls._reconciliation_note: Single direct reading of 10.4 ft kept (amber, uncorroborated); width assumed symmetric with the 444in left wall because photo 6's 180in WALL REF spans only part of the wall and trees hide the right end — flagged assumed_symmetric, not measured.

openings._reconciliation_note: Contractor-pinned gable 2SL on head-on photo 0 (WIN REF 48in anchored). Photo 1's 24x40 (null position) and photo 7's 24x36 @8 ft are foreshortened/wrong-plane reads of the same lone gable window — head-on photo 0 shows exactly one front window, so their sizes were rejected.

Photo 0 @7.0 ft, photo 1 @8 ft → same door (within 2 ft). Width 96in from WALL-REF-anchored photo 0; height 84in from photos 1+7 consensus since photo 0 self-flagged vertical compression on its 75in read.
Photo 0 @18.1 ft, photo 1 @18 ft → same door. Width 99in kept as measured from photo 0's WALL REF (no rounding); height 84in from photos 1+7 (photo 0's 75in flagged compressed).

Single entry door: photo 0 @24.1 ft vs photo 7 @19 ft — position delta exceeds 2 ft but photo 7 is an oblique elevated corner shot and both photos show exactly one entry door; kept photo 0's direct-ref 32x80.
Contractor-pinned DH. Photo 1's left-lower-w1 (30x52 @4.5 ft) is the same unit with corner-compressed position; photo 2 (direct, WALL REF 444in) wins on size and position.
Contractor-pinned DH; photo 1's left-lower-w2 (32x48 @12 ft) merged as the same unit (foreshortening compressed its position).
Contractor-pinned DH (pin overrides photo 1's '2SL' guess at conf 55). Photo 1's @22 ft and photo 3's 28x58 (null position, matching DH profile) merged as the same rear-most unit.

TWIN preserved: photo 2 emitted dormer sliders at 14.6 ft and 20.6 ft — delta 6.0 ft < sum-of-widths 8.7 ft, classic twin pair, both kept. Photo 1's upper-w1 (46x36 @11 ft) is this unit seen obliquely.

TWIN preserved (see left-d1sl). Photo 1's upper-w2 (42x36 @15 ft) and photo 3's low-conf 36x36 (null position) merged as oblique views of this same dormer slider; contractor's direct photo 2 shows exactly 5 left-wall openings.
Contractor-pinned. Photo 4 @7.0 ft, photo 5 @5.4 ft on back wall (delta 1.6 ft < 2 ft, widths 48 vs 48) → same window; photo 4's head-on size kept.
Contractor-pinned. Photo 4 @13.1 ft, photo 5 @11.4 ft (delta 1.7 ft, widths 43 vs 41) and photo 3 @13 ft → same window; photo 4 head-on size kept.

Single patio slider seen in three photos (photo 4 @20.0, photo 3 @14, photo 5 @16.1 — corner positions compressed by foreshortening); only one patio door exists on the back wall, kept photo 4's head-on 70x80.
Low-confidence small window on the lower rear section, seen only from rear-left; no match on head-on photo 4 (lower section may sit outside its frame) — kept as-is, unverified.
Tree-obscured small window on the shaded lower section; along_wall_ft null and size disagrees with photo 3's 30x24, so per rule 3c NOT deduped against it — false duplicate cheaper than a lost window.

Contractor-pinned dormer slider. Photo 7's right-w1 (48x36, null position) matches within 1in and is the only dormer-height window on this face → merged despite null along_wall (dormer-opening exception).
Contractor-pinned; shrub-occluded so size carries ±15%.
Contractor-pinned; shrub-occluded so size carries ±15%.
Null along_wall + size mismatch vs photo 6's lower windows (37x28/34x28) → not deduped per rule 3c; likely sits in the tree-hidden right third photo 6 could not see. Unverified.
Null along_wall, no size match on photo 6 → kept separate per rule 3c; possibly on the taller two-level rear-right section noted in photo 7. Unverified.

_reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Confidence-weighted average of final per-wall heights: front 10.7 (w=75), left 9.7 (w=80), back 8.9 (w=72), right 10.4 (w=62) → 2864.1/289 = 9.9 ft. Rejected reads: photo 3's 8.4 and photo 5's 9.6 on back (>1 ft mutual spread — kept head-on photo 4's 8.9), photo 1's front-wall attempt (foreshortened), photo 7 (null). No regression toward a typical height; the 8.9-to-10.7 spread across walls is retained as measured.
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: Gable triangles measured head-on on 2 walls (front photo 0, back photo 4); photos 2 and 6 each report dormers_observed_count=1 → gable-shed-dormer.
_reconciliation_notes.dormers: Detected 2 shed dormers: one on the LEFT slope (photo 2 direct, 17.5 ft, twin 52in sliders) and one on the RIGHT slope (photo 6 direct, 15.0 ft, single 47in slider). Different faces → never collapsed. Each has exactly one direct width reading → both tagged direct_single_reading (amber). Photo 1 confirmed the left dormer's window count; photo 7 rejected for the right dormer (foreshortened, reported 0 dormers).
_reconciliation_notes.story_count: 7 of 8 photos read 1.5 stories; photo 3's '2' reflects the taller rear-left section, not a full second story → 1.5 kept.
_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: Wall+gable+dormer area ≈ 1,646 sqft; brick exclusions ≈ 40 sqft on back (photo 4 hatched), ~30 sqft foundation band (photo 5, same back-wall skirt) and photo 1's ~100 sqft brick section (uncertain wall, counted at half weight) → ~95% siding.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: Siding/trim/roof from photo 4 (sunlit back elevation; roof #8A8D90 is the median of the three sunlit roof samples); door from photo 7 (best mixed-light sample — no sunlit door sample exists).
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: 39 raw openings across 8 photos → 19 after dedup. Key merges: 2 garage doors seen in 3 photos each (positions 7/8 and 18.1/18 ft agree); back patio door seen in 3 photos; left wall's 8 oblique reads (photos 1,3) collapsed onto photo 2's 5 contractor-pinned openings; right dormer window merged across photos 6+7 by 1in size match. Twins preserved: left dormer 52in sliders at 14.6/20.6 ft (delta 6.0 ft < 8.7 ft sum-of-widths). Kept un-deduped per rule 3c: photo 5's 13x23 and photo 3's 30x24 back windows (null/uncertain positions, size mismatch) and photo 7's two null-position right-wall windows (tree-hidden section). NOTE: per-photo extractions carried no bbox data, so all bboxes are zeroed placeholders — no photo captured normalized boxes.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is annotated 'FRONT ELEVATION' and shows a full gable-end wall with two garage doors and an entry door, viewed head-on.
photos.eave_reasoning: Entry door (assumed 80in slab, 258px tall) gives vertical scale of 3.23 px/in; grade at slab (~y915) to eave/soffit line at wall corners (~y500) = 415px = ~129in = 10.7 ft.
photos.notes: Front-gable wall, 324in (27 ft) wide per WALL REF (1110px = 3.43 px/in horizontal). Window width confirmed by WIN REF 48in. Garage door heights measure ~75in via door-derived vertical scale — shorter than typical 84in; possible slight vertical compression from elevated camera position, but reported as measured per no-rounding rule. Yellow pin '2SL' = contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider on the upper gable window.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-LEFT and the photo shows the long left wall receding plus the front garage-door wall at the corner.
photos.eave_reasoning: WIN REF 36in vertical bar ≈ 90px → ~2.5 px/in at mid-wall depth; lower-wall eave to woodpile top ≈ 200px ≈ 80in visible, woodpile hides an estimated ~38in of wall to true grade (grade line visible left of pile) → ~118in ≈ 9.8 ft.
photos.eave_vertical_scale_basis: WIN_REF 36in vertical bar (blue) at upper-wall window
photos.notes: 1.5-story clerestory/shed-roof design — upper red wall with two windows sits above the lower roof, no true gable or dormers from this angle. WALL REF 48in (red, ~145px) and WIN REF 36in (blue, ~90px) are both vertical bars at the upper wall; used WIN REF for vertical scale. Front-wall garage door bottoms hidden by trash bins; brick section at far right appears to be an attached front-wall masonry area (also flagged as brick on photo 5 back elevation) — reconcile against front photo. Woodpile occlusion makes the eave read soft (±6in).

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and matches the Photo-3 annotation set (WALL REF = 444", WIN REF = 36", 3 DH + 2 2SL pins).
photos.eave_reasoning: Measured at the exposed left corner where grade is visible: ~285 px from grade to eave/soffit line ÷ 2.47 px/in (WIN REF vertical scale) = ~115 in = 9.6 ft; center of wall base is hidden by the woodpile but the measurement point was clear.
photos.eave_vertical_scale_basis: WIN_REF 36in vertical bar on right dormer window (~89 px = 36 in -> 2.47 px/in); note dormer is set back so main-wall vertical scale may be slightly larger, biasing this read slightly high
photos.notes: Horizontal scale: WALL REF 444 in spans ~1303 px (2.94 px/in). Vertical scale from WIN REF (2.47 px/in) is at dormer depth; main-wall verticals (eave, DH heights) may be biased slightly high (~5-10%). Woodpile hides the base of the wall behind DH3 — its lower sash size is partially inferred from the other two matching DH units. Brick chimney/side of another wall visible at far right belongs to the rear/right, not this elevation. Ductless mini-split unit at left wall base; downspout at far left.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-LEFT ELEVATION and the shot captures the rear gable wall (patio slider) plus the long left wall receding toward the driveway/truck at front.
photos.eave_reasoning: Used the rear patio slider (standard 80in) as vertical ruler on the gable wall: door = ~252px -> 3.15 px/in; grade-to-eave ~317px = ~101in = 8.4 ft on the one-story rear section. Taller two-story section eave estimated ~14-15 ft (see notes) but its grade is occluded by woodpile.
photos.eave_vertical_scale_basis: patio slider door height 80in on rear gable wall
photos.notes: Prompt says index 3 but this image is the REAR-LEFT annotation set (WALL REF 48in vertical bar near tall-section eave, WIN REF 36in on upper left-wall window); no yellow style pins present in this photo. WALL REF 48in vertical bar gives ~2.8 px/in at the tall wall plane -> two-story rear-left section eave ~14.2 ft, but its base is hidden by a woodpile (~est. 12in hidden) so it is reported only here, not as the primary eave. Horizontal widths on both walls are foreshortened at the corner; left-wall along_wall positions left null to avoid false dedup. Small chimney/stove pipe and smoker obstruct lower-left wall detail.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads BACK ELEVATION and the full gable-end wall with patio slider matches the back-wall description (WALL REF = 324").
photos.eave_reasoning: Patio door (80in) gives vertical scale of ~2.8 px/in at the wall plane; eave-return line at corners sits ~102in above visible grade + ~5in of base hidden by plantings/slope ≈ 107in ≈ 8.9 ft.
photos.notes: WALL REF 324in spans ~915 px (2.82 px/in horizontal); patio door 80in used as vertical scale for lower-wall reads, WIN REF 36in vertical bar used at gable window height. Gable 2SL window matches WIN REF height exactly (36in). Wall base partially occluded by plantings at both corners and the marked brick skirt band at lower left. Decorative signs on wall are not openings.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo banner reads 'REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION' and the annotations (WALL REF = 84", WIN REF = 48") match the rear-right photo spec; back gable wall faces camera with right wall receding.
photos.eave_reasoning: Used the 80" patio door as vertical ruler (153 px tall -> 1.91 px/in); eave line at right side of gable wall is ~220 px above grade -> ~115 in = 9.6 ft. The vertical red WALL REF = 84" bar at the left corner independently reads that lower section's eave-to-grade at ~7.3 ft, consistent with sloping grade and a lower one-story section.
photos.eave_vertical_scale_basis: patio door assumed 80in tall (153 px) on the back gable wall; cross-checked with vertical WALL_REF 84in bar at left corner
photos.notes: This photo carries photo-6 annotations (REAR-RIGHT, WALL REF 84", WIN REF 48") despite being passed as index 5; no yellow style pins are visible in this frame so slider styles are photo-inferred, not contractor ground truth. Back gable wall spans ~21 ft in view; WIN REF 48" over back-w1 yields 1.96 px/in horizontal, agreeing with the 80" door vertical scale (1.91 px/in). Small window back-w3 is on the shaded lower section and partially tree-obscured — sizes there are low confidence and its along-wall position could not be fixed (foreshortened, corner hidden). Brick foundation band at wall base excluded from siding.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads RIGHT ELEVATION and the annotation set for this photo (3 contractor 2SL pins, WALL REF 180", vertical WIN REF 36") matches the 'Photo 7' right-elevation description.
photos.eave_reasoning: Vertical scale from 36" vertical WIN REF on dormer window (~83 px -> 2.31 px/in); lower-wall eave line y~595 to visible grade y~860 = ~265 px = ~115 in to visible grade + ~10 in hidden by shrubs/mulch = ~125 in = 10.4 ft. Lap courses too indistinct at this resolution to count reliably.
photos.eave_vertical_scale_basis: WIN REF 36in vertical bar across dormer window height
photos.notes: Both first-floor 2SL windows are partially occluded by shrubs, so their width/height reads carry ±15% uncertainty. Vertical WIN REF (36") on the dormer window used for all vertical measurements; horizontal red WALL REF (~462 px = 180") used for widths and along-wall positions. Right end of the wall is hidden behind trees — total wall length not measurable from this photo. Photo appears taken from a slightly elevated deck position, adding mild downward perspective.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo shows the front gable wall with garage doors plus the right side wall receding to the right, matching the FRONT-RIGHT ELEVATION banner.
photos.eave_reasoning: Not measurable: right-wall grade line is hidden by shrubs, the only vertical reference (WALL REF 48in) sits on the upper-story wall plane at a different camera depth than the lower eave, and the elevated oblique angle prevents a trustworthy grade-to-eave pixel read; lap exposure not confirmed for course counting.

measurements._ai_reference_used: Contractor WALL_REF bars (324" front photo 0, 444" left photo 2, 324" back photo 4) cross-checked with plane-correct lap-course counting at 3.75" exposure
measurements._ai_story_count_reasoning: 6 of 8 photos read 1.5 stories (main level plus shed-dormered half-story); photo 3's '2' and photo 4's '1' are corner/gable-end misreads of the same half-story.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows a full frontal gable-end wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses from grade to the eave return at the left corner: ~33 courses × 3.75 in exposure = ~124 in ≈ 10.4 ft; cross-checked with WALL REF 324 in scale (3.41 px/in) giving ~125 in — readings agree.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-end front wall of what appears to be a garage wing; wall width matches WALL REF 324 in (27 ft). Window sized via WIN REF 48 in (3.5 px/in on window plane): 48x33. Garage door heights read ~72 in from photo — slightly shorter than the standard 78-84 in, possibly due to sloped driveway grade at the door base. Roof surface not visible from this gable-end angle so roof_hex is null.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Corner shot showing the long left wall in near-full view with the front wall (garage doors) receding sharply to the right; banner also states FRONT-LEFT.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses on the left wall from grade to the main (lower) eave line where visible between the woodpile gaps: ~32 courses × 3.75" exposure = 120" ≈ 10 ft. Course count is same-plane so no cross-plane scale needed.
_ai_photos.notes: WALL REF = 48" and WIN REF = 36" bars are on the upper (set-back) half-story wall plane, so they were used only for the upper windows; lower-wall openings and eave were read via course counting (3.75" exposure) which is plane-correct. Garage door sizes on the front wall are estimates due to severe foreshortening — Photo 1 (front) should override them. along_wall_ft measured from the front corner of the left wall (LEFT corner as viewed from outside), assuming ~37 ft wall per Photo 3's 444" ref.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads LEFT ELEVATION and a single long eave-only wall with shed dormer fills the frame.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Measured with the same-plane WALL REF (444" = ~1300 px, 2.93 px/in): grade (~y860) to eave/gutter line (~y530) is ~330 px = ~112 in. Lap courses not countable due to deep shade, so course count omitted.
_ai_photos.notes: Wall width per WALL REF is 444" (~37 ft). WIN REF (36") lies on the shed dormer plane and was used only for the two dormer 2SL sliders; main-wall openings and eave were scaled by the same-plane 444" wall ref. Bottom heights of left-w2 and left-w3 are estimated (matched to left-w1) because the woodpile hides their sills. Small brick section visible at far right belongs to the front wall corner, not this elevation.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels this REAR-LEFT and the on-image WALL REF=48"/WIN REF=36" match the Photo 4 (rear-left) annotation set; gabled back wall faces camera with the long left wall receding right.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses on the back (gable) wall from grade to the wall/rake intersection at the left corner: ~30 courses x 3.75" exposure = ~112" = 9.4 ft; patio door (~21 courses tall) used as sanity check on the same plane.
_ai_photos.notes: WIN REF=36" is on an upper-level window of the receding left wall — used only for that wall plane's openings per cross-plane rule; back-wall openings scaled by course counting (3.75" exposure) on their own plane. Upper level on the left wall appears to be a raised second-story section, not a dormer. Smoker and firewood stack may hide 1+ additional openings on the left wall's right half — flag for reconciliation with Photo 3 (left elevation) which has 5 contractor-tagged windows.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is annotated 'BACK ELEVATION' and matches the described back-elevation markup (324" wall ref, brick no-siding zone, two 2SL pins).
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Grade-to-eave at the left corner measures ~350 px; wall-ref scale (910 px = 324 in, 2.81 px/in) gives ~124 in. Lap courses at 3.75 in exposure imply ~33 courses grade-to-eave, consistent with the pixel read; scale reference is on this same wall plane.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-end back wall, single plane, so both WALL REF (324" = ~910 px) and WIN REF (36" vertical on gable window) are plane-correct. Gable window read as 48x36 via WIN REF scale; lower pinned window matches at 48x36 via wall scale. Patio door reads ~68x79, likely a nominal 72x80 slider with slight perspective compression. Brick no-siding band along lower-left foundation is partially hidden by furniture; sqft is approximate. Door hex omitted because the patio door is glass in shadow.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION and the annotations (WALL REF = 84", WIN REF = 48") match the rear-right photo spec; we face a rear gable wall with the right side receding into trees.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Right-side eave/soffit of the gable wall sits ~240 px above grade; using the patio-door/WIN-REF plane scale (~2.0 px/in on this wall plane) that is ~120 in = 10 ft. The 84" WALL REF is on the nearer lower-wing corner (different plane) so it was not used for this wall. Lap courses too fine at this resolution to count individually with confidence.
_ai_photos.notes: MISMATCH: prompt says index 5 = BACK elevation with WALL REF 324", but this photo's banner and annotations (WALL REF = 84", WIN REF = 48") match the photo-6 REAR-RIGHT spec — reconciliation should verify indexing. WALL REF 84" sits on the nearer lower-wing corner plane, so the gable wall was scaled from same-plane openings (WIN REF/patio door) instead. Patio door width likely slightly foreshortened; blue WIN REF line itself not clearly drawn, only the label visible.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads RIGHT ELEVATION and the photo contains three contractor 2SL pins matching photo 7's annotation list, though the prompt assigned index 6 (rear-right) — content indicates this is the right wall.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Lower-wall eave measured via pixel scale from the red WALL REF line (assumed 180" per photo-7 annotation, ~2.6 px/in): grade-to-eave ~270 px ≈ 105 in ≈ 8.7 ft. The ref line is drawn across the raised dormer/upper wall plane, so this is a cross-plane read; lap courses could not be reliably counted because shrubs and shadow obscure the lower half of the wall.
_ai_photos.notes: Annotation conflict: prompt says index 6 = REAR-RIGHT (WALL REF 84", WIN REF 48") but the in-photo banner says RIGHT ELEVATION and the three 2SL pins match photo 7's tag list (WALL REF 180", WIN REF 36"). I used the photo-7 interpretation. WALL REF and WIN REF lines both sit on the raised dormer/upper wall plane, so all main-wall pixel measurements (eave height, lower window sizes) are cross-plane and should be down-weighted vs. same-plane reads from other photos. Window sizes derived from wall-ref scale because the WIN REF bar extends visibly beyond the window frame, making a direct 36" edge match unreliable. Lower-left window is ~50% hidden behind a conifer; its width is an estimate. Wall length estimated ~37 ft for along_wall positions; right end of wall obscured by trees so positions carry ±2 ft uncertainty.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-RIGHT; photo shows the front gable wall with garage doors plus the right-side wall receding to the right.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses on the front wall plane beside the garage doors from driveway grade to the eave/soffit line: ~27 courses × 3.75" exposure ≈ 101" ≈ 8.5 ft; count is plane-correct on the front wall.
_ai_photos.notes: In-photo annotations show WIN REF = 36" on the front gable window edge (used for opening scale on the front wall plane) and WALL REF = 48" on the UPPER right wall plane — that ref was NOT used for the front-wall eave (course count used instead, plane-correct). Right-wall openings foreshortened and partially bush-obscured, so along_wall_ft left null to avoid false dedup; sizes there are lower-confidence. The upper right wall is a raised half-story wall above the main roof slope, supporting the 1.5-story read. No yellow contractor pins visible in this frame.

_ai_pin_gap_hints.message: Dormer on the left slope reports 15.0 ft wide but NO reference marker was in frame. Unanchored dormer widths drift 25-90% — re-shoot the left elevation with a WALL_REF or WIN_REF bar in frame before quoting.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct right elevation with dormer window full-front; note WALL REF sits on dormer plane so width is ref-scaled and plausible
front-right corner confirms raised upper wall on right slope; too foreshortened for width

_ai_dormers._reconciliation_note: Different face from photo 2's dormer so NOT collapsed — this is a second physical shed dormer on the right slope; single direct width reading (15 ft) from photo 6.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct left elevation, dormer face parallel to camera, two full-front 2SL windows on face
corner shot confirms upper windows on left slope but too foreshortened for width
rejected — corner view interpreted upper plane as raised half-story; no width read

raw_ai.reference_used: Contractor WALL_REF bars (324" front photo 0, 444" left photo 2, 324" back photo 4) cross-checked with plane-correct lap-course counting at 3.75" exposure
raw_ai.story_count_reasoning: 6 of 8 photos read 1.5 stories (main level plus shed-dormered half-story); photo 3's '2' and photo 4's '1' are corner/gable-end misreads of the same half-story.
raw_ai.roof_type_reasoning: Gable triangles confirmed on front (photos 0,7) and back (photos 3,4,5) with eave-side left/right walls each carrying a shed dormer (photos 2 and 6).

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct left elevation, dormer face parallel to camera, two full-front 2SL windows on face
corner shot confirms upper windows on left slope but too foreshortened for width
rejected — corner view interpreted upper plane as raised half-story; no width read

dormers._reconciliation_note: Only photo 2 gave a direct width (15 ft); single reading so flagged amber; face=left from cardinal left elevation; offset ~0 (dormer windows at 15.0/20.5 ft straddle the 18.5 ft centerline of the 37 ft wall).

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct right elevation with dormer window full-front; note WALL REF sits on dormer plane so width is ref-scaled and plausible
front-right corner confirms raised upper wall on right slope; too foreshortened for width

dormers._reconciliation_note: Different face from photo 2's dormer so NOT collapsed — this is a second physical shed dormer on the right slope; single direct width reading (15 ft) from photo 6.

walls.label: front
walls.confidence_reasoning: Full-span 324" WALL REF anchors width; eave kept from the ref-cross-checked direct front photo despite a 1.9 ft disagreement with the corner shot.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct front view, 33 courses × 3.75" cross-checked against WALL REF 324" (both ≈124-125 in)
rejected for eave — elevated corner shot, 27-course count likely truncated by sloped driveway/shrub-hidden grade; gable read also rejected (foreshortened rake)
rejected — front wall heavily foreshortened in this corner shot

walls._reconciliation_note: 10.4 vs 8.5 disagree by >1 ft so not averaged; kept photo 0 (course count + contractor-ref agreement, strongest reasoning class); gable triangle 8.3 ft from the direct frontal read, photo 7's 10 ft discarded.

walls.label: back
walls.confidence_reasoning: Three independent direct readings landed within 1 ft and the 324" ref plus course counts agree on scale.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept for eave (30 courses, same plane); gable/pitch rejected — corner angle exaggerates triangle
kept — direct back elevation, 324" same-plane ref + 33-course corroboration; gable read kept (square-on, same-plane refs)
kept for eave (same-plane opening scale); gable rejected — 84" ref off-plane and tree occlusion

walls._reconciliation_note: Eaves 9.4/10.0/10.4 all within ±1 ft → median 10.0 from 3 photos; gable triangle taken from photo 4 (direct, plane-correct refs) with photos 3/5 corner reads (11.0/9.4) discarded and the pitch conflict (6/12 vs 10/12) noted.

walls.label: left
walls.confidence_reasoning: Full-span 444" ref fixes width; two independent same-plane eave reads agree within 0.7 ft despite woodpile occlusion.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — 32-course same-plane count between woodpile gaps
kept — pixel read against same-plane 444" WALL REF
rejected — left wall recedes at >45° in this rear-left corner shot; its eave reading belongs to the back wall

walls._reconciliation_note: Two valid direct readings (10.0, 9.3) within ±1 ft → median 9.65 rounded to 9.7; eave-only wall, shed dormer face (68 sqft) carried separately.

walls.label: right
walls.confidence_reasoning: Only one eave read exists and its 180" ref sits on the dormer plane (cross-plane scale); width was never ref-spanned so it's copied from the left wall.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept as the only reading, but eave_scale_cross_plane=true (ref on dormer/upper plane) — flagged cross_plane per 79j.67(c); shrubs prevented course counting
rejected — right wall heavily foreshortened from front-right corner

walls._reconciliation_note: No full-span ref covered this wall so width_ft=37 copied from the opposite (left) wall — assumed_symmetric, amber; single cross-plane eave reading 8.7 ft kept with height_scale_flag=cross_plane — capture a direct right-side shot with same-plane scale before quoting.

openings._reconciliation_note: Contractor-pinned 2SL sized via WIN REF 48" on photo 0 (48x33 @13.5 ft); photo 7's 24x36 'casement' @11 ft is the same gable window half-hidden/foreshortened from the corner — merged, pin style is ground truth.

Photo 0 @7.1 ft (direct, ref-scaled 100x72), photo 7 @5 ft, photo 1 null-along; only two doors physically fit the 27 ft wall and photo 1's notes self-flag its 108x84 as foreshortened estimates — merged, photo 0 sizes kept.
Photo 0 @18.3 ft vs photo 7 @14 ft (elevated corner, position unreliable); same second bay — merged with photo 0's ref-scaled 101x73 kept over the corner reads.
Photo 0 @24.3 ft, photo 7 @21 ft — same single entry door on the 27 ft front wall; direct photo 0 sizes kept (31x76).
Photo 2 (pinned DH 32x54) @8.5 ft, photo 1 @8 ft (misread as 2SL behind woodpile), photo 3 @9 ft — all within ±2 ft → same window; contractor pin governs style/size.
Photo 2 @17.5 ft (pinned), photo 1 @18 ft (height under-read 42" — sill hidden by woodpile), photo 3 @18 ft → merged; pin size 32x54 kept.
Photo 2 @27.0 ft (pinned) and photo 1 @28 ft → same window; photo 1's 28" width off by 4" due to occlusion, pin size kept.

Left-dormer slider, WIN-REF-scaled on photo 2 @15.0 ft; photo 1's upper window @23 ft and photo 3's 2SL @16 ft are corner reads of the same twin pair (only 2 upper openings exist per direct photo 2) — merged with position discrepancy noted.
Twin to left-d1-w1: photo 2 @20.5 ft and photo 1 @20 ft agree within 0.5 ft; both dormer twins preserved as separate rows (5.5 ft apart > shared-stud distance).
Single low-confidence sighting of a narrow wing window from photo 5; along_wall_ft null and no size match to any pinned left-wall opening, so kept separately per rule 3c — verify on site.
Back gable window: photo 4 (pinned, WIN REF) @13.3 ft; photo 5's gable window @11.5 ft (40x32 via weaker cross-wing scale) is the same opening — merged, pin sizes kept.
Photo 4 @7.1 ft (pinned 48x36), photo 3 @8 ft ('Picture' 32x48 — foreshortened misread), photo 5 @6 ft (50x45) — all one lower back window; direct pinned read governs.

One patio slider seen 3× (photo 4 @20.0 ft 68x79, photo 3 @13 ft 72x80, photo 5 @16 ft 58x80); positions scatter with corner foreshortening but photo 4 (direct) confirms a single door — sized at nominal 72x80 per photo 3/4 agreement.

Kept per rule 3b: nearest photo-4 opening is @7.1 ft (delta 2.1 ft > ±2 ft) and width differs 12"; flagged as possible duplicate of back-w2-lower since direct photo 4 saw only 3 back openings — verify on site.
Right-dormer slider from pinned direct photo 6 @18 ft; photo 7's right-w1 (40x48, null along) matched 1:1 by count/style on the foreshortened corner view — merged.
Pinned direct read @11 ft (conifer-occluded, width estimated); photo 7's null-along right-wall windows collapse 1:1 by count (3↔3) onto photo 6's pinned trio — merged rather than doubling the wall.
Twin-like pair with right-w2 preserved as two rows (@11 and @24 ft, delta 13 ft); photo 7 confirmation merged via 1:1 count/style correspondence.

_reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Confidence-weighted average of final per-wall eaves (front 10.4 @85, back 10.0 @85, left 9.7 @80, right 8.7 @55) = 9.8 ft; front used the ref-corroborated photo 0 over photo 7's 8.5 (>1 ft disagreement, not averaged), back is the median of 3 agreeing reads (9.4/10.0/10.4), left the median of 2 (9.3/10.0), right a single cross-plane read.
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: Gable triangles seen square-on on front (photos 0,7) and back (photos 3,4,5) with eave-only left/right walls; photos 2 and 6 each report dormers_observed_count=1, upgrading gable → gable-shed-dormer.
_reconciliation_notes.dormers: Detected 2 shed dormers — one on the left slope (photo 2 direct, 15 ft, 2 twin 2SL windows) and one on the right slope (photo 6 direct, 15 ft, 1 2SL window); different faces so never collapsed; each has exactly one direct width reading → both flagged direct_single_reading; photos 3/7 saw the upper planes but read them as a raised half-story (face/count corroboration only).
_reconciliation_notes.story_count: Median of per-photo reads (1.5,1.5,1.5,2,1,1.5,1.5,1.5) = 1.5; the shed dormers and knee walls confirm a half-story, ruling out photo 3's '2' and photo 4's gable-end '1'.
_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: Total wall+gable+dormer area ≈ 1,573 sqft with ~165 sqft non-siding (90 sqft brick at the front-left corner per photos 1/2, 40 sqft brick on back per photo 4's NO SIDING markup, 35 sqft stone per photo 5) → ~90% siding; front and back walls set to 80%, sides 100%.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: Siding/trim from photo 4 (sunlit, direct, conf 88 — #B23A30/#F0EDE4); roof is the median of the sunlit reads (#4E4F4F from photo 1, between photo 4's shadowed #3B3E42 and photo 5's washed #8A8A88); door from photo 0 (#F0EFEB), the only frames with a door sample; photo 2's backlit samples rejected.
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: 39 raw openings across 8 photos → 17 after dedup. Key collapses: front garage doors/entry seen in photos 0/1/7 merged onto photo 0's ref-scaled reads (photo 1 self-flagged its foreshortened sizes); left wall's 5 contractor-pinned windows absorbed photo 1 and photo 3's occluded misreads (e.g. photo 1 @8/@18/@28 vs photo 2 @8.5/@17.5/@27); dormer twin pairs on left (@15.0/@20.5) preserved as separate rows; back trio (gable window, lower window, patio door) merged across photos 3/4/5 with photo 3's @5 ft window kept separately (delta 2.1 ft > ±2 ft, rule 3b); right wall's 3 pinned windows merged 1:1 with photo 7's null-along corner sightings by count/style correspondence per photo 7's own foreshortening note. Photo 5's 18x40 wing window kept un-deduped (null along, rule 3c). NOTE: source extractions carried no bbox data, so all bboxes are zeroed placeholders — no photo captured normalized coordinates.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows a full frontal gable-end wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses from grade to the eave return at the left corner: ~33 courses × 3.75 in exposure = ~124 in ≈ 10.4 ft; cross-checked with WALL REF 324 in scale (3.41 px/in) giving ~125 in — readings agree.
photos.notes: Gable-end front wall of what appears to be a garage wing; wall width matches WALL REF 324 in (27 ft). Window sized via WIN REF 48 in (3.5 px/in on window plane): 48x33. Garage door heights read ~72 in from photo — slightly shorter than the standard 78-84 in, possibly due to sloped driveway grade at the door base. Roof surface not visible from this gable-end angle so roof_hex is null.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Corner shot showing the long left wall in near-full view with the front wall (garage doors) receding sharply to the right; banner also states FRONT-LEFT.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses on the left wall from grade to the main (lower) eave line where visible between the woodpile gaps: ~32 courses × 3.75" exposure = 120" ≈ 10 ft. Course count is same-plane so no cross-plane scale needed.
photos.notes: WALL REF = 48" and WIN REF = 36" bars are on the upper (set-back) half-story wall plane, so they were used only for the upper windows; lower-wall openings and eave were read via course counting (3.75" exposure) which is plane-correct. Garage door sizes on the front wall are estimates due to severe foreshortening — Photo 1 (front) should override them. along_wall_ft measured from the front corner of the left wall (LEFT corner as viewed from outside), assuming ~37 ft wall per Photo 3's 444" ref.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads LEFT ELEVATION and a single long eave-only wall with shed dormer fills the frame.
photos.eave_reasoning: Measured with the same-plane WALL REF (444" = ~1300 px, 2.93 px/in): grade (~y860) to eave/gutter line (~y530) is ~330 px = ~112 in. Lap courses not countable due to deep shade, so course count omitted.
photos.notes: Wall width per WALL REF is 444" (~37 ft). WIN REF (36") lies on the shed dormer plane and was used only for the two dormer 2SL sliders; main-wall openings and eave were scaled by the same-plane 444" wall ref. Bottom heights of left-w2 and left-w3 are estimated (matched to left-w1) because the woodpile hides their sills. Small brick section visible at far right belongs to the front wall corner, not this elevation.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels this REAR-LEFT and the on-image WALL REF=48"/WIN REF=36" match the Photo 4 (rear-left) annotation set; gabled back wall faces camera with the long left wall receding right.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses on the back (gable) wall from grade to the wall/rake intersection at the left corner: ~30 courses x 3.75" exposure = ~112" = 9.4 ft; patio door (~21 courses tall) used as sanity check on the same plane.
photos.notes: WIN REF=36" is on an upper-level window of the receding left wall — used only for that wall plane's openings per cross-plane rule; back-wall openings scaled by course counting (3.75" exposure) on their own plane. Upper level on the left wall appears to be a raised second-story section, not a dormer. Smoker and firewood stack may hide 1+ additional openings on the left wall's right half — flag for reconciliation with Photo 3 (left elevation) which has 5 contractor-tagged windows.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is annotated 'BACK ELEVATION' and matches the described back-elevation markup (324" wall ref, brick no-siding zone, two 2SL pins).
photos.eave_reasoning: Grade-to-eave at the left corner measures ~350 px; wall-ref scale (910 px = 324 in, 2.81 px/in) gives ~124 in. Lap courses at 3.75 in exposure imply ~33 courses grade-to-eave, consistent with the pixel read; scale reference is on this same wall plane.
photos.notes: Gable-end back wall, single plane, so both WALL REF (324" = ~910 px) and WIN REF (36" vertical on gable window) are plane-correct. Gable window read as 48x36 via WIN REF scale; lower pinned window matches at 48x36 via wall scale. Patio door reads ~68x79, likely a nominal 72x80 slider with slight perspective compression. Brick no-siding band along lower-left foundation is partially hidden by furniture; sqft is approximate. Door hex omitted because the patio door is glass in shadow.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION and the annotations (WALL REF = 84", WIN REF = 48") match the rear-right photo spec; we face a rear gable wall with the right side receding into trees.
photos.eave_reasoning: Right-side eave/soffit of the gable wall sits ~240 px above grade; using the patio-door/WIN-REF plane scale (~2.0 px/in on this wall plane) that is ~120 in = 10 ft. The 84" WALL REF is on the nearer lower-wing corner (different plane) so it was not used for this wall. Lap courses too fine at this resolution to count individually with confidence.
photos.notes: MISMATCH: prompt says index 5 = BACK elevation with WALL REF 324", but this photo's banner and annotations (WALL REF = 84", WIN REF = 48") match the photo-6 REAR-RIGHT spec — reconciliation should verify indexing. WALL REF 84" sits on the nearer lower-wing corner plane, so the gable wall was scaled from same-plane openings (WIN REF/patio door) instead. Patio door width likely slightly foreshortened; blue WIN REF line itself not clearly drawn, only the label visible.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads RIGHT ELEVATION and the photo contains three contractor 2SL pins matching photo 7's annotation list, though the prompt assigned index 6 (rear-right) — content indicates this is the right wall.
photos.eave_reasoning: Lower-wall eave measured via pixel scale from the red WALL REF line (assumed 180" per photo-7 annotation, ~2.6 px/in): grade-to-eave ~270 px ≈ 105 in ≈ 8.7 ft. The ref line is drawn across the raised dormer/upper wall plane, so this is a cross-plane read; lap courses could not be reliably counted because shrubs and shadow obscure the lower half of the wall.
photos.notes: Annotation conflict: prompt says index 6 = REAR-RIGHT (WALL REF 84", WIN REF 48") but the in-photo banner says RIGHT ELEVATION and the three 2SL pins match photo 7's tag list (WALL REF 180", WIN REF 36"). I used the photo-7 interpretation. WALL REF and WIN REF lines both sit on the raised dormer/upper wall plane, so all main-wall pixel measurements (eave height, lower window sizes) are cross-plane and should be down-weighted vs. same-plane reads from other photos. Window sizes derived from wall-ref scale because the WIN REF bar extends visibly beyond the window frame, making a direct 36" edge match unreliable. Lower-left window is ~50% hidden behind a conifer; its width is an estimate. Wall length estimated ~37 ft for along_wall positions; right end of wall obscured by trees so positions carry ±2 ft uncertainty.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-RIGHT; photo shows the front gable wall with garage doors plus the right-side wall receding to the right.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses on the front wall plane beside the garage doors from driveway grade to the eave/soffit line: ~27 courses × 3.75" exposure ≈ 101" ≈ 8.5 ft; count is plane-correct on the front wall.
photos.notes: In-photo annotations show WIN REF = 36" on the front gable window edge (used for opening scale on the front wall plane) and WALL REF = 48" on the UPPER right wall plane — that ref was NOT used for the front-wall eave (course count used instead, plane-correct). reference_dim:
Pre-AI photo annotations:
Photo 1: FRONT ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 324"" is a known 324-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this. Blue line marked "WIN REF = 48"" is a known 48-inch span across a window edge — use this as a TIGHTER per-window scale for ALL window sizing on this photo (the wall ref handles whole-wall geometry, this one handles openings). Window measurements should be within ±5% of this anchor.. YELLOW PINS with brown style badges mark CONTRACTOR-TAGGED WINDOW STYLES — treat each tagged STYLE as GROUND TRUTH (overrides whatever your photo-inference would have guessed for that pin's style). Tagged styles in this photo: 2-Lite Slider. Each yellow pin = ONE window — include it in your openings list with the contractor's exact style and style_confidence=100, BUT YOU still measure width_in and height_in from the photo using your normal scale reference. Don't size-tag a yellow pin as 0×0 — measure it like any other window..

measurements._ai_reference_used: Taped WALL REF bars (324" front/back, 444" left, 84"/180" partials) plus plane-correct lap-course counts at 3.75" exposure
measurements._ai_story_count_reasoning: 7 of 8 photos report 1.5 stories (main level plus dormer/clerestory upper level); photo 3's lone '2' reading is outvoted.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows a full straight-on gable-front wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: WALL REF 324" spans ~1110 px (3.43 px/in) on this same wall plane; grade (~y930) to eave/soffit line (~y505) is ~425 px = ~124 in = ~10.3 ft; lap course count of ~33 courses x 3.75" = ~124 in corroborates.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-front wall ~27 ft wide (WALL REF 324"). No SHAKE accent visible on this elevation — continuous lap siding grade-to-peak. Garage door heights read ~76 in via wall scale; standard 84 in is possible if the doors sit slightly recessed, but reporting the measured value. Stacked stone retaining wall at right edge abuts (does not cover) the siding corner. Roof surface not visible from this straight-on gable view, so roof_hex is null.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-LEFT ELEVATION and the photo shows the long left wall receding to the front wall with garage doors at the right.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses (3.75" exposure) on the left wall from eave down to the top of the woodpile (~22 courses) then extrapolated ~8 hidden courses to grade using visible grade at the far rear corner; ~30 courses x 3.75" ≈ 9.4 ft. WALL REF 48" bar is on the upper clerestory plane so it was not used for the main wall.
_ai_photos.notes: WALL REF 48" and WIN REF 36" are both on the upper clerestory wall plane; they were used only for upper-window sizing, while the main-wall eave height came from a lap-course count (plane-correct). Along-wall positions are measured from the REAR corner of the left wall (left corner as viewed from outside), assuming the ~37 ft (444") wall length given for photo 3. Lower-wall window sizes are approximate — woodpile hides sills. Red brick section at far right may be an attached breezeway/adjacent structure; flagged as no-siding brick with low certainty. Front garage doors and upper front window heavily backlit by sun flare.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and shows a single long eave-side wall with a shed dormer above, matching the left-side description (WALL REF = 444").
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Pixel-scale from on-plane 444" WALL REF (~2.92 px/in): grade to eave line ~270-310 px ≈ 92-106 in; cross-checked against window head height, yielding ~8.5-9 ft. Lap courses are not reliably countable due to heavy backlight/shadow on the red siding.
_ai_photos.notes: Dormer window sizes measured with the on-plane 36" WIN REF (~2.64 px/in on the dormer plane); main-wall openings and eave measured with the on-plane 444" WALL REF. Dormer window along_wall_ft values are approximate (set-back plane). Lower wall partially hidden by woodpile so window sill positions for w2/w3 are estimated; heights assumed to match w1. Brick chimney/wall of an attached structure visible at far right belongs to another elevation.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels REAR-LEFT and the photo shows the rear gable wall with patio door plus the long left wall receding to the right.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Scaled from the patio door (nominal 80 in, ~245 px -> ~3.06 px/in) on the rear wall plane; grade-to-eave at the shared rear-left corner spans ~385 px = ~126 in = ~10.5 ft. Lap courses at 3.75 in exposure are too fine to count reliably at this resolution/angle, so pixel-scale with a same-corner reference was used. WALL REF 48 in bar sits on the upper dormer/second-story plane and was NOT used for the lower wall.
_ai_photos.notes: Blue WIN REF=36in bar sits on the shed-dormer window (dormer plane, ~2.1 px/in) and was applied only to that dormer window; the red WALL REF=48in bar is also on the upper plane, so lower-wall scale came from the patio door on the rear plane. Along_wall_ft for left-w2-far is rough due to heavy foreshortening and woodpile occlusion. Possible additional upper glazing behind/near the ref bars could not be confirmed.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo banner reads 'BACK ELEVATION' and the view is a square-on gable wall with patio slider matching the back-elevation annotation set (WALL REF = 324").
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Scaled with the same-plane WALL REF = 324" (≈910 px → 2.81 px/in); grade (~y770) to eave line (~y535) ≈ 235-330 px depending on grade point, and lap-course spacing (~10.5 px/course at 3.75" exposure) gives ~30 courses grade→eave ≈ 112-115" ≈ 9.5 ft; course count reported per rule 5.
_ai_photos.notes: Prompt labels this index 4 (manifest says rear-left, WALL REF=48"), but the delivered photo is clearly the BACK ELEVATION with WALL REF=324" and two 2SL pins matching the photo-5 annotation spec — extraction follows the actual image content. WIN REF=36" sits on the gable window; per rule 6 it was used only for that gable-plane window, while the main-wall window and patio door were scaled with the same-plane 324" wall ref. Gable window measures ~48x36 consistent with the 36" WIN REF height. Lap coursing is continuous into the gable (no shake detected on this elevation despite the SHAKE hint from photo 8).

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION and annotations (WALL REF = 84", WIN REF = 48") match the rear-right photo description; we see a gable-end rear wall with the right roof plane receding to the right.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Red WALL REF bar (84") runs on the near corner wall plane from its eave line nearly to grade; measured 238 px = 84" (2.83 px/in), eave-to-grade span ~265 px ≈ 93.5" ≈ 7.8 ft on that same plane.
_ai_photos.notes: This photo matches the 'Photo 6 / REAR-RIGHT' annotation set (WALL REF=84", WIN REF=48") despite being given index 5. Gable wall in full sun with narrow-exposure red lap siding (consistent with 3.75" exposure). Courses visible but not resolvable enough for a reliable grade-to-eave count, so eave read anchored to the same-plane 84" WALL REF bar. Patio door dimensions carry moderate uncertainty due to perspective. No SHAKE accent visible on this elevation. Wall plaques/decor on the gable wall are not openings.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-RIGHT ELEVATION and the view shows the front gable with garage doors plus the right-side wall receding to the rear.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Front garage-door heads (~84 in standard) sit roughly 1.5-2 ft below the front eave line on the same front wall plane, giving ~9 ft grade-to-eave; lap courses are too fine and foreshortened at this elevated angle to count reliably.
_ai_photos.notes: Refs visible in this photo: red WALL REF = 48" vertical on the upper right wall plane and blue WIN REF = 36" vertical on the front gable window — each used only on its own plane. Right-wall window positions (along_wall_ft) left null due to severe rake foreshortening; keep those openings rather than dedup on position. No SHAKE accents visible on these elevations — body and gable are all narrow lap consistent with the 3.75" exposure.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Single wall plane viewed nearly straight-on with driveway/truck to the left and front walkway visible; consistent with the right side of the house per photo sequence (front-right position).
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses from grade to the lower (main) eave line at 3.75" contractor-calibrated exposure: ~26 courses x 3.75 in = 97.5 in = 8.1 ft; course count is plane-correct and preferred over pixel scaling since the 180" bar sits on the set-back upper clerestory wall plane.
_ai_photos.notes: This photo carries the 180" (15 ft) contractor bar spanning the upper clerestory wall (x=0.328-0.646) — used for upper-plane widths only; the upper clerestory wall spans almost exactly the 15 ft bar. WIN REF 36" blue bar is vertical across the upper window height, anchoring that window at 36" tall. Lower main-wall eave was course-counted (plane-correct) rather than scaled off the cross-plane bar. Upper clerestory story adds roughly 5-6 ft of additional red lap-clad wall above the lower roof. No shake visible on this elevation despite SHAKE annotation on photo 8; right third of wall unmeasurable behind tree — additional openings may be hidden there.

rejected for width — rear-left corner shot, left wall strongly foreshortened; confirms same-face dormer exists (within ±3 ft of photo 2)
