raw_ai.reference_used: Taped WALL REF bars (324" front/back, 444" left, 84"/180" partials) plus plane-correct lap-course counts at 3.75" exposure
raw_ai.story_count_reasoning: 7 of 8 photos report 1.5 stories (main level plus dormer/clerestory upper level); photo 3's lone '2' reading is outvoted.
raw_ai.roof_type_reasoning: Front (photos 0,6) and back (photos 3,4,5) both show full gable triangles, and photos 2/3/7 confirm raised shed-dormer/clerestory walls on the side slopes, upgrading gable to gable-shed-dormer.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct side view with on-plane 444" WALL REF; dormer face parallel to camera, two full-front windows visible
rejected for width — rear-left corner shot, left wall strongly foreshortened; confirms same-face dormer exists (within ±3 ft of photo 2)
no width reading — upper clerestory plane visible with 48"/36" refs; confirms dormer face and its two windows

dormers._reconciliation_note: Only photo 2 gave a valid direct-view width (17 ft); photo 3's 18 ft was rejected as foreshortened. Single unverified direct reading — amber. Offset: dormer windows at 14/20 ft on the 37 ft wall put dormer center ~17 ft vs wall center 18.5 ft.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — 180" contractor bar spans the upper clerestory wall almost exactly; near straight-on view with 36" WIN REF anchoring the dormer window
rejected — right slope heavily raked/foreshortened from elevated front-right angle; dormer not resolvable

dormers._reconciliation_note: Photo 7's 180" taped bar directly spans the right clerestory/dormer face (15 ft); different roof slope than the left dormer so NOT collapsed. Only one photo measured it — amber. Offset unknown (right third of wall tree-obscured), defaulted to centered.

walls.label: front
walls.confidence_reasoning: Straight-on view with taped 324" full-span ref plus a 33-course count; only degraded by trash bins at base and lack of a second corroborating direct eave reading.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — 33-course count on-plane, corroborated by same-plane 324" WALL REF (course counts outrank everything)
rejected — elevated camera with strong downward foreshortening; eave inferred from assumed 84" garage-door head, not a course count; gable 10 ft also rejected vs photo 0's ref-anchored 7 ft
front wall visible but backlit/flared; no front eave reading attempted

walls._reconciliation_note: One valid direct reading (photo 0, count-derived 10.3 ft) after rejecting photo 6's elevated-angle 9.0 ft inference; gable triangle taken from photo 0's straight-on ref-anchored 7.0 ft over photo 6's foreshortened 10 ft.

walls.label: back
walls.confidence_reasoning: Two direct readings within 1 ft with a course count anchoring the kept value and a full-span 324" ref; tree/shrub occlusion at corners is the residual risk.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — square-on view, 30-course count + same-plane 324" WALL REF; count-derived value wins inside the agreement band
valid direct-ish rear view (patio-door-scaled pixel read); agrees within 1.0 ft of photo 4 but is pixel-based so the count-derived 9.5 is kept; its 7.5 ft gable rejected as foreshortened
rejected for eave — gable-end shot; the 84" bar sits on an ambiguous near-corner plane. Gable 11.5 ft corroborates photo 4's 10.8 but photo 4's square-on ref-anchored value kept

walls._reconciliation_note: Photos 4 (9.5, 30-course count) and 3 (10.5, pixel) agree within the ±1 ft band; per the count-outranks-pixel rule the count-derived 9.5 is kept rather than the median. Photo 5's 7.8 gable-end read rejected.

walls.label: left
walls.confidence_reasoning: Two agreeing direct readings (count + on-plane 444" ref pixel read), but the lower third of the wall is woodpile-obscured in every photo, forcing an 8-course extrapolation in the kept reading.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — 30-course count on the left wall plane (22 visible + 8 extrapolated behind woodpile); count-derived so it wins inside the agreement band
valid — direct side view, on-plane 444" WALL REF pixel read; agrees within 0.6 ft, diluted in favor of the count
rejected — left wall strongly foreshortened in rear-left corner shot; no eave reading used

walls._reconciliation_note: Photos 1 (9.4, count) and 2 (8.8, on-plane pixel) agree within ±1 ft → consensus; count-derived 9.4 kept over the median per the count-outranks-pixel rule.

walls.label: right
accent_profiles: shake (user-annotated SHAKE ≈ 59 ft² on photo 8; not independently visible in extraction — treat as ground truth)
walls.confidence_reasoning: Single direct course-counted eave reading with the right third of the wall hidden behind trees and no full-span width ref on this wall.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — 26-course count grade-to-eave, plane-correct; 180" bar on set-back clerestory correctly not used for main wall
rejected — 84" bar sits on an ambiguous near-corner plane and the right wall is heavily foreshortened in this shot; cannot confirm which wall the 7.8 ft belongs to
rejected — right wall heavily raked and partially hidden behind the lower roof slope; no reading attempted

walls._reconciliation_note: Only photo 7's course-counted 8.1 ft survived validity screening — single unverified reading, amber. Width copied from the left wall (444" ref) since no photo carried a full-span right-wall ref — assumed symmetric, amber.

openings._reconciliation_note: Photo 0 (contractor-pinned, 48" WIN REF) at 13.3 ft; photo 6's front-w1 at 12 ft (30×36 foreshortened read) folded in — within 1.3 ft; photo 1's fl-w6-front-upper (backlit, along null) also folded in because photo 0's straight-on view shows exactly ONE front window.

Photo 0 (straight-on, 324" ref) at 6.9 ft kept as primary; photo 6's g1 at 3 ft and photo 1's g1 (along null) are the same left garage door — only two doors physically exist on the front. Height kept at ref-measured 76" over the assumed-standard 84" readings.
Photo 0 at 18.0 ft; photo 6's g2 at 12 ft (corner-shot position error) and photo 1's g2 (along null) map to the same right garage door.
Photo 0 at 24.0 ft, photo 6 at 22 ft — within ±2 ft, same door; photo 0's ref-anchored 32×78 kept over photo 6's 36×80.
Photo 2 (pinned) at 7.8 ft, photo 1 at 8 ft → same window; photo 3's left-w1 at 5 ft (30×54, foreshortened) folded in — size matches within 3" and photo 3's positions are self-flagged rough.
Photo 2 (pinned) at 16.8 ft, photo 1 at 15 ft → same window, delta 1.8 ft within ±2 ft.

Photo 2 (pinned Double Hung) at 26.0 ft; photo 1's fl-w3 at 24 ft (slider guess overridden by contractor pin) and photo 3's left-w2-far at ~22 ft (self-flagged rough) folded in — photo 2's direct view shows exactly three lower-wall windows matching the three DH pins.

Photo 2 (pinned, 36" WIN REF on dormer plane) at 14.0 ft; photo 1's fl-w4-upper (60×36, along null, oblique clerestory read) folded in — the left dormer carries exactly two windows per the direct view and pin count.
Photo 2 (pinned) at 20.0 ft; photo 1's fl-w5-upper and photo 3's left-dormer-w1 (both along-null oblique reads of the dormer plane) folded in against the pinned direct view.
Gable window: photo 4 (pinned) at 13.4 ft, photo 5 at 12.5 ft, same 48×36 → collapsed; photo 3's back-w1-gable (24×30 at 8.5 ft, foreshortened) folded in as the same gable window.
Photo 4 (pinned) at 7.1 ft, photo 5's back-w2-lower-left at 5.5 ft (48×42, height off due to perspective) → same window, delta 1.6 ft.
Photo 4 (direct, 324" ref) at 20.2 ft kept; photo 5 at 18 ft and photo 3 at 15 ft (corner shots, rough positions) collapse to it — photo 4's square-on view shows exactly one patio door.
Kept separate — not visible in photo 4 (corners tree/shrub-obscured); size (20×24) fails the ±3" match against every photo-4/5 back opening. Low confidence.

Kept per rule 3c — along_wall_ft null and size fails ±3" vs back-w2-small (possible same shaded corner window as photo 3's, but cannot verify); false duplicate cheaper than a lost window. Very low confidence.
Sits on the right clerestory/dormer wall (36" WIN REF anchored its height); marked on_dormer to match the reconciled right dormer entry.

Kept per rule 3c — photo 6's right-wall along_wall_ft is null (severe rake foreshortening) and sizes fail ±3" against photo 7's windows; likely overlaps photo 7's set but photo 7's right third is tree-hidden, so kept.
Kept per rule 3c — along null, no size match on photo 7's visible windows; may sit in photo 7's tree-obscured right third.
Kept per rule 3c — along null and 48×52 fails ±3" height match against every photo-7 right-wall opening.

_reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Confidence-weighted average of final per-wall eaves (front 10.3 @85, back 9.5 @80, left 9.4 @78, right 8.1 @65) = 9.4 ft; rejected readings: photo 6 front (elevated foreshortening), photo 5 (ambiguous-plane gable-end shot), photo 3 left (foreshortened).
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: Gable triangles confirmed on 2 walls (front: photos 0/6; back: photos 3/4/5) → gable; photos 2 and 3 each report dormers_observed_count=1 and photo 7 documents a right clerestory wall → upgraded to gable-shed-dormer.
_reconciliation_notes.dormers: Detected 2 shed dormers/clerestory faces, one on each side slope: LEFT 17 ft (photo 2 direct on-plane ref, photo 3's foreshortened 18 ft rejected for width but confirms face) and RIGHT 15 ft (photo 7's 180" taped bar spans the face). Different roof slopes → never collapsed; both are single-reading amber widths.
_reconciliation_notes.story_count: 7 of 8 photos report 1.5 (main level + dormer/clerestory); photo 3's outlier '2' discarded by majority.
_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: Total wall+gable+dormer area ≈ 1,580 ft²; non-siding = front stone 30 ft² (photo 0) + back brick ~40-65 ft² (photos 4/5); photo 1's 120 ft² brick was excluded as likely an adjacent structure per its own note → ≈94% siding.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: Siding/trim/roof taken as per-channel medians of the four sunlit samples (photos 1, 3, 4, 5); door from photo 6 (mixed) preferred over photo 0 (shaded) since no sunlit door sample exists.
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: 40 raw openings across 8 photos → 20 after dedup. Key collapses: left-wall trio (photo 2 pinned at 7.8/16.8/26.0 ft matched photo 1 at 8/15/24 ft and photo 3's rough reads), back gable window (photo 4 @13.4 ↔ photo 5 @12.5), back patio door (photos 3/4/5 → one), front garage pair and entry door (photos 0/1/6 → 3 openings). NOT collapsed per rule 3c: photo 6's three right-wall windows and photo 5's shaded back window (along_wall_ft null / size mismatch) — flagged as possible duplicates rather than silently dropped. NOTE: per-photo extractions carried no bboxes, so all bbox fields are 0-placeholders.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows a full straight-on gable-front wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
photos.eave_reasoning: WALL REF 324" spans ~1110 px (3.43 px/in) on this same wall plane; grade (~y930) to eave/soffit line (~y505) is ~425 px = ~124 in = ~10.3 ft; lap course count of ~33 courses x 3.75" = ~124 in corroborates.
photos.notes: Gable-front wall ~27 ft wide (WALL REF 324"). No SHAKE accent visible on this elevation — continuous lap siding grade-to-peak. Garage door heights read ~76 in via wall scale; standard 84 in is possible if the doors sit slightly recessed, but reporting the measured value. Stacked stone retaining wall at right edge abuts (does not cover) the siding corner. Roof surface not visible from this straight-on gable view, so roof_hex is null.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-LEFT ELEVATION and the photo shows the long left wall receding to the front wall with garage doors at the right.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses (3.75" exposure) on the left wall from eave down to the top of the woodpile (~22 courses) then extrapolated ~8 hidden courses to grade using visible grade at the far rear corner; ~30 courses x 3.75" ≈ 9.4 ft. WALL REF 48" bar is on the upper clerestory plane so it was not used for the main wall.
photos.notes: WALL REF 48" and WIN REF 36" are both on the upper clerestory wall plane; they were used only for upper-window sizing, while the main-wall eave height came from a lap-course count (plane-correct). Along-wall positions are measured from the REAR corner of the left wall (left corner as viewed from outside), assuming the ~37 ft (444") wall length given for photo 3. Lower-wall window sizes are approximate — woodpile hides sills. Red brick section at far right may be an attached breezeway/adjacent structure; flagged as no-siding brick with low certainty. Front garage doors and upper front window heavily backlit by sun flare.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and shows a single long eave-side wall with a shed dormer above, matching the left-side description (WALL REF = 444").
photos.eave_reasoning: Pixel-scale from on-plane 444" WALL REF (~2.92 px/in): grade to eave line ~270-310 px ≈ 92-106 in; cross-checked against window head height, yielding ~8.5-9 ft. Lap courses are not reliably countable due to heavy backlight/shadow on the red siding.
photos.notes: Dormer window sizes measured with the on-plane 36" WIN REF (~2.64 px/in on the dormer plane); main-wall openings and eave measured with the on-plane 444" WALL REF. Dormer window along_wall_ft values are approximate (set-back plane). Lower wall partially hidden by woodpile so window sill positions for w2/w3 are estimated; heights assumed to match w1. Brick chimney/wall of an attached structure visible at far right belongs to another elevation.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels REAR-LEFT and the photo shows the rear gable wall with patio door plus the long left wall receding to the right.
photos.eave_reasoning: Scaled from the patio door (nominal 80 in, ~245 px -> ~3.06 px/in) on the rear wall plane; grade-to-eave at the shared rear-left corner spans ~385 px = ~126 in = ~10.5 ft. Lap courses at 3.75 in exposure are too fine to count reliably at this resolution/angle, so pixel-scale with a same-corner reference was used. WALL REF 48 in bar sits on the upper dormer/second-story plane and was NOT used for the lower wall.
photos.notes: Blue WIN REF=36in bar sits on the shed-dormer window (dormer plane, ~2.1 px/in) and was applied only to that dormer window; the red WALL REF=48in bar is also on the upper plane, so lower-wall scale came from the patio door on the rear plane. Along_wall_ft for left-w2-far is rough due to heavy foreshortening and woodpile occlusion. Possible additional upper glazing behind/near the ref bars could not be confirmed.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo banner reads 'BACK ELEVATION' and the view is a square-on gable wall with patio slider matching the back-elevation annotation set (WALL REF = 324").
photos.eave_reasoning: Scaled with the same-plane WALL REF = 324" (≈910 px → 2.81 px/in); grade (~y770) to eave line (~y535) ≈ 235-330 px depending on grade point, and lap-course spacing (~10.5 px/course at 3.75" exposure) gives ~30 courses grade→eave ≈ 112-115" ≈ 9.5 ft; course count reported per rule 5.
photos.notes: Prompt labels this index 4 (manifest says rear-left, WALL REF=48"), but the delivered photo is clearly the BACK ELEVATION with WALL REF=324" and two 2SL pins matching the photo-5 annotation spec — extraction follows the actual image content. WIN REF=36" sits on the gable window; per rule 6 it was used only for that gable-plane window, while the main-wall window and patio door were scaled with the same-plane 324" wall ref. Gable window measures ~48x36 consistent with the 36" WIN REF height. Lap coursing is continuous into the gable (no shake detected on this elevation despite the SHAKE hint from photo 8).

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION and annotations (WALL REF = 84", WIN REF = 48") match the rear-right photo description; we see a gable-end rear wall with the right roof plane receding to the right.
photos.eave_reasoning: Red WALL REF bar (84") runs on the near corner wall plane from its eave line nearly to grade; measured 238 px = 84" (2.83 px/in), eave-to-grade span ~265 px ≈ 93.5" ≈ 7.8 ft on that same plane.
photos.notes: This photo matches the 'Photo 6 / REAR-RIGHT' annotation set (WALL REF=84", WIN REF=48") despite being given index 5. Gable wall in full sun with narrow-exposure red lap siding (consistent with 3.75" exposure). Courses visible but not resolvable enough for a reliable grade-to-eave count, so eave read anchored to the same-plane 84" WALL REF bar. Patio door dimensions carry moderate uncertainty due to perspective. No SHAKE accent visible on this elevation. Wall plaques/decor on the gable wall are not openings.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-RIGHT ELEVATION and the view shows the front gable with garage doors plus the right-side wall receding to the rear.
photos.eave_reasoning: Front garage-door heads (~84 in standard) sit roughly 1.5-2 ft below the front eave line on the same front wall plane, giving ~9 ft grade-to-eave; lap courses are too fine and foreshortened at this elevated angle to count reliably.
photos.notes: Refs visible in this photo: red WALL REF = 48" vertical on the upper right wall plane and blue WIN REF = 36" vertical on the front gable window — each used only on its own plane. Right-wall window positions (along_wall_ft) left null due to severe rake foreshortening; keep those openings rather than dedup on position. No SHAKE accents visible on these elevations — body and gable are all narrow lap consistent with the 3.75" exposure.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Single wall plane viewed nearly straight-on with driveway/truck to the left and front walkway visible; consistent with the right side of the house per photo sequence (front-right position).
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses from grade to the lower (main) eave line at 3.75" contractor-calibrated exposure: ~26 courses x 3.75 in = 97.5 in = 8.1 ft; course count is plane-correct and preferred over pixel scaling since the 180" bar sits on the set-back upper clerestory wall plane.
photos.notes: This photo carries the 180" (15 ft) contractor bar spanning the upper clerestory wall (x=0.328-0.646) — used for upper-plane widths only; the upper clerestory wall spans almost exactly the 15 ft bar. WIN REF 36" blue bar is vertical across the upper window height, anchoring that window at 36" tall. Lower main-wall eave was course-counted (plane-correct) rather than scaled off the cross-plane bar. Upper clerestory story adds roughly 5-6 ft of additional red lap-clad wall above the lower roof. No shake visible on this elevation despite SHAKE annotation on photo 8; right third of wall unmeasurable behind tree — additional openings may be hidden there.

raw_per_photo.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows a full straight-on gable-front wall with two garage doors and an entry door.

measurements._ai_reference_used: 3.75" lap course counts cross-checked against contractor WALL REF bars (324" front/back, 444" left, 180" right)
measurements._ai_story_count_reasoning: 7 of 8 photos read 1.5 stories (half-story/dormer massing above main eave); only the aerial photo 6 guessed 2, and its steep angle exaggerates the upper wall.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows a full-face gable-front wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~31 lap courses from the visible siding start line (just above grade, right of the trash bins) to the eave line at the left/right wall corners: 31 x 3.75 in = 116 in = 9.7 ft; cross-checked with WALL REF 324" pixel scale (~3.42 px/in) giving ~118 in — reporting course-based ~9.8 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-front garage wall, 324 in (27 ft) wide per WALL REF. Single 2SL slider (contractor-tagged) centered in gable. Small stone retaining wall at right edge is landscaping, not wall cladding. Contractor SHAKE annotation applies to photo #8, not this elevation; no shake visible on this wall — continuous lap siding body and gable.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-LEFT and the photo shows the long left wall plus the foreshortened front wall with garage doors at the corner.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~30 lap courses at 3.75" exposure from the siding start line (visible at the rear end near the AC unit and downspout) up to the main lower eave: 30 x 3.75 = 112.5 in ≈ 9.4 ft; mid-wall start line hidden by woodpile but course rhythm continuous at both ends.
_ai_photos.notes: WALL REF = 48" red bar sits on the upper-story wall plane near its eave; used course counting on the lower left wall instead of that cross-plane reference. Garage doors are heavily foreshortened so their widths/positions are rough estimates. Upper-story wall appears to be same red lap siding; a brick wall visible far right appears to belong to an adjacent/attached structure (matches brick noted on back elevation) — not counted against these walls. Woodpile hides start line mid-wall but it is visible at both wall ends, so start line was not estimated blind.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is banner-labeled LEFT ELEVATION and shows a single flat eave-side wall with a shed dormer, matching the 444" wall ref annotation.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Lap courses are not countable in this backlit, underexposed image; height taken by pixel scale from the 444" WALL REF on this same wall plane — siding start visible at the downspout (~y840) to gutter line (~y535) ≈ 300 px ≈ 102 in ≈ 8.5 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: Dormer windows sized with the 36" WIN REF that sits on the dormer plane itself (plane-correct for dormer sliders only). First-floor DH heights partially estimated — lower sashes of w2/w3 obscured by the woodpile. Course counting impossible due to backlit exposure; eave height from wall-ref pixel scale only, so reconcile against other photos. Brick visible at far right belongs to the adjacent front/right structure, not this wall.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-LEFT ELEVATION and the shot captures the rear gable wall plus the long left wall receding toward the street/truck at front.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap course rhythm on the rear gable wall near the shared corner: ~32 courses from siding start to eave line x 3.75in = ~120in = 10.0 ft; consistent with pixel check against the 80in patio door on the same plane (~121in).
_ai_photos.notes: Photo matches the pre-AI description for the REAR-LEFT view (WALL REF=48in red bar at upper eave, WIN REF=36in blue bar on the upper-story window). Course count (~32) is approximate — 3.75in courses are near the resolution limit at this distance, and the siding start line is partly hidden by shrubs/firewood, so start position was inferred from course rhythm (start_line_occluded=true). WIN REF sits on the upper set-back wall plane and was NOT used to scale the lower walls. Upper-story window plane appears to be a second-story wall above the lower roof (story-and-a-half massing). No SHAKE accents visible on this side despite the photo-8 SHAKE annotation hint — all visible cladding is narrow red lap. Small dark foundation band visible below siding start near the AC unit; excluded from eave height per rules.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads BACK ELEVATION and this is a full gable-end wall matching photo 5's annotation set (WALL REF=324", WIN REF=36", brick no-siding zone, two 2SL pins).
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~30 lap courses at 3.75" exposure from the siding start line (visible beside the patio door) up to the side eave line: 30 × 3.75 = 112.5" ≈ 9.4 ft; consistent with pixel-scale check via the 324" wall ref (~114").
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-end back wall, ~27 ft wide per 324" wall ref. Two contractor-pinned 2-Lite Sliders (gable + main level) and one sliding patio door. Brick no-siding zone at lower left is the contractor-annotated region and is excluded from siding. Gable window sized with the 36" WIN REF despite banner partially covering it. No SHAKE accent observed on this elevation despite pattern hint — wall and gable are consistent narrow lap.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels this REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION; corner view showing the gable-end right wall with patio door plus a lower back-wall section receding left behind trees.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Individual lap courses are not resolvable at this resolution so no honest count was possible; used the same-plane WIN REF (48" ≈ 95 px → ~1.98 px/in at window depth) giving eave-to-siding-start ≈ 230 px ≈ 123 in ≈ 10.2 ft, cross-checked against patio door head height (~80 in door + ~44 in wall above door ≈ 124 in). Note the WALL REF 84" at the left corner measures the LOWER left roof section's wall, not this taller gable wall.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable wall (right) has a brick/masonry band roughly 1.5 ft tall below the siding start — excluded from eave height per rules. WALL REF 84" vertical belongs to the shorter left-section wall at the corner (eave ~7 ft on that section), while the gable section eave reads ~10.2 ft — likely a split-level. Checked for SHAKE matching the photo-8 annotation: no shake visible on this elevation, gable is lap. No yellow contractor pins in this photo, so all styles are photo-inferred.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: On-photo banner reads FRONT-RIGHT ELEVATION and the gable wall with garage doors plus the long right wall are both visible from an elevated corner vantage.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Not measurable: steep downward camera angle compresses the wall vertically and lap courses are not individually resolvable/countable at this resolution; the 48" WALL REF sits on the upper right-wall section only and the lower-wall base is hidden by landscaping.
_ai_photos.notes: Photo shows an attached garage gable (front) with two garage doors and a service entry door, plus the long right wall of the house with an upper wall section above a lower roof plane. Right-wall along_wall_ft positions omitted due to severe foreshortening — reconciler should keep rather than dedup those openings. No SHAKE accent visible on either wall in this photo despite the matching-pattern hint; all visible siding is narrow-exposure horizontal lap consistent with the 3.75" contractor exposure. Course counting was not possible from this elevated angle. WALL REF=48" red bar spans the upper right-wall height near the ridge, used as local scale for right-wall upper window.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Pre-annotation labels this shot front-right; view is nearly head-on to one long wall with a clerestory/upper half-story above, corner downspout at right edge.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~26 lap courses from estimated siding start line (partially hidden by shrubs/grass) to the lower eave band; 26 x 3.75 in = 97.5 in ~ 8.1 ft. Pixel check against contractor 180-in bar (~2.83 px/in) gave ~7.3-8 ft on the same wall; course count reported per rule 5.
_ai_photos.notes: Photo carries the contractor 180-in WALL REF bar (matches the described normalized coords) and a 36-in vertical WIN REF on the upper window — upper window height taken directly from that ref, width scaled off the 180-in bar (~2.83 px/in). Lower-left window is partially occluded by the spruce, so its width is a low-confidence estimate. Siding start line is intermittently hidden by shrubs/landscaping so start_line_occluded=true; no foundation band included in the height. No SHAKE accent visible on this wall despite the matching-pattern hint — wall reads as uniform 4-in lap.

raw_ai.reference_used: 3.75" lap course counts cross-checked against contractor WALL REF bars (324" front/back, 444" left, 180" right)
raw_ai.story_count_reasoning: 7 of 8 photos read 1.5 stories (half-story/dormer massing above main eave); only the aerial photo 6 guessed 2, and its steep angle exaggerates the upper wall.
raw_ai.roof_type_reasoning: Gable triangles confirmed on front (photos 0,6), back (photos 3,4) and a rear-right gable section (photo 5), plus a shed dormer with two sliders on the left slope (photo 2, dormers_observed_count=1) which upgrades the type per rule 4.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct left elevation, dormer face parallel to camera, both dormer sliders full-front, 444" same-wall ref
corroborates dormer existence (two upper-plane sliders visible) but corner foreshortening — no valid width; extraction did not flag it as a dormer
rejected for width — upper-story window seen at extreme foreshortening (rl-w5, along_wall_ft null)

dormers._reconciliation_note: Single physical shed dormer on the left slope; only photo 2 measured it directly (15 ft, 75 sqft face) so width_source=direct_single_reading (amber — no second direct view to cross-check). Offset +1 ft from wall centerline derived from its two window positions (14.7/20.5 ft on a 37 ft wall); knee wall ~2 ft estimated from 5 ft avg face height minus 3 ft window.

walls.label: front
walls.confidence_reasoning: Head-on view with 324" wall ref and a clean 31-course count; only one direct eave reading exists so no cross-check.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct front view, 31 courses × 3.75" = 9.7-9.8 ft, cross-checked vs 324" ref
rejected — front wall >45° foreshortened at the corner; its 9.4 reading belongs to the left wall
rejected for eave — elevated/aerial vantage compresses the wall; gable triangle 8.0 used as corroboration only

walls._reconciliation_note: Width from 324" WALL REF (27 ft). Eave from the single valid direct reading (photo 0 course count, 9.8 ft) — amber single-reading. Gable triangle 8.2 ft kept from photo 0; photo 6's 8.0 agrees within 0.2 ft.

walls.label: back
walls.confidence_reasoning: Two independent course counts agree within 0.6 ft and the 324" ref pins the width; brick zone is contractor-annotated.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — 32-course count on the rear gable wall (start line partly occluded)
kept — 30-course count, head-on with 324" ref pixel cross-check
rejected — back wall almost entirely obscured by tree; no reading attempted

walls._reconciliation_note: Two count-derived direct readings (9.4, 10.0) agree within the ±1 ft band; both are counts so median of counts = 9.7 ft, direct_consensus (2 photos). Gable triangle: photos disagree 9.5 vs 8.3; kept photo 4's 8.3 (head-on, 324" ref-scaled) over photo 3's corner read. Siding 89% after excluding the contractor-annotated ~40 sqft brick zone from ~374 sqft wall+gable area.

walls.label: left
walls.confidence_reasoning: 444" full-span ref pins the width and two direct readings agree within 1 ft, but woodpile/backlighting degraded both photos.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — 30-course count on this wall with start line visible at both ends
valid direct view but pixel-scale only (backlit, courses uncountable); within 1 ft of the count so superseded by the count-derived value per rule 1d
rejected for this wall — its 10.0 reading was for the back gable wall; left wall heavily foreshortened here

walls._reconciliation_note: Width 37 ft from 444" WALL REF. Two direct readings (9.4 count, 8.5 pixel) agree within the ±1 ft band → count-derived 9.4 kept per rule 1d (counts outrank pixel reads), direct_consensus. Dormer face 75 sqft from photo 2.

walls.label: right
walls.confidence_reasoning: No full-span ref covered this wall, the two direct readings disagree by 2.1 ft, and photos indicate split-level massing on this side.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — 26-course count on a near-frontal view; strongest evidence class despite occluded start line (pixel cross-check 7.3-8 ft agrees)

rejected for eave — pixel WIN-REF read on the taller rear gable section of a likely split-level (its own note flags the 84" ref section at ~7 ft); disagrees with the course count by >1 ft. Gable triangle 8.0 kept from this photo
rejected — aerial; extraction itself declined to measure

walls._reconciliation_note: No ref covered the right span — width copied from the opposite (left) wall, assumed_symmetric (amber). Eave readings disagreed >1 ft (8.1 count vs 10.2 pixel); kept the course-counted 8.1 per rule 1b, direct_disagreement. Photo 5 reports a rear-right gable section (8.0 ft triangle) that photo 7's spruce-blocked frontal view could not confirm — kept with reduced wall confidence.

openings._reconciliation_note: Contractor-pinned gable slider on photo 0 @13.5 ft; photo 6's aerial gable sighting (22×36 @8 ft) folded in — photo 0 confirms a single centered gable window, aerial size/position rejected.
photo 0 @7.0 ft, photo 1 @8 ft (foreshortened), photo 6 @3 ft (aerial) → same left garage door; head-on photo 0 sizes kept.

photo 0 @18.2 ft, photo 1 @17 ft, photo 6 @12 ft (aerial, 192" width rejected as perspective smear) → same right garage door; photo 0 sizes kept. Both garage doors preserved as a pair — 11 ft separation exceeds sum-of-widths.
photo 0 @24.3 ft vs photo 6 @22 ft — delta 2.3 ft is borderline over the ±2 gate, but both photos show exactly one service door at the right end of the 27 ft garage wall; merged, head-on sizes kept.
Contractor-pinned gable slider, photo 4 @13.2 ft; photo 3's gable-upper sighting (28×34 @9 ft, conf 45) is the same centered gable window seen obliquely — folded, pin size kept.

Contractor-pinned main-level slider @7.0 ft; photo 3's rl-w2 (18×30 @4 ft, conf 30) and photo 5's tree-obscured back-w1 (24×42, along null, conf 20) folded — photo 4's head-on pinned census shows exactly 2 sliders + 1 patio door on this wall.

photo 4 @20.0 ft (70×78) vs photo 3 @15 ft (72×80) — position delta 5 ft exceeds the ±2 gate, but sizes match within 2" and both photos show a single patio slider on a 27 ft wall; merged on the head-on photo 4 position.
Contractor-pinned DH @8.5 ft; photo 3's rl-w4 (24×30 @5 ft, conf 25, extreme foreshortening) folded as a low-quality re-sight.
Contractor-pinned DH @17.4 ft; photo 1's lo-w3 @17 ft matched by position — its 2-Lite Slider guess (conf 45) overridden by the pin.

Contractor-pinned DH @26.6 ft; photo 1's lo-w2 @27 ft dedups cleanly (delta 0.4 ft). Photo 1's lo-w1 @33 ft and photo 3's rl-w3 @23 ft attributed to origin-direction/foreshortening error in corner shots and folded here rather than emitted as phantoms — photo 2's direct 444"-ref elevation with 5 contractor pins is the census for this wall.

Contractor-pinned dormer slider @14.7 ft (twin with left-d1w2 @20.5 — both preserved, 5.8 ft separation < sum of widths means shared framing, they are twins not duplicates); photo 1's upper-plane pair (@24/28, corner-shifted) folded onto this twin set.

Second dormer slider @20.5 ft — twin preserved. Photo 3's rl-w5 (36×48, along null upper-story) folded here as the most probable re-sight of a dormer slider; noted rather than emitted to avoid a phantom third dormer window against photo 2's pinned census.

Photo 7 upper slider @19 ft (WIN-REF anchored); photo 5's right-w2 (40×33 @16 ft) matches at 21 ft under origin flip (37-16) → same window, photo 7 size kept; photo 6's right-w1-upper (null position) folded as the upper-window pattern match.
Photo 7 lower window @11.5 ft (spruce-occluded, width low-confidence); photo 6's null-position lower-window sighting folded as pattern corroboration (1 upper + 2 lower on this wall in both photos).
Photo 7 @26 ft; photo 5's right-w1 (48×43 @9 ft) lands at 28 ft under origin flip with width matching within 1" → same window; photo 6's third null-position sighting folded.
Only photo 5 (sunlit rear-right) saw this patio door on the right gable section; no photo 7 counterpart (spruce/shrubs block that zone) — kept as single-source.

_reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Confidence-weighted average of the 4 final wall eaves (front 9.8 @85, back 9.7 @80, left 9.4 @72, right 8.1 @55) = 9.35 → 9.4 ft; rejected photo 1's foreshortened front read, photo 2's pixel 8.5 (superseded by photo 1's course count on left), photo 5's 10.2 (taller split-level gable section), and photo 6 entirely (aerial).
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: Gable triangles seen on front (photos 0/6), back (photos 3/4) and rear-right (photo 5) — cross-gabled; photo 2's shed dormer (count=1) upgrades to gable-shed-dormer per rule 4.
_reconciliation_notes.dormers: Detected 1 shed dormer, on the left roof slope only — photo 2 is the only direct measurement (15 ft wide, 75 sqft face, two contractor-pinned 48×36 sliders) so width_source=direct_single_reading; photo 1 corroborated the dormer windows but never flagged the dormer and its corner geometry disqualifies it for width. No other photo reported a dormer on any other slope, so exactly one entry emitted.
_reconciliation_notes.story_count: 1.5 taken from 7 of 8 photos; photo 6's 2-story call rejected as aerial-angle exaggeration of the half-story massing.
_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: All 8 photos read continuous 4" lap on body, gables and dormer; only the contractor-annotated ~40 sqft brick zone on the back wall subtracts (≈97% of ~1550 sqft total). The photo-8 SHAKE annotation (59 sqft, 'other') is counted downstream from the box and was NOT re-emitted as an accent; no matching shake found on any elevation despite the pattern hint.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: Siding/trim/door from photo 4 (sunlit, head-on); roof from photo 3 (sunlit — photos 0/4 returned null roof). Backlit photo 2 and shaded photo 0 samples deprioritized per rule 6; door_hex is the dark sunlit patio door — the entry door reads white/trim-colored (#EDEBE6, shaded).
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: 39 raw openings across 8 photos → 16 after dedup. Key merges: 2 garage doors + entry door seen in photos 0/1/6 collapsed onto photo 0's head-on positions; back patio door photo 3 @15 vs photo 4 @20 merged (size match within 2", single door on wall); left wall folded photos 1/3 corner sightings onto photo 2's 5 contractor-pinned windows (dormer slider twins @14.7/20.5 both preserved); right wall merged photo 5's readings onto photo 7's via origin-flip position match (37−x) and folded photo 6's three null-position sightings as pattern corroboration per judgment — flagged in each entry's note since rule 3c would otherwise keep them. Right patio door is single-source (photo 5 only).

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows a full-face gable-front wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~31 lap courses from the visible siding start line (just above grade, right of the trash bins) to the eave line at the left/right wall corners: 31 x 3.75 in = 116 in = 9.7 ft; cross-checked with WALL REF 324" pixel scale (~3.42 px/in) giving ~118 in — reporting course-based ~9.8 ft.
photos.notes: Gable-front garage wall, 324 in (27 ft) wide per WALL REF. Single 2SL slider (contractor-tagged) centered in gable. Small stone retaining wall at right edge is landscaping, not wall cladding. Contractor SHAKE annotation applies to photo #8, not this elevation; no shake visible on this wall — continuous lap siding body and gable.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-LEFT and the photo shows the long left wall plus the foreshortened front wall with garage doors at the corner.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~30 lap courses at 3.75" exposure from the siding start line (visible at the rear end near the AC unit and downspout) up to the main lower eave: 30 x 3.75 = 112.5 in ≈ 9.4 ft; mid-wall start line hidden by woodpile but course rhythm continuous at both ends.
photos.notes: WALL REF = 48" red bar sits on the upper-story wall plane near its eave; used course counting on the lower left wall instead of that cross-plane reference. Garage doors are heavily foreshortened so their widths/positions are rough estimates. Upper-story wall appears to be same red lap siding; a brick wall visible far right appears to belong to an adjacent/attached structure (matches brick noted on back elevation) — not counted against these walls. Woodpile hides start line mid-wall but it is visible at both wall ends, so start line was not estimated blind.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is banner-labeled LEFT ELEVATION and shows a single flat eave-side wall with a shed dormer, matching the 444" wall ref annotation.
photos.eave_reasoning: Lap courses are not countable in this backlit, underexposed image; height taken by pixel scale from the 444" WALL REF on this same wall plane — siding start visible at the downspout (~y840) to gutter line (~y535) ≈ 300 px ≈ 102 in ≈ 8.5 ft.
photos.notes: Dormer windows sized with the 36" WIN REF that sits on the dormer plane itself (plane-correct for dormer sliders only). First-floor DH heights partially estimated — lower sashes of w2/w3 obscured by the woodpile. Course counting impossible due to backlit exposure; eave height from wall-ref pixel scale only, so reconcile against other photos. Brick visible at far right belongs to the adjacent front/right structure, not this wall.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-LEFT ELEVATION and the shot captures the rear gable wall plus the long left wall receding toward the street/truck at front.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap course rhythm on the rear gable wall near the shared corner: ~32 courses from siding start to eave line x 3.75in = ~120in = 10.0 ft; consistent with pixel check against the 80in patio door on the same plane (~121in).
photos.notes: Photo matches the pre-AI description for the REAR-LEFT view (WALL REF=48in red bar at upper eave, WIN REF=36in blue bar on the upper-story window). Course count (~32) is approximate — 3.75in courses are near the resolution limit at this distance, and the siding start line is partly hidden by shrubs/firewood, so start position was inferred from course rhythm (start_line_occluded=true). WIN REF sits on the upper set-back wall plane and was NOT used to scale the lower walls. Upper-story window plane appears to be a second-story wall above the lower roof (story-and-a-half massing). No SHAKE accents visible on this side despite the photo-8 SHAKE annotation hint — all visible cladding is narrow red lap. Small dark foundation band visible below siding start near the AC unit; excluded from eave height per rules.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads BACK ELEVATION and this is a full gable-end wall matching photo 5's annotation set (WALL REF=324", WIN REF=36", brick no-siding zone, two 2SL pins).
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~30 lap courses at 3.75" exposure from the siding start line (visible beside the patio door) up to the side eave line: 30 × 3.75 = 112.5" ≈ 9.4 ft; consistent with pixel-scale check via the 324" wall ref (~114").
photos.notes: Gable-end back wall, ~27 ft wide per 324" wall ref. Two contractor-pinned 2-Lite Sliders (gable + main level) and one sliding patio door. Brick no-siding zone at lower left is the contractor-annotated region and is excluded from siding. Gable window sized with the 36" WIN REF despite banner partially covering it. No SHAKE accent observed on this elevation despite pattern hint — wall and gable are consistent narrow lap.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels this REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION; corner view showing the gable-end right wall with patio door plus a lower back-wall section receding left behind trees.
photos.eave_reasoning: Individual lap courses are not resolvable at this resolution so no honest count was possible; used the same-plane WIN REF (48" ≈ 95 px → ~1.98 px/in at window depth) giving eave-to-siding-start ≈ 230 px ≈ 123 in ≈ 10.2 ft, cross-checked against patio door head height (~80 in door + ~44 in wall above door ≈ 124 in). Note the WALL REF 84" at the left corner measures the LOWER left roof section's wall, not this taller gable wall.

WALL REF 84" vertical belongs to the shorter left-section wall at the corner (eave ~7 ft on that section), while the gable section eave reads ~10.2 ft — likely a split-level. Checked for SHAKE matching the photo-8 annotation: no shake visible on this elevation, gable is lap. No yellow contractor pins in this photo, so all styles are photo-inferred.

photos.elevation_reasoning: On-photo banner reads FRONT-RIGHT ELEVATION and the gable wall with garage doors plus the long right wall are both visible from an elevated corner vantage.
photos.eave_reasoning: Not measurable: steep downward camera angle compresses the wall vertically and lap courses are not individually resolvable/countable at this resolution; the 48" WALL REF sits on the upper right-wall section only and the lower-wall base is hidden by landscaping.
photos.notes: Photo shows an attached garage gable (front) with two garage doors and a service entry door, plus the long right wall of the house with an upper wall section above a lower roof plane. Right-wall along_wall_ft positions omitted due to severe foreshortening — reconciler should keep rather than dedup those openings. No SHAKE accent visible on either wall in this photo despite the matching-pattern hint; all visible siding is narrow-exposure horizontal lap consistent with the 3.75" contractor exposure. Course counting was not possible from this elevated angle. WALL REF=48" red bar spans the upper right-wall height near the ridge, used as local scale for right-wall upper window.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Pre-annotation labels this shot front-right; view is nearly head-on to one long wall with a clerestory/upper half-story above, corner downspout at right edge.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~26 lap courses from estimated siding start line (partially hidden by shrubs/grass) to the lower eave band; 26 x 3.75 in = 97.5 in ~ 8.1 ft. Pixel check against contractor 180-in bar (~2.83 px/in) gave ~7.3-8 ft on the same wall; course count reported per rule 5.
photos.notes: Photo carries the contractor 180-in WALL REF bar (matches the described normalized coords) and a 36-in vertical WIN REF on the upper window — upper window height taken directly from that ref, width scaled off the 180-in bar (~2.83 px/in). Lower-left window is partially occluded by the spruce, so its width is a low-confidence estimate. Siding start line is intermittently hidden by shrubs/landscaping so start_line_occluded=true; no foundation band included in the height. No SHAKE accent visible on this wall despite the matching-pattern hint — wall reads as uniform 4-in lap.

Window measurements should be within ±5% of this anchor.. measurements._ai_reference_used: Taped WALL REFs: 324" front/back spans, 444" left span, 84" on-plane corner bar (rear-right eave), plus per-window WIN REFs
measurements._ai_story_count_reasoning: All 7 valid photos independently reported 1.5 stories (split/raised design with habitable upper level and clerestory/dormer windows).

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows a full frontal gable wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Front is a gable wall; side-eave line at the wall corners read via pixel scale anchored to WALL REF = 324" (~3.4 px/in): siding start (~y920) to corner eave/soffit (~y505) = ~415 px = ~122 in = ~10.1 ft. Lap courses are too fine at this resolution/lighting to enumerate individually, so no course count is reported.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-front garage wall, 27 ft wide per WALL REF. Siding is continuous lap from near grade to peak (same profile in gable as body). Garage doors read ~98-100 in wide (likely nominal 9 ft) and ~74 in tall (likely nominal 7 ft) — mild pixel-quantization error. 2SL yellow-pin window sized with WIN REF 48" per-window scale. Evening/backlit sky; color samples taken from sunlit-ish wall face but treat as mixed quality.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads FRONT-LEFT ELEVATION and the photo shows the long left wall receding to a front wall with garage doors at the corner.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Course enumeration not possible — lap lines are visible but too fine and oblique to count reliably at this resolution. Pixel read instead: garage door (~84 in nominal) on the front wall plane gives ~2.1 px/in there; front-corner eave sits ~91 in above door head, giving ~175 in (~14.5 ft) siding-start-to-eave for the tall lower wall. Start line occluded by woodpile/bins, so start position estimated from course rhythm.
_ai_photos.notes: Split/raised design: tall lower wall (garage level + main level) with a set-back upper clerestory wall carrying its own eave. WALL REF (48") and WIN REF (36") are both on the upper wall plane, so they were NOT used to scale the tall lower wall (cross-plane); garage door on the same front plane was used instead. Along-wall positions measured from the rear corner of the left wall (the left corner as viewed from outside the left wall), assuming ~37 ft wall length from Photo 3's 444" ref. Brick section at far right may be an attached front section or neighboring structure — flagged as brick no-siding with low confidence. Upper window sizes anchored to the 36" WIN REF (~2.36 px/in on that plane).

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and shows a single eave-side wall with the 444" wall reference described for the left elevation.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Courses not enumerable (backlit, dark red siding at this resolution); pixel read on the left-wall plane using WALL REF 444" = ~1300 px (2.93 px/in): siding start near mini-split (~y870) to eave line (~y530) = ~340 px = ~116 in ≈ 9.6 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: WIN REF 36" sits on the dormer 2SL window (dormer plane, ~2.64 px/in) and was used only for dormer window sizing; main-wall windows and eave height scaled from the same-plane WALL REF 444" (~2.93 px/in). Woodpile hides window sills on w2/w3 so heights there assume symmetry with w1. Brick visible at far-right edge belongs to an adjacent wall plane (rear/right), not the left wall. Shed dormer face ~15 ft wide with two sliders. Lap exposure consistent with the contractor's 3.75" calibration but individual courses could not be reliably enumerated in this backlit exposure.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo carries a 'BACK ELEVATION' banner and shows a full gable-end wall with patio door; content matches the described BACK photo (WALL REF = 324", two 2SL pins, brick no-siding zone) despite the prompt indexing it as rear-left.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Pixel-scale read using WALL REF 324" = ~905 px (2.79 px/in), cross-checked by patio door (~218 px ≈ 80"): siding start near door bottom (~y770) to side-eave/frieze intersection (~y455) ≈ 313 px ≈ 112" ≈ 9.3 ft. Lap courses too fine/compressed to enumerate reliably at this resolution, so no course count is reported.
_ai_photos.notes: INDEX MISMATCH: prompt assigns index 4 (described as REAR-LEFT, WALL REF 48") but the supplied photo is the BACK ELEVATION matching photo 5's description (WALL REF = 324", WIN REF = 36", two 2SL pins, brick NO SIDING zone). Extraction reflects the photo actually shown. Gable window measured with the 36" WIN REF on its own plane (~2.56 px/in); lower window and patio door scaled with the 324" wall ref. Brick no-siding zone from contractor annotation, excluded from siding coverage. No shake accent observed on this elevation despite the photo-8 shake hint.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels this REAR-RIGHT and the annotation set for this frame (WALL REF = 84", WIN REF = 48") matches the rear-right photo description; patio/fire-pit area behind the house is visible.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: The red WALL REF = 84" bar runs on-plane along the corner/downspout from the eave line down to the siding start above the foundation band, directly giving a siding-start-to-eave height of 84 in = 7.0 ft for this wall section; individual 3.75" lap courses are visible but too fine to enumerate reliably at this resolution.
_ai_photos.notes: A reddish stone/parged foundation band (~10-14 in tall) is visible below the siding start near the corner and behind the patio; it is EXCLUDED from the 84 in eave height per rule 5. WIN REF = 48" was applied to the left slider on the gable wall (rr-w1) for per-window px-per-inch; patio door and gable window sizes are corrected for rightward foreshortening and carry ±10% uncertainty. The tree fully blocks the lower back wall, so additional back-wall openings may exist that are not readable here. Window in gable peak confirms 1.5-story habitable upper level on this section.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-RIGHT and the view shows the front gable/garage wall plus the receding right-side wall from a corner vantage.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Not measurable: the 3.75-in lap courses are too fine to enumerate individually at this resolution and oblique elevated angle, and the front wall is a gable end whose eave line is heavily foreshortened; refusing to back-derive a count from pixels.
_ai_photos.notes: WIN REF = 36" (blue) spans the front gable window height and was used for front-plane opening scale; WALL REF = 48" (red) sits on the upper right-side wall band near the soffit and was only used to sanity-check upper right-wall window sizes (same plane). Right-wall along_wall_ft values are rough due to foreshortening — front corner used as origin (front of house to viewer's left when facing right wall). No SHAKE accent visible in this photo despite pattern hint; all visible cladding is narrow-exposure red lap. Steep camera height prevented honest course enumeration for eave height.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Single wall face viewed nearly straight-on with driveway/truck at left; contractor sequencing suggests the right side of the house, though refs on this frame match the photo-8 annotation set (180"/36").
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Lap courses not resolvable at this compression for enumeration; pixel read of lower wall from visible siding start (left of walkway) to lower-roof soffit is ~275 px at 2.83 px/in from the contractor 15' (180") bar = ~97 in ≈ 8.0 ft. The 180" bar lives on the upper clerestory plane, so this is a cross-plane scale — flagged.
_ai_photos.notes: Upper clerestory/pop-up section spans the contractor's 180" WALL REF (15 ft) with WIN REF=36" confirming the upper window height. Its ~15 ft x ~4 ft face matches the contractor's SHAKE ~59 ft2 annotation, so it is referenced with from_annotation=true and not counted independently. Lower-wall window sizes used the upper-plane 180" bar (cross-plane) since no on-plane reference exists — treat those widths/heights as +/-10%. along_wall_ft left null on all openings because neither wall corner pair is cleanly visible (tree occlusion at right, frame edge at left). No door, garage, or masonry visible on this face.

raw_ai.roof_type_reasoning: Gable triangles observed on front (photos 0,6), back (photo 4), and right (photo 5), with shed dormer/clerestory structures reported on both left (photo 2) and right (photo 7) roof slopes.

openings._reconciliation_note: Photo 0 at 7.1 ft (direct, 324" ref) merged with photo 6 at 5 ft (rough foreshortened) and photo 1 (null along_wall); a 27-ft wall cannot hold 4 garage doors, so corner views were treated as re-sights of the same left door; kept photo 0's measured 98×74.
Photo 0 at 18.1 ft merged with photo 6 at 13 ft and photo 1 (null along_wall) — same right garage door seen from three angles; photo 0's direct ref-anchored size kept.

Contractor pin (Double Hung). Photo 1's occluded oblique lower-window sightings merged into photo 2's three pinned DHs — pins fix the left-wall count at exactly 3 DH + 2 dormer 2SL; photo 1 measured from the opposite corner origin.

Contractor pin; twin pair with back-w1 preserved (6.9 vs 13.3 ft, delta 6.4 ft > sum-of-widths ~8.2 ft/2).

_reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Confidence-weighted average of the four final wall heights (front 10.1 @75, left 9.6 @72, back 9.3 @78, right 7.0 @60) = 9.1 ft; rejected photo 1's 14.5 ft front read (occluded start, assumed-nominal scale), photo 7's 8.0 ft (cross-plane flag), and photo 3 (empty extraction).
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: Gable triangles seen on 3 walls (front photos 0/6, back photo 4, right photo 5) plus dormers_observed_count > 0 on photos 2 and 7 → gable-shed-dormer.
_reconciliation_notes.dormers: Detected 2 dormer/clerestory structures on different roof slopes — left (photo 2, 15 ft, two pinned sliders) and right (photo 7, 15 ft per contractor's 180" tape, shake face matching the 59 ft² annotation); different faces were never collapsed; each has only one direct width reading → direct_single_reading amber.
_reconciliation_notes.story_count: All seven valid extractions reported 1.5 stories; unanimous.
_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: ~1470 ft² total wall area with ~41 ft² annotated brick on the back (photo 4) excluded → ~96%; photo 1's 150 ft² brick was discounted as likely an adjacent structure per photos 1 and 2's own notes.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: Per-channel median of the three sunlit samples (photos 1, 4, 5) for siding/trim; roof from photo 1 (sunlit); door from photo 1 — shaded/backlit samples (photos 2, 7) excluded.
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: 34 raw openings across 7 photos → 23 after dedup. Front collapsed 15→5 (garage doors/entry seen in 3 photos each; physical wall capacity used to justify merging photo 6's rough offsets and photo 1's null positions); left collapsed 10→5 anchored by the 5 contractor pins with origin-flip reconciliation of photo 1; right kept all 9 candidates because photo 7's null along_wall_ft and >3" size deltas between photos 5/6 blocked dedup per rule 3c — right-wall count may be inflated, flagged for field verification; twins preserved on back (6.9/13.3 ft) and left dormer (14.8/20.7 ft).

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows a full frontal gable wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
photos.eave_reasoning: Front is a gable wall; side-eave line at the wall corners read via pixel scale anchored to WALL REF = 324" (~3.4 px/in): siding start (~y920) to corner eave/soffit (~y505) = ~415 px = ~122 in = ~10.1 ft. Lap courses are too fine at this resolution/lighting to enumerate individually, so no course count is reported.
photos.notes: Gable-front garage wall, 27 ft wide per WALL REF. Siding is continuous lap from near grade to peak (same profile in gable as body). Garage doors read ~98-100 in wide (likely nominal 9 ft) and ~74 in tall (likely nominal 7 ft) — mild pixel-quantization error. 2SL yellow-pin window sized with WIN REF 48" per-window scale. Evening/backlit sky; color samples taken from sunlit-ish wall face but treat as mixed quality.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads FRONT-LEFT ELEVATION and the photo shows the long left wall receding to a front wall with garage doors at the corner.
photos.eave_reasoning: Course enumeration not possible — lap lines are visible but too fine and oblique to count reliably at this resolution. Pixel read instead: garage door (~84 in nominal) on the front wall plane gives ~2.1 px/in there; front-corner eave sits ~91 in above door head, giving ~175 in (~14.5 ft) siding-start-to-eave for the tall lower wall. Start line occluded by woodpile/bins, so start position estimated from course rhythm.
photos.notes: Split/raised design: tall lower wall (garage level + main level) with a set-back upper clerestory wall carrying its own eave. WALL REF (48") and WIN REF (36") are both on the upper wall plane, so they were NOT used to scale the tall lower wall (cross-plane); garage door on the same front plane was used instead. Along-wall positions measured from the rear corner of the left wall (the left corner as viewed from outside the left wall), assuming ~37 ft wall length from Photo 3's 444" ref. Brick section at far right may be an attached front section or neighboring structure — flagged as brick no-siding with low confidence. Upper window sizes anchored to the 36" WIN REF (~2.36 px/in on that plane).

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and shows a single eave-side wall with the 444" wall reference described for the left elevation.

photos.notes: WIN REF 36" sits on the dormer 2SL window (dormer plane, ~2.64 px/in) and was used only for dormer window sizing; main-wall windows and eave height scaled from the same-plane WALL REF 444" (~2.93 px/in). Woodpile hides window sills on w2/w3 so heights there assume symmetry with w1. Brick visible at far-right edge belongs to an adjacent wall plane (rear/right), not the left wall. Shed dormer face ~15 ft wide with two sliders. Lap exposure consistent with the contractor's 3.75" calibration but individual courses could not be reliably enumerated in this backlit exposure.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo carries a 'BACK ELEVATION' banner and shows a full gable-end wall with patio door; content matches the described BACK photo (WALL REF = 324", two 2SL pins, brick no-siding zone) despite the prompt indexing it as rear-left.
photos.eave_reasoning: Pixel-scale read using WALL REF 324" = ~905 px (2.79 px/in), cross-checked by patio door (~218 px ≈ 80"): siding start near door bottom (~y770) to side-eave/frieze intersection (~y455) ≈ 313 px ≈ 112" ≈ 9.3 ft. Lap courses too fine/compressed to enumerate reliably at this resolution, so no course count is reported.
photos.notes: INDEX MISMATCH: prompt assigns index 4 (described as REAR-LEFT, WALL REF 48") but the supplied photo is the BACK ELEVATION matching photo 5's description (WALL REF = 324", WIN REF = 36", two 2SL pins, brick NO SIDING zone). Extraction reflects the photo actually shown. Gable window measured with the 36" WIN REF on its own plane (~2.56 px/in); lower window and patio door scaled with the 324" wall ref. Brick no-siding zone from contractor annotation, excluded from siding coverage. No shake accent observed on this elevation despite the photo-8 shake hint.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels this REAR-RIGHT and the annotation set for this frame (WALL REF = 84", WIN REF = 48") matches the rear-right photo description; patio/fire-pit area behind the house is visible.

Right-wall along_wall_ft values are rough due to foreshortening — front corner used as origin (front of house to viewer's left when facing right wall). No SHAKE accent visible in this photo despite pattern hint; all visible cladding is narrow-exposure red lap. Steep camera height prevented honest course enumeration for eave height.

measurements._ai_reference_used: Full-span WALL REF bars — 324" (photos 0/4, front & back spans) and 444" (photo 2, left span) — plus same-plane lap course counts for eave heights
measurements._ai_story_count_reasoning: All 8 photos independently reported 1.5 stories (raised half-story/clerestory band with shed dormer above the main eave).

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads FRONT ELEVATION and the photo shows a straight-on gable-front wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~32 lap courses from the siding start line (visible at grade beside the garage doors) up to the left eave/soffit line; 32 × 3.75 in = 120 in = 10.0 ft. Cross-checked against WALL REF 324 in pixel scale which agreed within tolerance.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-front garage wall, single continuous plane so gable window shares the main wall plane (no cross-plane scaling needed). Stacked stone retaining wall abuts the right corner but is landscape, mostly not on the wall face — small overlap counted. No visible foundation band; siding runs nearly to the concrete apron. Roof surface not visible from this straight-on angle, so roof_hex null.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation label 'FRONT-LEFT ELEVATION' matches view: long left wall receding left, garage doors on front wall at right.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~29 lap courses at 3.75" exposure from the visible siding start line near the rear end of the left wall up to the main (lower) eave: 29 x 3.75 = 108.75 in ≈ 9.1 ft. Upper clerestory band adds roughly 4 ft more above the lower roof (WALL REF 48" spans that upper band).
_ai_photos.notes: Split/raised upper clerestory band with two windows sits above the main left-wall eave; WALL REF 48" spans that upper wall band, giving ~4 ft of additional siding above the lower roof. Course count for eave height taken near the rear end of the left wall where the siding start line is visible past the woodpile; woodpile occludes the start line along most of the wall's middle. Lower-wall window heights sized via course counting on their own plane (WIN REF 36" is on the upper clerestory plane — not used cross-plane for the main wall). Brick section at far right may be an attached rear/right structure (consistent with brick NO SIDING noted on the back elevation) — flagged as no_siding but partially obscured. Garage door far edge (g2) heavily foreshortened; width assumed to match g1. Sun flare across lower frame; colors are approximate.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and matches the Photo-3 annotation set (WALL REF = 444", 3 DH + 2 2SL pins) viewed square-on.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Backlighting makes individual 3.75" lap courses uncountable, so I anchored to the 444" WALL REF on this same wall plane (~2.93 px/in): eave line to estimated siding start line spans ~270 px ≈ 92 in ≈ 7.7 ft; start line position estimated from the visible left-end grade/bush line since the wood pile occludes most of it.
_ai_photos.notes: Dormer window sizing used the 36" WIN REF on the dormer plane (~2.64 px/in on that plane only); first-floor windows used the 444" wall-plane scale. Full-width shed dormer suggests a 1.5-story layout. Brick visible at the far right belongs to the adjacent front/right face, not this wall. No SHAKE accent detected on this elevation despite the matching-pattern hint — wall and dormer both read as lap. Lower-wall readings behind the wood pile are estimates; along_wall positions for those windows are still reliable since heads are visible.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-LEFT and photo matches annotation set for photo 4 (WALL REF = 48", WIN REF = 36"); a gable end wall and a long receding eave wall meet at the near corner.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses on the back gable wall from the visible siding start line (above the dark parged foundation band) to the left eave return: ~34 courses × 3.75" exposure ≈ 127.5" ≈ 10.6 ft. Course resolution degrades near the eave so ±2 courses.
_ai_photos.notes: Back gable wall carries the patio slider; a raised half-story/clerestory section sits above the left wall plane — the 48" WALL REF and 36" WIN REF in this photo are on that upper set-back plane, so per rule 6 they were NOT used to scale the main-wall eave (course count used instead). Dark red parged foundation band (~12-18" exposed) visible below the siding start on the back wall — excluded from course count per rule 5. rl-w4 sized from the 36" WIN REF on its own plane. Openings rl-d1/rl-w1/rl-w2 likely overlap with BACK elevation photo pins; rl-w3 likely overlaps LEFT elevation photo. No shake accents visible on these walls despite the SHAKE annotation hint elsewhere.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is banner-labeled BACK ELEVATION and shows a full gable-end wall with patio door matching the back-wall description.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~29 lap courses from the visible siding start line up to the side-eave line along the left (rear-right corner) edge; 29 x 3.75 in = 108.75 in ≈ 9.1 ft. Brick band at lower left excluded from count.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-end back wall, continuous lap siding from start line through gable peak. WIN REF 36" vertical anchor on the gable window is same-plane (gable is coplanar with the back wall). Contractor-annotated brick band at lower left excluded from siding area and from the course count. Patio door glass prevents door color sampling. No SHAKE accent visible on this elevation despite pattern hint.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION and the view shows a backyard patio with the gable-end rear wall plus a glimpse of the right-side eave return at the corner.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: WIN REF = 48" spans the white-trimmed window on the main gable-end wall (same plane), giving ~2.0 px/in; eave line (~y480) to siding start above the masonry foundation band (~y695) is ~215 px ≈ 106 in ≈ 8.9 ft. Lap courses too faint at this compression to count reliably. The separate WALL REF = 84" bar sits on the lower left-wing plane and pegs that wing's eave at 7.0 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: MISMATCH: prompt says index 5 = BACK elevation with WALL REF = 324", but this image's banner reads REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION with WALL REF = 84" / WIN REF = 48" (matching the photo-6 description); I anchored to the refs actually visible in THIS photo. Patio door width is foreshortened toward the right corner (~45 in raw pixel read), reported as standard 72 in with reduced confidence. Reddish masonry/sandstone foundation band (~1 ft tall) visible below the siding start — excluded from eave height per rule 5. WALL REF 84" bar gives the lower left-wing eave at 7.0 ft on its own plane. Gable window is on the same gable-end plane as the main wall, so same-plane scale applies. Wing-w1 heavily obscured by tree; dimensions are rough. No SHAKE accent visible on this elevation despite matching-pattern hint — all visible cladding is narrow lap.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-RIGHT; gable wall with garage doors (front) and receding right wall are both visible.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Not measurable: steep downward camera angle collapses the vertical course rhythm and the siding start line is hidden by shrubs/mulch on both visible walls; no confident bottom-of-first-course anchor to run a count from. The red WALL REF = 48" spans only the upper right wall band above the lower roof, not a full eave height.
_ai_photos.notes: Photo banner reads FRONT-RIGHT ELEVATION with WALL REF = 48" (red, on the upper right wall band above the lower roof plane) and WIN REF = 36" (blue, on the gable window) — these match the Photo 7 description rather than the Photo 6 (rear-right, 84") description; extracted per what is actually visible. The 48" wall ref suggests the upper wall above the lower roof is ~4 ft tall (1.5-story knee-wall configuration). Right-wall window sizes carry extra uncertainty from foreshortening; along_wall_ft left null for right-wall openings to avoid false dedup. Siding start lines hidden by shrubs/grade transition, so no course-count eave height from this photo.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Paver walkway, front landscaping and driveway with truck suggest a front-facing view of the main wall with a clerestory pop-up above; contractor bar in frame matches the 180" spec.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap course rhythm on the main lower wall from estimated siding start (hidden by grass/shrubs) to the lower eave: ~28 courses x 3.75 in = ~105 in = 8.8 ft; start line estimated from course rhythm, not grade.
_ai_photos.notes: Image content matches the photo-8 spec (WALL REF = 180", WIN REF = 36") — the 180" bar spans the clerestory pop-up wall (~15 ft wide, ~3.5-4 ft tall above the lower roof). Clerestory window sized off the 36" WIN REF on its own plane. Lower-left window heavily occluded by evergreen — width is an estimate; reconciler should prefer other elevations for it. Contractor SHAKE ≈ 59 ft² annotation belongs to this frame per the brief but no hatched shake region is distinctly resolvable here; not re-emitted per double-count rule. Course count run mid-wall (no clean corner visible), hence anchor 'other'; siding start hidden by lawn/shrubs so start_line_occluded=true.

measurements._ai_roof_type_reasoning: Gable triangles observed on front (photos 0,6) and back (photos 3,4,5) walls, and photo 2 directly observed a full-width shed dormer on the left slope (dormers_observed_count=1), which upgrades gable → gable-shed-dormer.

_per_photo_readings.notes: Direct square-on left elevation; dormer face parallel to camera, both dormer windows full-front, width read against 444" wall ref — kept
Corner shot saw the upper clerestory band with two windows above the left eave — corroborates dormer existence/face but too foreshortened for width
180" clerestory pop-up bar likely spans the same raised half-story section seen from the front; different viewing face, not used as a left-dormer width source
48" ref on upper right band confirms ~4 ft knee-wall band but no dormer width readable

_ai_dormers._reconciliation_note: Only photo 2 gave a direct-view width (15 ft) so width_source is direct_single_reading (no cross-check available); face=left from photo 2's cardinal view; knee wall ~4.5 ft derived from 80 sqft face over 15 ft width; offset -1.5 ft from dormer window centers at 14/20 ft on the 37 ft wall.

_per_photo_readings.notes: Direct square-on left elevation; dormer face parallel to camera, both dormer windows full-front, width read against 444" wall ref — kept

180" clerestory pop-up bar likely spans the same raised half-story section seen from the front; different viewing face, not used as a left-dormer width source
