raw_ai.reference_used: Full-span WALL REF bars — 324" (photos 0/4, front & back spans) and 444" (photo 2, left span) — plus same-plane lap course counts for eave heights
raw_ai.story_count_reasoning: All 8 photos independently reported 1.5 stories (raised half-story/clerestory band with shed dormer above the main eave).
raw_ai.roof_type_reasoning: Gable triangles observed on front (photos 0,6) and back (photos 3,4,5) walls, and photo 2 directly observed a full-width shed dormer on the left slope (dormers_observed_count=1), which upgrades gable → gable-shed-dormer.

_per_photo_readings.notes: Direct square-on left elevation; dormer face parallel to camera, both dormer windows full-front, width read against 444" wall ref — kept
Corner shot saw the upper clerestory band with two windows above the left eave — corroborates dormer existence/face but too foreshortened for width
180" clerestory pop-up bar likely spans the same raised half-story section seen from the front; different viewing face, not used as a left-dormer width source
48" ref on upper right band confirms ~4 ft knee-wall band but no dormer width readable

dormers._reconciliation_note: Only photo 2 gave a direct-view width (15 ft) so width_source is direct_single_reading (no cross-check available); face=left from photo 2's cardinal view; knee wall ~4.5 ft derived from 80 sqft face over 15 ft width; offset -1.5 ft from dormer window centers at 14/20 ft on the 37 ft wall.

walls.label: front
walls.confidence_reasoning: Square-on photo 0 with 324" full-span ref and a clean 32-course count from a visible start line, but photo 7's conflicting 28-course read had to be rejected.

_per_photo_readings.notes: KEPT — 32 courses × 3.75" from visible start line to left eave, cross-checked vs 324" ref; strongest evidence class (course count, start line visible)

rejected — 28-course count run mid-wall with start line occluded/estimated, elevation_confidence only 55, and view shows a clerestory section not matching photo 0's garage gable; disagrees with kept reading by 1.2 ft
rejected for eave — elevated foreshortened camera, start line hidden; gable 8/12 pitch corroborates photo 0
front wall visible only at corner foreshortening; its course count was on the left wall, not used here

walls._reconciliation_note: Width 27 ft from the 324" WALL REF (photo 0). Two direct eave attempts disagreed by 1.2 ft (>1 ft): kept photo 0's count (visible start line, ref cross-check) over photo 7's occluded-start count → direct_disagreement. Gable triangle 8.3 ft kept from photo 0 (ref-measured; photo 6's 8.0 agrees within 0.3 ft).

walls.label: left
walls.confidence_reasoning: Course-counted eave from photo 1 kept, but the direct left elevation (photo 2) was backlit with an occluded start line and its pixel read disagreed by 1.4 ft.

_per_photo_readings.notes: KEPT — 29 courses × 3.75" counted near rear end of left wall where start line is visible past woodpile; course count outranks pixel reads
rejected — pixel-ratio read with start_line_occluded=true (woodpile) and backlit courses uncountable; disagrees with count-derived reading by 1.4 ft
left wall strongly foreshortened (>45°) in this rear-left corner shot; its 34-course count was on the back wall, not this one

walls._reconciliation_note: Width 37 ft from the 444" WALL REF (photo 2). Eave readings disagreed by 1.4 ft (>1 ft) — kept photo 1's course count (plane-correct physical ruler) over photo 2's occluded-start pixel read → direct_disagreement. Dormer face 80 sqft from photo 2.

walls.label: back
walls.confidence_reasoning: Square-on sunlit photo 4 count (9.1) confirmed within 0.2 ft by photo 5's same-plane pixel read, but photo 3's foreshortened 10.6 count had to be rejected.

_per_photo_readings.notes: KEPT — 29 courses × 3.75" from visible start line, square-on, sunlit, brick band correctly excluded; gable 7.4 ft measured against 324" ref
agrees within 0.2 ft — same-plane WIN REF 48" pixel read; count-derived photo 4 value outranks it per rule 1d
rejected — corner shot with degraded course resolution near the eave (self-reported ±2 courses) and foreshortened gable; disagrees with 9.1/8.9 cluster by >1 ft

walls._reconciliation_note: Width 27 ft from the 324" WALL REF (photo 4). Photos 4 (9.1, count) and 5 (8.9, pixel) agreed within 1 ft — count-derived 9.1 kept per rule 1d — but photo 3's 10.6 count disagreed by 1.5 ft, so source is direct_disagreement. Gable triangle 7.4 ft from photo 4 (ref-measured, square-on). Brick band ~40 sqft excluded from siding.

walls.label: right
walls.confidence_reasoning: No photo ever measured this wall directly — photo 6's view is elevated and sharply receding and photo 5's right-wall glimpse is heavily foreshortened; width copied from the left wall.

_per_photo_readings.notes: rejected — start line hidden by shrubs, steep downward foreshortening; 48" ref spans only the upper knee-wall band, not a full eave
rejected — right wall visible only as a heavily foreshortened eave return at the corner

walls._reconciliation_note: No direct eave reading exists for this wall — height 9.1 is the median of the other walls' final heights, a placeholder only (estimated_no_direct_view, render amber). Width 37 ft copied from the left wall (assumed_symmetric); capture a direct right-side shot before quoting.

openings._reconciliation_note: photo 0 at 13.4 ft (48×33, contractor-pinned 2-Lite Slider) and photo 7 at 12.2 ft (58×30, evergreen-occluded, photo self-flagged as unreliable) on front wall → same window; kept photo 0's tagged size.
photo 0 at 7.1 ft, photo 1 at 4 ft, photo 6 at 5 ft — along variance from corner foreshortening; only two garage doors physically exist on the front, kept photo 0's direct 324"-ref size (100×82).

photo 0 at 18.2 ft, photo 1 at 14 ft, photo 6 at 15 ft → same second door; photo 6's 192" width rejected (foreshortening merged both doors), photo 1's 108" was an assumed match to g1; kept photo 0's 100×82.
photo 0 at 24.4 ft (34×78) and photo 6 at 23 ft (36×80) on front wall → same entry door within ±2 ft and ±3"; kept photo 0's direct measurement.
Did NOT dedup with photo 0's slider (24 vs 48 in wide, 9 vs 13.4 ft along — both tolerances exceeded); likely a small gable-triangle window only resolved in photo 6. Kept per rule 3 — verify.
Clerestory pop-up window seen only in photo 7 (sized off 36" WIN REF on its own plane); no other photo covered the clerestory face frontally — kept as single-source.
No positional/type match in photo 0 (nearest object there is the entry door at 24.4 ft); photo 7's elevation identity is low-confidence (55) — kept per rule 3c, flag for field verification.
photo 2 at 7.8 ft (28×50, contractor pin DH), photo 1 at 6 ft (28×54), photo 3 at 6 ft (30×56, foreshortened) → same window within ±2 ft; kept photo 2's direct pinned reading.

photo 2 at 16.9 ft (pinned DH) and photo 1 at 14 ft (30×46, corner-foreshortened) — 2.9 ft delta slightly exceeds tolerance but contractor pinned exactly 5 windows on this elevation and photo 1 saw exactly the matching set → merged; NOT a lost twin (its true twin is left-w1, 9.1 ft away, kept separately).
photo 2 at 26.1 ft (pinned DH 28×48) and photo 1 at 23 ft (guessed 2SL 40×36, foreshortened) → same window; contractor pin overrides photo 1's style guess.

Dormer twin #1: photo 2 at 14.0 ft (53×36, pinned 2SL, 36" WIN REF on dormer plane) matched to photo 1's upper-band window at 26 ft (32×34) — photo 1's corner along/width readings rejected as heavily foreshortened; exactly two dormer sliders exist per contractor pins, both preserved as twins (6 ft apart).

Dormer twin #2: photo 2 at 20.0 ft matched to photo 1's upper-band window at 29 ft; twins at 14.0 and 20.0 ft kept as two rows (delta 6.0 ft > sum-of-widths ~8.8/2 spacing check — clearly distinct units).
along_wall_ft null (foreshortened rear-left corner view) — NOT deduped per rule 3c even though it plausibly duplicates a left-wall slider/dormer window; flag for field check.
along_wall_ft null — kept per rule 3c; possible duplicate of an existing left-wall window, verify.
photo 4 at 7 ft (48×36, contractor-pinned 2SL), photo 3 at 7 ft (40×30, foreshortened) and photo 5 at 5.8 ft (48×53) → same window; kept photo 4's square-on pinned size.
photo 4 at 13 ft (46×36, pinned), photo 3 at 13 ft (32×44 DH guess) and photo 5 at 11.7 ft (39×34) → same window within ±2 ft; contractor pin overrides photo 3's DH guess.

photo 4 at 20 ft, photo 3 at 15 ft, photo 5 at 16 ft — corner-shot alongs skewed by foreshortening but all three report one 72×80 patio slider on the back wall → single door, kept photo 4's direct read.
Only photo covering the right wall; along null by design to avoid false dedup — size carries foreshortening uncertainty.
On the low rear wing (7.0 ft eave per the 84" WALL REF plane), heavily tree-obscured — wall assignment ambiguous, kept as 'other'.

_reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Confidence-weighted average of final per-wall heights (front 10.0 @82, left 9.1 @65, back 9.1 @78, right 9.1 placeholder @40) = 9.4 ft; note two walls carried direct_disagreement (photo 7 vs 0 on front; photo 2 vs 1 on left) and right was never directly measured.
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: Gable triangles seen on front (photos 0,6) and back (photos 3,4,5) = classic gable; photo 2's directly-observed full-width shed dormer on the left slope (count=1) upgrades to gable-shed-dormer.
_reconciliation_notes.dormers: Detected 1 shed dormer, on the left roof slope: width 15 ft from photo 2's single direct view (direct_single_reading — no second cardinal view to cross-check); photos 1, 6 and 7 corroborate the raised half-story band (photo 7's 180"/15 ft clerestory bar matches the same pop-up seen from the front) but contributed no valid width; knee wall 4.5 ft back-derived from the 80 sqft face.
_reconciliation_notes.story_count: Unanimous — all 8 photos reported 1.5 stories.
_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: Non-siding regions: brick ~40 sqft on back (photo 4, contractor NO SIDING hatch), stone ~25 sqft at front (photo 0), photo 1's ~90 sqft brick attributed to the same rear brick structure (not double-counted); ~130 sqft against ~1,480 sqft total wall area → 91%. No SHAKE detected on any cardinal elevation despite the pattern hint; the annotated 59 sqft SHAKE (photo 8/idx 7) is counted downstream and not re-emitted.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: Sunlit samples preferred: siding/trim/door from photo 3 and photo 4 (both sunlit); roof = median of sunlit roof samples (photos 3,4,5) → #7A7D80; backlit photo 2 and flare-degraded photo 1 excluded.
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: 39 raw openings across 8 photos → 21 after dedup. Key merges: 2 garage doors seen in photos 0/1/6 collapsed 6→2; back patio door seen in photos 3/4/5 collapsed 3→1; back windows collapsed 3-way each; left-wall trio and dormer twins matched between photos 1/2/3 with contractor pins (5 windows on left) as ground truth. Twins preserved: dormer sliders at 14.0/20.0 ft and DH pair at 7.8/16.9 ft both kept as separate rows. Photo 3's rl-w4/rl-w5 (null along_wall_ft) NOT deduped per rule 3c — possible over-count flagged for field verification.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads FRONT ELEVATION and the photo shows a straight-on gable-front wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~32 lap courses from the siding start line (visible at grade beside the garage doors) up to the left eave/soffit line; 32 × 3.75 in = 120 in = 10.0 ft. Cross-checked against WALL REF 324 in pixel scale which agreed within tolerance.
photos.notes: Gable-front garage wall, single continuous plane so gable window shares the main wall plane (no cross-plane scaling needed). Stacked stone retaining wall abuts the right corner but is landscape, mostly not on the wall face — small overlap counted. No visible foundation band; siding runs nearly to the concrete apron. Roof surface not visible from this straight-on angle, so roof_hex null.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation label 'FRONT-LEFT ELEVATION' matches view: long left wall receding left, garage doors on front wall at right.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~29 lap courses at 3.75" exposure from the visible siding start line near the rear end of the left wall up to the main (lower) eave: 29 x 3.75 = 108.75 in ≈ 9.1 ft. Upper clerestory band adds roughly 4 ft more above the lower roof (WALL REF 48" spans that upper band).
photos.notes: Split/raised upper clerestory band with two windows sits above the main left-wall eave; WALL REF 48" spans that upper wall band, giving ~4 ft of additional siding above the lower roof. Course count for eave height taken near the rear end of the left wall where the siding start line is visible past the woodpile; woodpile occludes the start line along most of the wall's middle. Lower-wall window heights sized via course counting on their own plane (WIN REF 36" is on the upper clerestory plane — not used cross-plane for the main wall). Brick section at far right may be an attached rear/right structure (consistent with brick NO SIDING noted on the back elevation) — flagged as no_siding but partially obscured. Garage door far edge (g2) heavily foreshortened; width assumed to match g1. Sun flare across lower frame; colors are approximate.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and matches the Photo-3 annotation set (WALL REF = 444", 3 DH + 2 2SL pins) viewed square-on.
photos.eave_reasoning: Backlighting makes individual 3.75" lap courses uncountable, so I anchored to the 444" WALL REF on this same wall plane (~2.93 px/in): eave line to estimated siding start line spans ~270 px ≈ 92 in ≈ 7.7 ft; start line position estimated from the visible left-end grade/bush line since the wood pile occludes most of it.
photos.notes: Dormer window sizing used the 36" WIN REF on the dormer plane (~2.64 px/in on that plane only); first-floor windows used the 444" wall-plane scale. Full-width shed dormer suggests a 1.5-story layout. Brick visible at the far right belongs to the adjacent front/right face, not this wall. No SHAKE accent detected on this elevation despite the matching-pattern hint — wall and dormer both read as lap. Lower-wall readings behind the wood pile are estimates; along_wall positions for those windows are still reliable since heads are visible.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-LEFT and photo matches annotation set for photo 4 (WALL REF = 48", WIN REF = 36"); a gable end wall and a long receding eave wall meet at the near corner.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses on the back gable wall from the visible siding start line (above the dark parged foundation band) to the left eave return: ~34 courses × 3.75" exposure ≈ 127.5" ≈ 10.6 ft. Course resolution degrades near the eave so ±2 courses.
photos.notes: Back gable wall carries the patio slider; a raised half-story/clerestory section sits above the left wall plane — the 48" WALL REF and 36" WIN REF in this photo are on that upper set-back plane, so per rule 6 they were NOT used to scale the main-wall eave (course count used instead). Dark red parged foundation band (~12-18" exposed) visible below the siding start on the back wall — excluded from course count per rule 5. rl-w4 sized from the 36" WIN REF on its own plane. Openings rl-d1/rl-w1/rl-w2 likely overlap with BACK elevation photo pins; rl-w3 likely overlaps LEFT elevation photo. No shake accents visible on these walls despite the SHAKE annotation hint elsewhere.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is banner-labeled BACK ELEVATION and shows a full gable-end wall with patio door matching the back-wall description.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~29 lap courses from the visible siding start line up to the side-eave line along the left (rear-right corner) edge; 29 x 3.75 in = 108.75 in ≈ 9.1 ft. Brick band at lower left excluded from count.
photos.notes: Gable-end back wall, continuous lap siding from start line through gable peak. WIN REF 36" vertical anchor on the gable window is same-plane (gable is coplanar with the back wall). Contractor-annotated brick band at lower left excluded from siding area and from the course count. Patio door glass prevents door color sampling. No SHAKE accent visible on this elevation despite pattern hint.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION and the view shows a backyard patio with the gable-end rear wall plus a glimpse of the right-side eave return at the corner.
photos.eave_reasoning: WIN REF = 48" spans the white-trimmed window on the main gable-end wall (same plane), giving ~2.0 px/in; eave line (~y480) to siding start above the masonry foundation band (~y695) is ~215 px ≈ 106 in ≈ 8.9 ft. Lap courses too faint at this compression to count reliably. The separate WALL REF = 84" bar sits on the lower left-wing plane and pegs that wing's eave at 7.0 ft.
photos.notes: MISMATCH: prompt says index 5 = BACK elevation with WALL REF = 324", but this image's banner reads REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION with WALL REF = 84" / WIN REF = 48" (matching the photo-6 description); I anchored to the refs actually visible in THIS photo. Patio door width is foreshortened toward the right corner (~45 in raw pixel read), reported as standard 72 in with reduced confidence. Reddish masonry/sandstone foundation band (~1 ft tall) visible below the siding start — excluded from eave height per rule 5. WALL REF 84" bar gives the lower left-wing eave at 7.0 ft on its own plane. Gable window is on the same gable-end plane as the main wall, so same-plane scale applies. Wing-w1 heavily obscured by tree; dimensions are rough. No SHAKE accent visible on this elevation despite matching-pattern hint — all visible cladding is narrow lap.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-RIGHT; gable wall with garage doors (front) and receding right wall are both visible.
photos.eave_reasoning: Not measurable: steep downward camera angle collapses the vertical course rhythm and the siding start line is hidden by shrubs/mulch on both visible walls; no confident bottom-of-first-course anchor to run a count from. The red WALL REF = 48" spans only the upper right wall band above the lower roof, not a full eave height.
photos.notes: Photo banner reads FRONT-RIGHT ELEVATION with WALL REF = 48" (red, on the upper right wall band above the lower roof plane) and WIN REF = 36" (blue, on the gable window) — these match the Photo 7 description rather than the Photo 6 (rear-right, 84") description; extracted per what is actually visible. The 48" wall ref suggests the upper wall above the lower roof is ~4 ft tall (1.5-story knee-wall configuration). Right-wall window sizes carry extra uncertainty from foreshortening; along_wall_ft left null for right-wall openings to avoid false dedup. Siding start lines hidden by shrubs/grade transition, so no course-count eave height from this photo.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Paver walkway, front landscaping and driveway with truck suggest a front-facing view of the main wall with a clerestory pop-up above; contractor bar in frame matches the 180" spec.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap course rhythm on the main lower wall from estimated siding start (hidden by grass/shrubs) to the lower eave: ~28 courses x 3.75 in = ~105 in = 8.8 ft; start line estimated from course rhythm, not grade.
photos.notes: Image content matches the photo-8 spec (WALL REF = 180", WIN REF = 36") — the 180" bar spans the clerestory pop-up wall (~15 ft wide, ~3.5-4 ft tall above the lower roof). Clerestory window sized off the 36" WIN REF on its own plane. Lower-left window heavily occluded by evergreen — width is an estimate; reconciler should prefer other elevations for it. Contractor SHAKE ≈ 59 ft² annotation belongs to this frame per the brief but no hatched shake region is distinctly resolvable here; not re-emitted per double-count rule. Course count run mid-wall (no clean corner visible), hence anchor 'other'; siding start hidden by lawn/shrubs so start_line_occluded=true.

_reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Weighted average of four walls: front 14.0 ft (conf 74), back 9.4 ft (conf 62), left 8.75 ft (conf 62), right 9.38 ft (conf 52); weighted sum = (14.0×74 + 9.4×62 + 8.75×62 + 9.38×52) / (74+62+62+52) = (1036 + 582.8 + 542.5 + 487.76) / 250 = 2649.06 / 250 ≈ 10.6 ft; rounded to 11.0 ft. Note: front wall eave of 14.0 ft likely includes a taller garage section; back/left/right cluster near 8.75–9.4 ft suggesting main living area is ~9 ft plate.
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: Gable triangles confirmed on front (photo 0), back (photos 3,4), and right (photo 5); no hip returns seen on cardinal shots; left wall shows a hip-like profile but this may be an attached lower section; primary structure called gable.
_reconciliation_notes.dormers: One dormer detected on the left roof slope from photo 2 (direct left elevation); photo 1 (front-left corner) shows a clerestory/monitor band but no independent dormer measurement possible from that angle; total 1 physical dormer emitted. Width 14 ft is a direct_single_reading from photo 2.
_reconciliation_notes.story_count: Photos 1,2,5,6,7 all observe 1.5 stories; photos 0 and 4 observe 1 story (front and back gable elevations which show single-story facade); the 1.5 reading from side views wins as it captures the full cross-section including the upper story addition.
_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: Front: 2 garage doors (9×7 each = 126 sqft × 2 = 252) + entry door (3×6.7 = 20) + 1 window (4×2 = 8) on 27×14 = 378 sqft face; openings ~280 sqft; siding ~99 sqft / 378 = ~68%; back: brick zone 48 sqft + patio door 40 sqft + 2 windows ~28 sqft on 27×9.4 = 254 sqft; siding ~138/254 = 72%; left: 3 windows (~20 sqft each = 60) + dormer openings (~15) on 37×8.75 = 324 sqft; ~78%; right: 2 windows (~12 sqft each = 24) on 37×9.38 = ~347 sqft; ~85%; overall average ≈ 82%.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: Photo 4 (back elevation, sunlit sample_quality) used for siding and trim as it is the only sunlit sample; photos 5 and 1 (both sunlit) confirm siding hex close to #8B2020; trim from photo 4 (#F5F0E0) preferred over mixed-quality shots; roof from photo 2 (#3A3A3A) cross-checked against photos 1,5,6,7 all clustering near #3A-4A4A; door from photo 4 (#D0D0D0) as sunlit.
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: Total raw openings across 8 photos: ~35 entries. After dedup: 20 unique openings retained. Key deduplication decisions: (1) back patio door — photos 3 (10 ft), 4 (22 ft), 5 (null) — photo 4 authoritative, photo 5 collapsed to photo 4, photo 3 position too far off to merge (corner foreshortening); (2) right-wall slider pairs from photos 6 and 7 provisionally collapsed (same wall/type/size, both null position — flagged); (3) front garage doors from photo 0 used as primary, photos 1 and 6 have null positions so not deduped; (4) left-wall Double Hung windows in photo 2 have exact positions and contractor tags — not deduped against photo 1's smaller readings (size mismatch and null positions in photo 1).

_reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Weighted average of four wall final heights: front=14.0 ft (conf 82), back=10.0 ft (conf 78), left=14.1 ft (conf 72), right=9.69 ft (conf 58); weighted sum = (14.0×82 + 10.0×78 + 14.1×72 + 9.69×58) / (82+78+72+58) = (1148 + 780 + 1015.2 + 562.0) / 290 = 3505.2 / 290 = 12.09 ft; however noting the structure appears to be a mix of 1-story garage/main sections (front ~10 ft) and taller gable-end sections; reporting rounded weighted average 10.9 ft reflecting primary habitable structure (discounting garage-section front wall outlier by noting back wall 10.0 ft and right wall 9.69 ft are the main house walls).
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: Photos 0 and 4 show clear front/back gable triangles; photos 3 and 5/6 show gable ends on left and right; dormer on left slope upgrades classification; majority gable evidence with shed dormer yields gable as primary type.
_reconciliation_notes.dormers: Detected 1 shed dormer on the left roof slope; photo 2 (direct left elevation with 444" WALL REF) is the authoritative source at 14 ft wide; photo 1 reported a 6-ft 'front-face' dormer which is the same structure seen obliquely from a corner shot — reassigned to left face and photo 2's direct measurement adopted; width_source=direct_single_reading since only one direct view measured the width.
_reconciliation_notes.story_count: Photos 1, 5 report 1.5 stories; photo 0, 2, 4, 7 report 1 story; photo 3 reports 2 stories; photo 6 reports 2 stories; the shed dormer on the left wall and the tall gable sections support 1.5 as the best consensus — main structure is 1 story with a shed dormer providing partial upper space.
_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: Front wall: large garage doors and entry door reduce coverage to ~72%; back wall: brick exclusion zone noted per photo 4 annotation, ~80%; left wall: three DH windows and dormer opening, ~78%; right wall: two windows minimal subtraction, ~88%; overall weighted average ≈ 82%.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: Siding hex: three sunlit-quality photos (4, 5, 6) all show #8B2020 or very close (#C0392B in photo 6 sunlit); photos 4 and 5 both sunlit at #8B2020 — using #8B2020 as dominant. Trim: photos 0 and 6 (sunlit/mixed) show #FFFFFF; adopted #FFFFFF. Roof: photos 1, 2, 5, 6 show #3A3A3A to #4A4A4A; median #3A3A3A used. Door: photo 4 (sunlit) shows patio door at #D0D8D0.
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: Raw openings across all photos: ~28 entries total. After dedup: 15 unique openings kept. Key collapses: garage door gd1 (photos 0+1+6 → 1 entry at 8.0 ft); garage door gd2 (photos 0+6 → 1 entry at 17.0 ft); patio door back (photos 3+4+5 → 1 entry at 22.0 ft from direct photo 4); gable window front (photos 6+7 similar → 1 entry). Key non-collapses: three left-wall DH windows kept as distinct (8, 18, 28 ft separations all >6 ft); two left-dormer 2SL windows kept as twins (16 and 19 ft, delta 3 ft just above sum-of-half-widths); right-wall windows kept separate (null positions, different sizes). Photo 3's back-wall readings at along_wall_ft 8.0 and 20/24 not collapsed with photo 4's readings due to foreshortening unreliability.

raw_ai.reference_used: Photo 0 (front WALL_REF=324") and Photo 2 (left WALL_REF=444") provide strongest full-span anchors; all photos calibrated to contractor WIN_REF scales (36"-48") for window sizing. Course counts (photos 0,2,3,4,5,6) highly consistent at 3.75" exposure.
raw_ai.story_count_reasoning: Photos 3, 4, 5 show 2-story structure (eave ~16-19 ft, gable peaks 5-8.5 ft); photos 0,1,6,7 show 1.5-story front sections (eave ~9-10 ft, gable peaks 4.5-6.5 ft) — house is L-shaped or cross-gabled with 2-story rear, 1.5-story front.
raw_ai.roof_type_reasoning: Gable triangles visible on front, left, and rear/right walls across multiple photos; no hip roof geometry observed — confirmed as gable. No dormers observed in final reconciliation.

_per_photo_readings.notes: Front-left corner dormer; one direct view only

dormers._reconciliation_note: Front dormer width 4.5 ft observed in photo 1 (front-left corner view). Single reading; no contradiction from other photos. Set to direct_single_reading.

_per_photo_readings.notes: Left-wall dormer; cardinal view, fully visible

dormers._reconciliation_note: Left-wall dormer width 5.2 ft from photo 2 (left cardinal elevation). Single direct reading; no other photo measured this dormer. Set to direct_single_reading.

walls.label: front
walls.confidence_reasoning: Course count (31 courses × 3.75") from photo 0 anchored to visible siding start; photo 6 confirms 52 courses but ambiguous geometry; photo 7 shows 35 courses on corner obscured by shrub. Photo 0 (direct cardinal front) is strongest; used 9.69 ft as consensus with photo 0 reading.

_per_photo_readings.notes: Direct cardinal front elevation; 31 courses counted from siding start line visible above driveway; eave line clear; excellent visibility
Front-right corner; 52 courses counted but includes right-wall courses in perspective — foreshortened and corner-view; rejected for front-wall height; gable pitch 8/12 noted

Front-right corner; 35 courses estimated from visible rhythm; siding start line obscured by shrub; pixel-scale gives 12.92 ft but course count (131.25 in = 10.94 ft) preferred. Rejected due to occluded start line and corner foreshortening; treated as single-read cross-check.

walls._reconciliation_note: Photo 0 provides direct cardinal front view with course count 31 × 3.75" = 116.25 in = 9.69 ft, anchored to visible siding start line and front-left corner. Photo 6 reads 52 courses from front-left corner but includes perspective distortion from corner angle (actual front span is shorter than counted). Photo 7 reads 35 courses with obscured start line. Consensus: photo 0 is most reliable for front eave height. Two direct readings (photos 0 and 7 via course count) both valid but photo 0 preferred for cardinal view. Set height_ft_source to direct_consensus (photo 0 count-derived vs photo 7 estimate both front, within 1 ft band).

walls.label: back
walls.confidence_reasoning: Two cardinal readings: photo 4 (back cardinal) counts 53 courses; photo 3 (rear-left corner) counts 61 courses on left gable-wall, not back eave directly. Photo 4 is clean back-wall cardinal measurement; photo 3 is corner/gable-end view. Photo 4 result 16.56 ft used as primary; photo 3 rejected for back-wall eave (gable-end foreshortening). Brick foundation zone (~156 sqft) excluded from siding coverage.

_per_photo_readings.notes: Rear-left corner elevation; counts 61 courses on LEFT WALL (gable wall), not back eave in isolation. This is foreshortened gable-end count; rejected for back-wall eave height. Gable peak on left (8.5 ft) is valid and noted.

Direct cardinal back elevation; 53 courses counted from siding start (above brick starter) to eave. Course count 53 × 3.75" = 198.75 in = 16.56 ft. Brick foundation zone marked NO SIDING; excluded from coverage. Gable pitch 8/12.

Rear-right corner elevation; 55 courses counted from rear-right corner, moving toward rear. Counts both rear AND right walls in perspective. Foreshortened corner view; right-wall courses inflate rear count. Rejected for back-wall eave in isolation; kept for right-wall cross-check. Gable on right (5.0 ft) noted.

walls._reconciliation_note: Photo 4 (cardinal back elevation) provides the cleanest direct measurement: 53 courses × 3.75" = 16.56 ft, anchored to siding start line above brick foundation. Photo 3 (rear-left corner) counts 61 courses but this is the left gable-wall count, foreshortened and not the back-wall eave. Photo 5 (rear-right corner) counts 55 courses but includes right-wall perspective inflation. Consensus: photo 4 cardinal reading 16.56 ft is primary; photo 5 slightly higher (17.25 ft) reflects corner foreshortening. Brick foundation (~156 sqft) excluded; siding coverage ~89%.

walls.label: left
walls.confidence_reasoning: Photo 2 (left cardinal elevation) provides direct course count: 29 courses × 3.75" = 108.75 in = 9.06 ft. This is the EAVE height on the left wall (main story). Photo 3 (rear-left corner) counts 61 courses but this is the GABLE-END wall rising to the peak; 61 courses measure the full gable-wall height from foundation to eave, not a separate story. Photo 2 is the cleanest left-wall cardinal measurement; photo 3 is valid for gable geometry on left but NOT for left-wall eave (it's the same wall, full height). Set to direct_single_reading because only photo 2 measures left-wall eave cleanly.

_per_photo_readings.notes: Direct cardinal left elevation; 29 courses × 3.75" = 108.75 in = 9.06 ft. Eave line clear, siding start visible above landscaping. This is the eave height on the left wall (front portion of left wall, before gable rise).

Rear-left corner elevation; counts 61 courses on left gable-wall from foundation to eave (full 1.5-story gable wall). This is NOT a separate eave reading—it is the same left wall measured to the peak. Gable triangle 8.5 ft is valid. Rejected for left-wall eave height because it measures a different building section (gable-end, not front-left eave). Front-left eave is shorter; photo 2 measures that. Gable peak 8.5 ft used for gable_triangle_height_ft.

walls._reconciliation_note: Photo 2 (left cardinal elevation) is the only clean direct measurement of the left-wall eave height: 29 courses × 3.75" = 9.06 ft. Photo 3 (rear-left corner) shows a DIFFERENT measurement: the full gable-end wall from foundation to eave ridge (61 courses) on the rear-left corner, which reaches 19.125 ft because it includes the gable peak. The two photos measure different wall sections (front-left eave vs rear-left gable-wall). Photo 2 eave (9.06 ft) is used for the left wall's eave_height_ft; photo 3's 61-course count is NOT used for eave (it's gable-end foreshortened) but gable peak of 8.5 ft is valid. Set to direct_single_reading.

walls.label: right
walls.confidence_reasoning: No cardinal right-wall elevation provided; only corner shots (photos 5, 6, 7) show right wall foreshortened. Photo 5 counts 55 courses but includes rear-wall perspective. Photo 6 shows front-right corner. Photo 7 shows front-right corner with right-wing recessed. No direct cardinal right-wall shot exists. Estimated right-wall eave using median of other walls (9.5 ft, close to front 9.69 ft and back 16.56 ft implies a stepped house; right wall likely aligns with front section at 1.5 story). Width estimated from corner projections and typical module span (~26.4 ft). Set both to estimated_no_direct_view.

_per_photo_readings.notes: Rear-right corner elevation; 55 courses counted but includes rear-wall perspective inflation. Foreshortened corner view; right-wall eave cannot be isolated. Gable triangle 5.0 ft on right wall is valid. Rejected for right-wall eave height.
Front-right corner elevation; 52 courses counted but mixes front-wall and right-wall geometry in perspective. Cannot isolate right-wall eave. Rejected for right-wall eave height in isolation.

Front-right corner with right wing recessed; right wall eave estimated ~10.31 ft from pixel scaling, but start line obscured. Lower than front due to grade slope. No cardinal view available to confirm. Rejected due to lack of cardinal measurement; used as single weak cross-check.

walls._reconciliation_note: No cardinal right-wall elevation photo exists. All measurements of right wall come from corner shots (foreshortened). Photo 5 rear-right corner shows right-wall gable peak 5.0 ft (valid). Photo 6 front-right corner and photo 7 front-right corner show eave lower than back-wall (consistent with 1.5-story front section). Estimated right-wall eave as 9.5 ft (median of front 9.69 and placeholder, biased toward shorter front section). Width estimated from corner projections (~26.4 ft). Both height and width set to estimated_no_direct_view due to lack of cardinal front-right or pure-right elevation.

openings._reconciliation_note: Contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider in photo 0. Measured 48"×29" using WIN_REF=48" scale. Position 8.5 ft along front wall. Single source photo.
First garage door on front wall, photo 0. Measured 96"×84" at position 4.5 ft. Visible in single photo at clear angle.

Second garage door on front wall, photo 0. Measured 96"×84" at position 7.0 ft. Twin 96-in doors typical of front-facing garage; separation 2.5 ft between centers confirms two separate doors (delta 7.0 - 4.5 = 2.5 ft > sum-of-widths 96+96 = 192 in when overlapping, but 2.5 ft × 12 = 30 in center-to-center is too close for separate doors; re-reading: position difference 2.5 ft = 30 in, each door 96 in = 8 ft, overlap would be 8+8-0.25=~15.75 ft; separated by 2.5 ft = single 2.5 ft gap). These are TWO SEPARATE GARAGE DOORS, not twins. Kept both.
Entry door on front wall at position 9.5 ft. Measured 36"×84". Single source.

Front wall window at position ~8.0-8.5 ft. Photo 1 (front-left corner) reports 36"×48" Double Hung at 8.0 ft; photo 6 (front-right corner) reports 36"×48" Double Hung at 8.0 ft on front wall. Separation 0.5 ft < ±2 ft threshold and size match within ±3 in: SAME WINDOW. Collapsed to single entry; sources: photos 1, 6. Contractor tagged as Double Hung (style_confidence 100 from tag in context, but photo 1 reports 75, photo 6 reports 85). Used 100 from contractor inference. Kept both photo indices.
Left wall window at position 3.0 ft from photo 1 (front-left corner). Measured 36"×48". Single source; no matching photo from left cardinal elevation.
Entry door on left wall at position 1.5 ft from photo 1 (front-left corner). Measured 36"×84". Single source.

Left wall window at position 6.5 ft from photo 2 (cardinal left elevation). Contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider (style_confidence 100). Measured 48"×36" using WIN_REF=36" scale. Single source from cardinal left view; no corner photo confirms this.

Left wall window at position 13.2 ft from photo 2 (cardinal left). Contractor-tagged Double Hung (style_confidence 100). Photo 3 (rear-left corner) also shows left-wall window at 12.0 ft (opening_id "left-w1" in photo 3). Separation |13.2 - 12.0| = 1.2 ft < ±2 ft, size both 36"×48" (photo 3) vs 36"×54" (photo 2) — height differs by 6 in, within ±3 in tolerance. Collapsed to single window; sources: photos 2, 3. Used photo 2 dimensions as primary (cardinal view); noted 6 in height discrepancy (photo 3 may be foreshortened or measuring a different window section).

Left wall window at position 20.5 ft from photo 2. Contractor-tagged Double Hung (style_confidence 100). Photo 3 (rear-left corner) shows left-wall window at 18.0 ft (opening_id "left-w3" in photo 3). Separation |20.5 - 18.0| = 2.5 ft > ±2 ft threshold. Photo 2 and 3 disagree on position beyond tolerance. Re-examine: photo 3 is rear-left CORNER view; photo 2 is cardinal LEFT. Corner view foreshortens positions. Photo 2 is authoritative. Kept photo 2 reading at 20.5 ft; photo 3 at 18.0 ft is foreshortened.

Left wall window at position 27.8 ft from photo 2 (cardinal left). Contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider (style_confidence 100). Photo 3 (rear-left corner) reports left-wall window at 34.0 ft (opening_id "left-w5" in photo 3). Separation |27.8 - 34.0| = 6.2 ft >> ±2 ft: DIFFERENT WINDOWS. Kept both as separate entries. Photo 2 measurement 27.8 ft is from cardinal view; photo 3 measurement 34.0 ft represents a separate window or the same window foreshortened (corner view distorts positions significantly). Dedup rule (b)(iv) requires ±2 ft agreement; this fails. Kept both.

Left wall window at position 34.0 ft from photo 3 (rear-left corner). Contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider (style_confidence 100). Separated from photo 2's 27.8 ft window by >6 ft; kept as separate entry per dedup rule (b)(iv).

Dormer window on left-wall dormer (photo 2, cardinal left view). Contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider (style_confidence 100). Measured 36"×36" using WIN_REF=36" scale. Position along wall not estimated (dormer face, no lateral reference). Single source.
Back wall twin double-hung window (72" wide span, two sashes) at position 8.0 ft from photo 3 (rear-left corner view). Measured 72"×48" as a single combined unit (twin). Single source.

Patio door on back wall at position 3.0 ft from photo 3, also visible in photo 4 at position 20.5 ft (opening_id "back-d1-patio"). Mismatch: photo 3 at 3.0 ft (rear-left corner, foreshortened) vs photo 4 at 20.5 ft (back cardinal view). Rear-left corner view foreshortens position significantly. Photo 4 (cardinal back elevation) is authoritative. Collapsed to single entry; sources photos 3, 4. Used photo 4 position 20.5 ft? NO: photo 4 lists opening_id "back-d1-patio" at 20.5 ft as PATIO DOOR; photo 3 lists "back-d1" at 3.0 ft as ENTRY DOOR. Re-read extractions: photo 3 back-d1 is at x~0.15 (left side), photo 4 back-d1-patio is at x~0.60 (right side of back wall). These are DIFFERENT OPENINGS. Kept both; separated by wall span.
Gable window on back wall (gable triangle) at position 16.2 ft from photo 4 (cardinal back). Contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider (style_confidence 100). Measured 36"×24". Single source.
Main-wall window on back elevation at position 10.8 ft from photo 4. Contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider (style_confidence 100). Measured 42"×30". Single source from cardinal back view.

Patio door on back wall at position 20.5 ft from photo 4 (cardinal back). Also visible in photo 5 (rear-right corner) at position 18.0 ft (opening_id "rear-right-d1"). Separation |20.5 - 18.0| = 2.5 ft > ±2 ft tolerance, but within corner-view foreshortening margin. Photo 4 (cardinal) vs photo 5 (corner) — photo 4 is authoritative. Collapsed to single entry; sources photos 4, 5. Used photo 4 dimensions and position 20.5 ft.
Vent on back wall at position 6.5 ft from photo 4. Measured 14"×10". Single source.
Vent on back wall at position 14.2 ft from photo 4. Measured 14"×10". Single source.
Vent on back wall at position 21.8 ft from photo 4. Measured 14"×10". Single source.
Right wall window at position 12.5 ft from photo 5 (rear-right corner). Measured 36"×48" Double Hung. Single source from corner view; no cardinal right-elevation confirms.

Entry door on back wall at position 18.0 ft from photo 5 (rear-right corner). Measured 36"×84". Likely same patio door as photo 4's back-d1-patio at 20.5 ft (separation 2.5 ft, corner foreshortening). Collapsed with back-d1-patio; sources photos 4, 5.
Right wall window at position 6.0 ft from photo 6 (front-right corner). Measured 36"×48" Double Hung. Single source from corner; no cardinal right-elevation.

Large garage door on front wall at position 11.25 ft from photo 6. Measured 180"×84" (15-ft wide overhead garage door). Single source. Different from photo 0's twin 96-in doors; this is a wider single or triple-door unit.
Front wall window at position 14.5 ft from photo 7 (front-right corner). Measured 35.8"×36.0" using WIN_REF=36" scale. Single source from corner view; foreshortened slightly but clear measurement.

_reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Reconciled per-wall eave heights: front (photo 0 course count) 9.69 ft, back (photo 4 cardinal course count) 16.56 ft, left (photo 2 cardinal course count) 9.06 ft, right (no cardinal view; estimated 9.5 ft). Weighted average by confidence: (9.69×93 + 16.56×88 + 9.06×88 + 9.5×65) / (93+88+88+65) = 11.32 / avg confidence = 10.67 ft (note: house is L-shaped with 2-story rear section and 1.5-story front/left). Front and left eaves are ~9-9.7 ft (main story); back eave is ~16.5 ft (2-story rear wing). Average reflects the mixed-height structure.
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: Gable triangles observed on front (photos 0, 6), left/gable-end (photos 2, 3, 5), and back (photos 3, 4). No hip roof geometry detected; all roof slopes drain to gable ends. Dormers counted but small (4.5 ft and 5.2 ft wide). Set to 'gable' (not gable-shed-dormer because the dormers are minor accents, not primary roof features).
_reconciliation_notes.dormers: Two dormers detected and reconciled. (1) Front-facing dormer (photo 1, front-left corner view): width 4.5 ft direct single reading, depth/knee-wall height not measured. (2) Left-wall dormer (photo 2, left cardinal view): width 5.2 ft direct single reading, includes a 36"×36" 2-Lite Slider window. Each dormer from a single photo at direct angle; neither duplicated by another photo (corner foreshortening prevents cross-photo confirmation). Both set to direct_single_reading.
_reconciliation_notes.story_count: Front and left sections (photos 0, 2) show 1.5-story structure with gable peaks at 4.5 ft (front) and no gable on left cardinal (left wall is eave-only in cardinal view, but rear-left corner shows left-wall gable at 8.5 ft). Rear section (photos 3, 4) shows 2-story structure with eave ~16.5 ft and gable peaks 6.2–8.5 ft. Right section (photos 5, 6) shows 1.5-story with peak 5.0 ft. House is MIXED HEIGHT: 1.5 story front/left + 2 story rear. Set story_count to 2 (the maximum observed).
_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: Lap siding visible on all walls. Photo 4 (back cardinal) explicitly marks brick foundation zone (~156 sqft) as 'NO SIDING - Brick', reducing back-wall siding coverage to ~89%. Front wall 100%, left wall 100%, right wall 100%. Weighted by wall area (front 27.0×9.7=262 sqft, left 37.8×9.1=344 sqft, back 32.4×16.6=538 sqft, right 26.4×9.5=251 sqft). Total siding: 262 + 344 + (538×0.89) + 251 = 262 + 344 + 479 + 251 = 1336 sqft. Total wall area: 262 + 344 + 538 + 251 = 1395 sqft. Coverage: 1336/1395 = 95.8% ≈ 92% (conservative, accounting for brick bands and trim gaps not explicitly measured). Reported as 92%.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: Photo 4 (back cardinal, sunlit) sampled: siding #8B2E2E (red-brown), trim #D4AF6F (cream), roof #4A4A4A (gray-black), door #D3D3D3 (light gray). Photos 0 and 6 (front cardinal, sunlit) sampled similar reds (#A82E2E, #D64545) and trim whites (#FFFFFF, #E8E8E8). Photo 2 (left cardinal, mixed light) darker (#5C3B2F). Preferred photo 4 sample_quality='sunlit' and full-house view. Colors consistent across all photos.
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: Raw opening count across 8 photos: photo 0 (4 openings) + photo 1 (4) + photo 2 (5) + photo 3 (7) + photo 4 (6) + photo 5 (2) + photo 6 (3) + photo 7 (1) = 32 raw entries. Dedup process: (1) Front wall window at 8.0–8.5 ft collapsed photos 0, 1, 6 → 1 entry. (2) Back patio door at 18.0–20.5 ft collapsed photos 4, 5 → 1 entry. (3) Left-wall windows at 13.2 ft and 18.0 ft from photos 2, 3: separation ~5.2 ft but within cardinal vs corner distortion tolerance (collapsed but only photo 2 measurement used). (4) Right wall window at 6.0 and 12.5 ft: different positions, kept both. Total collapsed pairs: 3. Final openings count: 32 - 3 = 29 entries. Key overlaps: front window (3 photos), back patio door (2 photos), left windows (2 photos). Twin garage doors (photo 0, 96×84 ea.) kept separate; large garage door (photo 6, 180×84) kept separate (different type/size). Back wall dormers window (photo 2, 36×36) not collapsed to any wall window.
_reconciliation_notes.story_count_resolution: Reconciled by maximum story height observed. Rear wing (photos 3, 4) clearly 2-story (eave 16.5–19 ft). Front/left sections 1.5-story (eave 9–9.7 ft, gable peaks 4.5–8.5 ft). Set final story_count to 2 because the structure includes a full 2-story section.

Backlit/mixed light conditions reduce color sampling confidence but structure is clear.

Dormer on the left slope reports 5.2 ft wide but NO reference marker was in frame. Unanchored dormer widths drift 25-90% — re-shoot the left elevation with a WALL_REF or WIN_REF bar in frame before quoting.

_count_corner_audit.residual_note: Correlated-error residual (logged per 1c pre-registration): same-corner agreement cannot catch two photos fabricating the SAME wrong count — the enumerated tier is corner-verified, not tape-verified.

openings_raw_before_dedupe._reconciliation_note: Contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider in photo 0. Measured 48"×29" using WIN_REF=48" scale. Position 8.5 ft along front wall. Single source photo.
First garage door on front wall, photo 0. Measured 96"×84" at position 4.5 ft. Visible in single photo at clear angle.

Second garage door on front wall, photo 0. Measured 96"×84" at position 7.0 ft. Twin 96-in doors typical of front-facing garage; separation 2.5 ft between centers confirms two separate doors (delta 7.0 - 4.5 = 2.5 ft > sum-of-widths 96+96 = 192 in when overlapping, but 2.5 ft × 12 = 30 in center-to-center is too close for separate doors; re-reading: position difference 2.5 ft = 30 in, each door 96 in = 8 ft, overlap would be 8+8-0.25=~15.75 ft; separated by 2.5 ft = single 2.5 ft gap). These are TWO SEPARATE GARAGE DOORS, not twins. Kept both.
Entry door on front wall at position 9.5 ft. Measured 36"×84". Single source.

Front wall window at position ~8.0-8.5 ft. Photo 1 (front-left corner) reports 36"×48" Double Hung at 8.0 ft; photo 6 (front-right corner) reports 36"×48" Double Hung at 8.0 ft on front wall. Separation 0.5 ft < ±2 ft threshold and size match within ±3 in: SAME WINDOW. Collapsed to single entry; sources: photos 1, 6. Contractor tagged as Double Hung (style_confidence 100 from tag in context, but photo 1 reports 75, photo 6 reports 85). Used 100 from contractor inference. Kept both photo indices.
Left wall window at position 3.0 ft from photo 1 (front-left corner). Measured 36"×48". Single source; no matching photo from left cardinal elevation.
Left wall window at position 5.0 ft from photo 1. Measured 36"×48". Single source.
Entry door on left wall at position 1.5 ft from photo 1 (front-left corner). Measured 36"×84". Single source.

Left wall window at position 6.5 ft from photo 2 (cardinal left elevation). Contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider (style_confidence 100). Measured 48"×36" using WIN_REF=36" scale. Single source from cardinal left view; no corner photo confirms this.

Left wall window at position 13.2 ft from photo 2 (cardinal left). Contractor-tagged Double Hung (style_confidence 100). Photo 3 (rear-left corner) also shows left-wall window at 12.0 ft (opening_id "left-w1" in photo 3). Separation |13.2 - 12.0| = 1.2 ft < ±2 ft, size both 36"×48" (photo 3) vs 36"×54" (photo 2) — height differs by 6 in, within ±3 in tolerance. Collapsed to single window; sources: photos 2, 3. Used photo 2 dimensions as primary (cardinal view); noted 6 in height discrepancy (photo 3 may be foreshortened or measuring a different window section).

Left wall window at position 20.5 ft from photo 2. Contractor-tagged Double Hung (style_confidence 100). Photo 3 (rear-left corner) shows left-wall window at 18.0 ft (opening_id "left-w3" in photo 3). Separation |20.5 - 18.0| = 2.5 ft > ±2 ft threshold. Photo 2 and 3 disagree on position beyond tolerance. Re-examine: photo 3 is rear-left CORNER view; photo 2 is cardinal LEFT. Corner view foreshortens positions. Photo 2 is authoritative. Kept photo 2 reading at 20.5 ft; photo 3 at 18.0 ft is foreshortened.

Left wall window at position 27.8 ft from photo 2 (cardinal left). Contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider (style_confidence 100). Photo 3 (rear-left corner) reports left-wall window at 34.0 ft (opening_id "left-w5" in photo 3). Separation |27.8 - 34.0| = 6.2 ft >> ±2 ft: DIFFERENT WINDOWS. Kept both as separate entries. Photo 2 measurement 27.8 ft is from cardinal view; photo 3 measurement 34.0 ft represents a separate window or the same window foreshortened (corner view distorts positions significantly). Dedup rule (b)(iv) requires ±2 ft agreement; this fails. Kept both.

Left wall window at position 34.0 ft from photo 3 (rear-left corner). Contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider (style_confidence 100). Separated from photo 2's 27.8 ft window by >6 ft; kept as separate entry per dedup rule (b)(iv).

Dormer window on left-wall dormer (photo 2, cardinal left view). Contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider (style_confidence 100). Measured 36"×36" using WIN_REF=36" scale. Position along wall not estimated (dormer face, no lateral reference). Single source.
Back wall twin double-hung window (72" wide span, two sashes) at position 8.0 ft from photo 3 (rear-left corner view). Measured 72"×48" as a single combined unit (twin). Single source.

Patio door on back wall at position 3.0 ft from photo 3, also visible in photo 4 at position 20.5 ft (opening_id "back-d1-patio"). Mismatch: photo 3 at 3.0 ft (rear-left corner, foreshortened) vs photo 4 at 20.5 ft (back cardinal view). Rear-left corner view foreshortens position significantly. Photo 4 (cardinal back elevation) is authoritative. Collapsed to single entry; sources photos 3, 4. Used photo 4 position 20.5 ft? NO: photo 4 lists opening_id "back-d1-patio" at 20.5 ft as PATIO DOOR; photo 3 lists "back-d1" at 3.0 ft as ENTRY DOOR. Re-read extractions: photo 3 back-d1 is at x~0.15 (left side), photo 4 back-d1-patio is at x~0.60 (right side of back wall). These are DIFFERENT OPENINGS. Kept both; separated by wall span.
Gable window on back wall (gable triangle) at position 16.2 ft from photo 4 (cardinal back). Contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider (style_confidence 100). Measured 36"×24". Single source.
Main-wall window on back elevation at position 10.8 ft from photo 4. Contractor-tagged 2-Lite Slider (style_confidence 100). Measured 42"×30". Single source from cardinal back view.

Patio door on back wall at position 20.5 ft from photo 4 (cardinal back). Also visible in photo 5 (rear-right corner) at position 18.0 ft (opening_id "rear-right-d1"). Separation |20.5 - 18.0| = 2.5 ft > ±2 ft tolerance, but within corner-view foreshortening margin. Photo 4 (cardinal) vs photo 5 (corner) — photo 4 is authoritative. Collapsed to single entry; sources photos 4, 5. Used photo 4 dimensions and position 20.5 ft.
Vent on back wall at position 6.5 ft from photo 4. Measured 14"×10". Single source.
Vent on back wall at position 14.2 ft from photo 4. Measured 14"×10". Single source.
Vent on back wall at position 21.8 ft from photo 4. Measured 14"×10". Single source.
Right wall window at position 12.5 ft from photo 5 (rear-right corner). Measured 36"×48" Double Hung. Single source from corner view; no cardinal right-elevation confirms.

Entry door on back wall at position 18.0 ft from photo 5 (rear-right corner). Measured 36"×84". Likely same patio door as photo 4's back-d1-patio at 20.5 ft (separation 2.5 ft, corner foreshortening). Collapsed with back-d1-patio; sources photos 4, 5.

Front wall window at position 8.0 ft from photo 6 (front-right corner). Also in photo 1 (front-left corner) at position 8.0 ft. Separation 0 ft, size match 36"×48". Collapsed to single window; sources photos 1, 6. Already merged with photo 1's front-left-w1 → final entry is at 8.0-8.5 ft.
Right wall window at position 6.0 ft from photo 6 (front-right corner). Measured 36"×48" Double Hung. Single source from corner; no cardinal right-elevation.

Large garage door on front wall at position 11.25 ft from photo 6. Measured 180"×84" (15-ft wide overhead garage door). Single source. Different from photo 0's twin 96-in doors; this is a wider single or triple-door unit.
Front wall window at position 14.5 ft from photo 7 (front-right corner). Measured 35.8"×36.0" using WIN_REF=36" scale. Single source from corner view; foreshortened slightly but clear measurement.

Contractor-drawn reference bar (180") anchors all front-wall geometry on this photo.

measurements._ai_reference_used: Contractor WALL REF bars (324" front/back, 444" left) cross-checked by 3.75" lap-course counts on photos 0, 1, 3, 4, 5
measurements._ai_story_count_reasoning: All seven valid extractions independently reported 1.5 stories (main level plus half-story under a shed-dormered roof).

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows the full gable-front garage wall with driveway.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~33 lap courses at the contractor-calibrated 3.75" exposure from the visible siding start line (at driveway level) up to the eave/soffit line along the front-left corner: 33 × 3.75 in ≈ 124 in ≈ 10.3 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-front garage elevation, 324 in (27 ft) wide per wall ref. Contractor-tagged 2SL pin applied to the gable window (style_confidence=100), sized off the 48" WIN REF on its own plane. Siding start line sits roughly at driveway grade; small stacked-stone landscape wall at right is landscaping, not a wall material. Same red lap siding continues into the gable — no profile change at the gable break.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads FRONT-LEFT ELEVATION and the view shows the long left wall receding to the front corner where garage doors face front.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses along the front-left corner from the visible siding start line near the driveway up to the left-wall eave: ~32 courses x 3.75" exposure = 120" = 10.0 ft. Courses are fine at this resolution so count carries +/-2 course uncertainty.
_ai_photos.notes: Camera is at the front-left corner; the long red lap-sided left wall dominates, with two white garage doors on the front plane at right (heavily backlit — sizes for garage doors are approximate). Upper half-story wall with two paired windows sits above the lower roofline like a raised shed-dormer wall — same red lap siding, no shake observed on this elevation despite the SHAKE hint from photo #8. Woodpile occludes the start line mid-wall but the start line is visible at the front-left corner where the count was anchored. Brick structure at far right edge appears to be a separate/attached wing partially out of frame — not measurable here. WALL REF 48" and WIN REF 36" annotations sit on the upper wall plane; window sizes for upper windows used the WIN REF on that plane, lower-wall windows scaled by course counting (3.75" exposure) to stay plane-correct. along_wall_ft measured from the rear corner (left corner as viewed facing the left wall).

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and shows one long eave-side wall with a shed dormer, matching the Photo 3 annotation set (WALL REF = 444", WIN REF = 36").
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Courses not countable due to backlit/dark exposure; measured via pixel scale from the in-plane 444" WALL REF (~2.93 px/in): eave line (~y600) to estimated siding start (~y870) = ~270 px = ~92 in = ~7.7 ft. Start line estimated from grass/woodpile line since it is occluded.
_ai_photos.notes: Dormer window sizing used the in-plane WIN REF = 36" (vertical, on the dormer plane, ~2.64 px/in); main-wall DH windows and eave height used the 444" WALL REF scale (~2.93 px/in) to keep each measurement on its own plane. Lap exposure of 3.75" is consistent with the visible course rhythm but individual courses could not be reliably counted in this backlit exposure. Firewood pile occludes the siding start along most of the wall; height read anchored at the far-left corner where grade is visible. Brick visible at far right belongs to an adjoining rear/right wall plane, not this elevation. Mini-split condenser at lower left against the wall.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo banner reads REAR-LEFT ELEVATION and the view shows a corner shot with a gabled back wall (patio door to backyard) plus the long left wall receding right.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~30 lap courses from the visible siding start line (above the exposed foundation band, which was excluded) to the eave on the long left wall: 30 x 3.75 in = 112.5 in ≈ 9.4 ft; count run along the rear-left corner.
_ai_photos.notes: Banner labels this REAR-LEFT despite prompt indexing (photo 3 listed as LEFT); reported what is actually visible. Taller 2-story section with upper window at right where WALL REF=48 and WIN REF=36 are drawn; along_wall_ft left null for left-wall openings because the wall is foreshortened and corners aren't both visible. Exposed foundation band (~1.5-2 ft on downhill side) excluded from course count per rule 5. No SHAKE accent independently observed on these walls — gable is lap. Upper window rl-w4 partially obscured by annotation labels; size from 36" WIN REF on its own plane.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is banner-labeled BACK ELEVATION and matches the photo-5 description (WALL REF = 324", NO SIDING brick box, two 2SL pins).
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~30 lap courses at 3.75" exposure from the estimated siding start line (visible near patio door threshold, projected across) up to the eave at the rear-left corner: 30 x 3.75 = 112.5 in ≈ 9.4 ft. Pixel read via 324" WALL REF gives ~112 in, consistent, but course count authored the value.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-end back wall, 324" (27 ft) wide, same plane top to bottom so the gable 36" WIN REF is plane-valid. Siding continues into the gable with the same lap profile. Brick zone at lower-left is contractor-annotated NO SIDING (from_annotation ground truth) and excluded from siding. Siding start line partially hidden by brick zone and plantings at corners; start position projected from visible start near patio door and course rhythm. Nominal patio door likely 72x80.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION and shows the rear gable-end of the addition plus the main-house rear wall receding to the left, with WALL REF = 84" and WIN REF = 48" matching the rear-right annotation set.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses at 3.75" exposure along the right corner of the rear gable wall from the visible siding start line (just above the dark-red foundation parging band) up to the side eave: ~31 courses x 3.75" = 116" = 9.7 ft. Distant course lines are fine, so count carries +/-2 course uncertainty. Foundation parging excluded per rule.
_ai_photos.notes: Prompt index 5 corresponds to the REAR-RIGHT annotation set (0-based); WALL REF 84" runs down the corner between the main-house rear wall and the rear gable addition, WIN REF 48" spans the twin/slider window left of the patio door. A dark-red foundation parging band (~6-10 in tall) is visible below the siding start on the gable wall and was excluded from the course count and eave height. Main-house rear wall to the left is heavily tree-shaded; only one small window readable there. Patio door width foreshortened slightly; reported nominal 60x80 based on 80" door-height scaling on its own plane. No SHAKE accent visible on this elevation despite matching-pattern hint — all visible cladding is narrow-exposure lap.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Prompt sequence labels this index front-right; the driveway, parked truck and stamped walkway suggest a front-facing side, though the shot reads nearly straight-on to a single wall.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Courses at 3.75" exposure are too fine to count reliably at this resolution/occlusion; measured pixel span from estimated siding start (~y830, behind shrubs) to lower eave line (~y555) using the contractor 15' (180") reference bar at 2.83 px/in ≈ 97 in ≈ 8.0 ft. Bar lives on the upper pop-up wall plane, not the lower wall — cross-plane scale, flagged.
_ai_photos.notes: Image carries WALL REF = 180" and WIN REF = 36" bars (matching the photo-8 description in the annotation list) rather than the 48"/36" pair listed for photo 7 — I anchored to the bars actually visible in THIS frame (180" bar ≈ 509 px → 2.83 px/in; the 36" vertical WIN REF on the upper window corroborates at ~102 px). WIN REF sits on the upper clerestory/pop-up plane, so lower-wall window sizes were scaled cross-plane and carry extra uncertainty; fr-lower-w1 is partially hidden behind the evergreen so its width is a low-confidence estimate. The upper section is a raised clerestory wall band under a shed-style roof, not a dormer, so dormers=0. Siding start line hidden behind shrubs/plantings; a small foundation band may exist but is not visible. The contractor SHAKE ≈ 59 ft² annotation for this photo was not independently re-emitted (no shake region distinguishable through occlusion) per the no-double-count rule.

_ai_empty_photos.reason: Two consecutive empty Claude responses — photo may be interior, blurry, or not a house exterior.

reference_dim:
Pre-AI photo annotations:
Photo 1: FRONT ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 324"" is a known 324-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this. Blue line marked "WIN REF = 48"" is a known 48-inch span across a window edge — use this as a TIGHTER per-window scale for ALL window sizing on this photo (the wall ref handles whole-wall geometry, this one handles openings). Window measurements should be within ±5% of this anchor.. YELLOW PINS with brown style badges mark CONTRACTOR-TAGGED WINDOW STYLES — treat each tagged STYLE as GROUND TRUTH (overrides whatever your photo-inference would have guessed for that pin's style). measurements._ai_reference_used: Taped WALL REFs (324" front, 324" back, 444" left) for horizontal spans; 3.75" lap-course counting for all vertical/eave measurements (course counts kept over pixel reads per plane-correct rule)
measurements._ai_story_count_reasoning: Six of seven elevation extractions read 1.5 stories (shed/clerestory raised center section); photo 3's lone '2' reading was the same raised plane counted as a full story and was outvoted.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows a full-frontal gable wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses along the front-right corner from the visible siding start line (just above the driveway apron) to the side-eave return: ~32 courses × 3.75" exposure = 120" ≈ 10 ft. This is the eave-line height at the wall corners; the gable rises above it.
_ai_photos.notes: Front-gable wall, 324" (27 ft) wide per WALL REF. Contractor pin '2SL' on the gable window honored as 2-Lite Slider; WIN REF 48" used for its width. Garage door heights measure ~70-72" via course-derived vertical scale — likely standard 7 ft doors with slight camera-elevation foreshortening. No masonry on this wall; small side-roof eaves of adjacent wings visible at frame edges but not measured here.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-LEFT and the shot shows the long left wall plus the foreshortened front wall with two garage doors at the corner.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~28 lap courses at 3.75 in exposure from the visible siding start line (near the AC unit at the rear corner) to the left-wall eave: 28 x 3.75 = 105 in ≈ 8.75 ft. Foundation band below the start excluded.
_ai_photos.notes: House is a shed-roof/clerestory 1.5-story: the upper story wall (with WALL REF=48" and WIN REF=36" annotations) is a separate, set-back plane above the lower left-wall roof — those refs were used only for the upper-story windows, per cross-plane rule. Lower-wall openings sized via 3.75 in course counting. Firewood stack hides the mid-wall siding start but the start line is visible at the rear-left corner where the count was anchored. Red brick visible at far right may be an attached front section or neighboring structure — flagged as brick no-siding pending confirmation from front/right photos. Garage door positions are rough due to severe foreshortening of the front wall.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and shows a single long eave-side wall with the annotated 444" WALL REF matching the Photo 3 (left) annotation set.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Lap courses are visible but too dark and low-contrast to count reliably at 3.75" exposure; height taken via pixel scale from the same-plane 444" WALL REF (≈2.93 px/in), eave at ~y=610 to estimated siding start ~y=890 ≈ 96 in. Start line hidden behind woodpile/grass — estimated from course rhythm.
_ai_photos.notes: Long left elevation with a wide shed dormer holding two contractor-tagged 2-lite sliders (WIN REF 36" used only for the dormer-plane window sizing; main-wall openings sized via the same-plane 444" WALL REF). Third DH is partially occluded below by the woodpile so its height is extrapolated. Brick chimney/wall corner of an attached structure visible at far right belongs to another elevation. Siding start line never visible — woodpile, HVAC unit, and vegetation occlude the wall base.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels REAR-LEFT and the shot looks across the rear gable wall with the long left wall receding right, matching the 48" WALL REF / 36" WIN REF annotations described for the rear-left photo.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses (3.75" exposure) up the rear-left corner from the estimated siding start line to the upper eave on the left-wall plane: ~45 courses x 3.75" = 168.75" = 14.1 ft; bottom 3-4 courses at the corner are hidden by shrubs/AC unit so the start line was projected from the visible course rhythm and the exposed start line beside the patio door on the adjoining gable wall.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable (back) wall is partly shaded, left wall sunlit — color sample mixed. A dark red foundation/parging band (~12-18 in) is visible below the siding start near the patio door; it was EXCLUDED from the course count and eave height. Upper wall with the WIN REF window sits set back above the lower-story roof (second-story wall, not read as a dormer). WIN REF (36") is on that set-back upper plane and was NOT used to scale the main lower wall per cross-plane rule; WALL REF 48" bar lies on the same upper-left-wall plane as the counted corner but the course count was kept as the authored value. along_wall_ft values on the left wall assume the rear-left corner is the left edge of that wall as viewed from outside.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled BACK ELEVATION and shows a straight-on gable-end wall with sliding patio door matching the back-wall description.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses at 3.75" exposure from the siding start line near grade (right of the patio door) up to the side-eave line at the rear-left corner: ~29 courses × 3.75" ≈ 108.75" ≈ 9.1 ft. Brick wainscot band on lower-left excluded from count per rule 5.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-end back wall, siding continuous from body into gable (same plane, so WIN REF on the gable window is plane-valid here). WALL REF 324" (~910 px) gives ~2.81 px/in wall scale; WIN REF 36" vertical on gable window (~90 px) confirms window sizing. Contractor-tagged brick region at lower-left excluded from siding and from the course count. Siding start line partially hidden by shrubs/planters at corners — start estimated from visible course rhythm right of the patio door. Sliding door likely a standard 72x80 unit; measured 70x78 within tolerance.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo banner reads REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION and we see the rear gable-end wall with a patio door, viewed from the right rear yard; note this photo carries WALL REF = 84" / WIN REF = 48", matching the rear-right annotation set even though it was passed as index 5.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap course rhythm (3.75" exposure) on the rear gable wall from the visible siding start line (just above the exposed reddish foundation band) up to the right-side eave/soffit line: ~26 courses × 3.75" ≈ 98" ≈ 8.1 ft. Courses are small at this resolution so the count is a rhythm-based estimate, not a crisp one-by-one count.
_ai_photos.notes: WALL REF 84" vertical line runs along the downspout at the corner of the lower-left wall section — it scales that plane, not the gable wall, so eave height was taken by course counting on the gable wall itself. A ~7-9 inch exposed foundation band (reddish parging/brick) is visible below the siding start line across the rear wall; excluded from course count per rules. WIN REF 48" appears to reference the mid-wall window width (back-w2), and its pixel scale is consistent with the patio-door-height cross-check on the same plane. Patio door width reported at 66" after correcting mildly for foreshortening (raw pixel read ~53"). The gable-section back wall spans roughly 23-26 ft; house continues left behind the tree, so along_wall_ft values are measured from the gable section's left corner as viewed, not necessarily the full back wall's left corner — reconciler should treat them with that offset caveat.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels this FRONT-RIGHT; the gable end with garage doors (front) and the long right wall recede to the right.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Not measurable: individual 3.75" lap courses are not cleanly resolvable at this compressed resolution/steep angle for a reliable count, the siding start line is occluded (bin, mulch bags, shrubs), and the only vertical refs (WIN REF on gable plane, WALL REF on upper right wall plane) are not on a plane spanning grade-to-eave.
_ai_photos.notes: Prompt says index 6 (rear-right, WALL REF 84"/WIN REF 48"), but banner and on-photo annotations (FRONT-RIGHT, WALL REF = 48", WIN REF = 36") match the Photo 7 description — I used the on-photo refs (36" window ref on gable plane, 48" wall ref on upper right wall plane). The 48" red WALL REF spans the upper right wall from its eave down to the lower roof intersection, indicating ~4 ft of upper knee wall on that plane. Right-wall lower window reads (right-w2/right-w3) are heavily foreshortened; sizes are approximate and along_wall_ft left null to avoid false dedup. No SHAKE accent regions observed on these elevations; body and gable are lap throughout.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Prompt sequence labels this the front-right shot; the paver walkway and driveway with truck at frame-left match a front-corner approach, though the wall reads nearly straight-on.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~26 lap courses (3.75" exposure) on the lower wall from the estimated siding start line (hidden by grass/plantings, position inferred from course rhythm) to the lower eave: 26 x 3.75 = 97.5 in ≈ 8.1 ft. Contractor 180" bar sits on the set-back clerestory plane so it was NOT used for this wall height.
_ai_photos.notes: Upper clerestory/pop-up wall carries the contractor 180" WALL REF bar and 36" WIN REF (used for the upper window height). Lower window sizes were estimated from course counting (3.75" exposure) on their own wall plane, not the cross-plane bar. Siding start line hidden by lawn/plantings and hardscape; no foundation band included in the eave height. No shake accent visible on this face despite SHAKE annotation on photo #8 — this wall reads all lap. Upper window opening_id fr-upper-w1 likely also appears in photo 8 (same 36" WIN REF window).

raw_per_photo.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels this FRONT-RIGHT; the gable end with garage doors (front) and the long right wall recede to the right.
raw_per_photo.obstructions: Elevated camera angle causes strong foreshortening on both walls; shrubs hide the base of the right wall and a trash bin blocks the lower-left front corner.
raw_per_photo.eave_reasoning: Not measurable: individual 3.75" lap courses are not cleanly resolvable at this compressed resolution/steep angle for a reliable count, the siding start line is occluded (bin, mulch bags, shrubs), and the only vertical refs (WIN REF on gable plane, WALL REF on upper right wall plane) are not on a plane spanning grade-to-eave.
raw_per_photo.pitch_reasoning: Front gable rake visible at left; apparent rake angle ~34-38° suggests ~8/12, but perspective skew limits precision.
raw_per_photo.notes: Prompt says index 6 (rear-right, WALL REF 84"/WIN REF 48"), but banner and on-photo annotations (FRONT-RIGHT, WALL REF = 48", WIN REF = 36") match the Photo 7 description — I used the on-photo refs (36" window ref on gable plane, 48" wall ref on upper right wall plane). The 48" red WALL REF spans the upper right wall from its eave down to the lower roof intersection, indicating ~4 ft of upper knee wall on that plane. Right-wall lower window reads (right-w2/right-w3) are heavily foreshortened; sizes are approximate and along_wall_ft left null to avoid false dedup. No SHAKE accent regions observed on these elevations; body and gable are lap throughout.

raw_per_photo.elevation_reasoning: Prompt sequence labels this the front-right shot; the paver walkway and driveway with truck at frame-left match a front-corner approach, though the wall reads nearly straight-on.
raw_per_photo.obstructions: Large deciduous tree/shrub mass covers the right third of the wall, a tall spruce blocks the lower-center wall, backlit sky and low sun flatten contrast on the upper clerestory wall.
raw_per_photo.eave_reasoning: Counted ~26 lap courses (3.75" exposure) on the lower wall from the estimated siding start line (hidden by grass/plantings, position inferred from course rhythm) to the lower eave: 26 x 3.75 = 97.5 in ≈ 8.1 ft. Contractor 180" bar sits on the set-back clerestory plane so it was NOT used for this wall height.
raw_per_photo.pitch_reasoning: No gable triangle visible; the low shed roof rising to the clerestory is viewed nearly edge-on and foreshortened, so rise/run cannot be measured honestly.
raw_per_photo.notes: Upper clerestory/pop-up wall carries the contractor 180" WALL REF bar and 36" WIN REF (used for the upper window height). Lower window sizes were estimated from course counting (3.75" exposure) on their own wall plane, not the cross-plane bar. Siding start line hidden by lawn/plantings and hardscape; no foundation band included in the eave height. No shake accent visible on this face despite SHAKE annotation on photo #8 — this wall reads all lap. Upper window opening_id fr-upper-w1 likely also appears in photo 8 (same 36" WIN REF window).

candidate2_read_series.note: p3 rear-left oblique still inflated (14.1 ft eave @ 10/12) — oblique inflation is out-of-scope for C2 per pre-registration.

Photo 1: FRONT ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 324"" is a known 324-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this. Blue line marked "WIN REF = 48"" is a known 48-inch span across a window edge — use this as a TIGHTER per-window scale for ALL window sizing on this photo (the wall ref handles whole-wall geometry, this one handles openings). Window measurements should be within ±5% of this anchor.. YELLOW PINS with brown style badges mark CONTRACTOR-TAGGED WINDOW STYLES — treat each tagged STYLE as GROUND TRUTH (overrides whatever your photo-inference would have guessed for that pin's style). Tagged styles in this photo: 2-Lite Slider.
