measurements._ai_reference_used: Contractor WALL REF bars (324" front/back, 444" left, 180" clerestory) plus 3.75" lap-course counting; WIN REFs (48"/36") for opening sizes
measurements._ai_story_count_reasoning: 6 of 7 story-count readings (photos 0,1,2,4,5,6,7) said 1.5 with a set-back upper half-story/dormer band; photo 3's lone 2-story read was an angled rear-corner over-read.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads FRONT ELEVATION and the photo shows a straight-on gable wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses along the front-left corner from the visible siding start line near the driveway up to the eave return: ~32 courses × 3.75" exposure = 120" ≈ 10.0 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: Front-gabled wall, 324" (27 ft) wide per WALL REF. WIN REF 48" anchored the slider window sizing (measured 48x33). Contractor-tagged 2SL pin applied to the gable window. Roofline of adjacent structures visible at left and top-right; not part of this wall. No masonry or shake accents visible on this elevation. Course count ran up the front-left corner; bins occlude only the bottom 2-3 courses at that spot but the start line is visible immediately to their right at the same level.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-LEFT and the photo shows the long left wall receding to a front-facing garage wall at the corner.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses on the left wall from the estimated siding start line (position inferred from course rhythm; bottom hidden by woodpile/bins) to the lower eave: ~28 courses x 3.75 in = 105 in = 8.75 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: Upper half-story is a set-back raised wall over the lower roof, so the 48" WALL REF and 36" WIN REF lines sit on the UPPER wall plane; they were used only for the upper windows, while lower-wall openings and the eave height were sized via the 3.75" course rhythm on their own plane. Garage doors are foreshortened — widths/along-wall positions are rough. Foundation not visible; woodpile and bins hide the base along most of the left wall. Brick structure at far right appears to be a neighboring/attached building, not part of the measured siding walls.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and shows a single full eave wall square-on with the 444" wall reference.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Backlit shadow makes lap courses uncountable, so measured pixel span from visible siding start line at far-left corner (~y865) to eave line (~y600) = ~265 px at 2.93 px/in from the 444" wall ref on this same wall plane, ~90 in ≈ 7.5 ft; low-moderate confidence due to using horizontal ref for a vertical span.
_ai_photos.notes: Dormer window heights anchored to the 36" WIN REF on the dormer plane (2.5 px/in); main-wall windows and eave anchored to the 444" wall ref on the main wall plane. Woodpile hides the siding start line across the middle of the wall, but the start line is visible at the far-left corner where the eave read was taken. Brick visible at far right belongs to the rear/adjacent wall plane, not this left wall. Lap exposure consistent with the 3.75" contractor calibration but courses were not individually countable in the backlit shadow. Windows 2 and 3 have bottoms partially hidden by the woodpile; heights inferred from window 1's rhythm.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled REAR-LEFT ELEVATION and shows a gable-end (back) wall with patio door plus the long left eave wall receding to the right; note the on-photo refs (WALL REF=48", WIN REF=36") match the rear-left annotation set, not the LEFT-elevation 444" ref cited for index 3.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses on the left (long) wall near the rear-left corner from the visible siding start line to the eave: ~31 courses x 3.75 in = ~116 in = 9.7-9.8 ft; start line partially hidden behind shrubs/AC so position was extended from visible course rhythm. Foundation parging below siding start excluded per rule 5.
_ai_photos.notes: Prompt says index 3 = LEFT ELEVATION (WALL REF 444") but the delivered image is labeled REAR-LEFT with WALL REF=48" / WIN REF=36" (matches the Photo 4 annotation block); extraction done from the actual image. WIN REF 36" sits on the setback upper-story slider (rl-w4) and was used only for that window's plane per rule 6; main-wall heights came from course counting. A dark foundation/parging band (~1 ft) is visible below the siding start at the rear corner and was excluded from the eave height. No yellow style pins are visible in this photo. No SHAKE accent regions observed on these walls - lap siding throughout including the gable.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled BACK ELEVATION and shows the full gable-end rear wall with the annotated 324" wall reference.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses at 3.75" exposure from the estimated siding start line (occluded by the brick band and plantings at lower-left; rhythm projected from visible courses near the patio door) up to the eave corner: ~29 courses x 3.75in = ~109-111in (~9.2 ft). Course count is plane-correct on this wall.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-end rear wall, 324in (27 ft) wide per contractor wall ref. WIN REF 36in on the gable window used for gable-plane opening scale (same plane as that window only); wall-level openings scaled from the 324in wall ref (~2.8 px/in). Contractor yellow pins confirm both windows as 2-Lite Sliders. Brick band at lower-left is annotated NO SIDING and was excluded from the course count and eave height. No shake accent visible on this elevation despite the shake hint from photo #8.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels this REAR-RIGHT and the WALL REF = 84" / WIN REF = 48" annotations match the rear-right photo spec; a rear gable-end wall with patio door plus a receding lower section are visible.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses (3.75" exposure) from the siding start line (visible above the reddish foundation band) up to the side eave at the rear-right corner of the gable wall: ~32 courses x 3.75" = 120" = 10 ft. Reddish exposed foundation (~10-12") below siding start excluded per rule 5. The adjacent lower section's eave aligns with the 84" WALL REF (7 ft) on its own plane.
_ai_photos.notes: A reddish exposed foundation band (~10-12 in tall) runs below the siding start line along the base of the gable wall — excluded from course count and eave height per rules. The left section of the house (behind the downspout with the 84" WALL REF) sits at a lower eave (~7 ft above its siding start on its own plane). Gable and body siding are the same narrow lap; no shake accents independently observed on this elevation despite the SHAKE pattern hint. rr-w3 is heavily shaded/partially obscured by foliage — dimensions are rough and position could not be reliably estimated.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-RIGHT and the shot shows the garage-door front gable wall plus the long right wall receding to the right.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Not measurable — elevated vantage plus foreshortening makes course counting unreliable on the front wall (garage doors interrupt courses) and the siding start line on the right wall is hidden behind shrubs; leaving null rather than guessing.
_ai_photos.notes: In-photo annotations read WIN REF = 36" (blue, on the front gable window height) and WALL REF = 48" (red, vertical on the upper right wall near its window) — matching the Photo 7 FRONT-RIGHT description rather than the Photo 6 REAR-RIGHT one; index set to 6 per instruction. The WALL REF 48" line lives on the upper right wall plane and was not used cross-plane. The gable window height is anchored at 36" via WIN REF. Right-wall window sizes are foreshortened estimates and should be reconciled against the dedicated right/cardinal photos. Elevated camera prevented a trustworthy grade/siding-start view — eave height deliberately null.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Prompt labels index 7 as front-right, but the on-photo annotations (WALL REF = 180", WIN REF = 36" on the clerestory) match the photo-8 description, so elevation identity is uncertain; walkway and basketball hoop suggest a front-facing side.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Lower-wall eave scaled off the contractor 15' (180"/15.00 ft) reference bar, which sits on the CLERESTORY plane — cross-plane read (~265 px wall / 2.83 px-per-in ≈ 94 in ≈ 7.8-8.0 ft). Siding start line hidden by shrubs/plantings so start estimated from course rhythm. Individual 3.75" lap courses not resolvable at this image scale, so no course count possible.
_ai_photos.notes: Clerestory/pop-up wall section carries the 180" wall ref and the 36" win ref; clerestory siding band is ~40 in tall (win-ref plane scale). Lower-wall window sizes were scaled off the 180" bar which lives on the clerestory plane — cross-plane, so treat lower-wall dimensions as ±10-15%. Left lower window is >50% hidden behind the evergreen shrub; its width is an estimate from visible frame edges. Right third of the wall is fully obscured by a tree — additional openings could exist there. No SHAKE accent independently observed on this wall (the contractor-annotated SHAKE region is on photo #8 and is not re-emitted here).

measurements._ai_roof_type_reasoning: Gable triangles confirmed on front (photos 0,6) and back (photos 3,4,5) walls, and photo 2 reported a shed dormer on the left slope (with a matching clerestory band on the right in photo 7), upgrading gable → gable-shed-dormer.

_per_photo_readings.notes: cardinal left elevation, dormer face square-on, 444" wall ref on same photo — kept for width and face
saw the set-back upper wall with the two slider windows; no width reading (foreshortened) — confirms existence only

_ai_dormers._reconciliation_note: Only photo 2 gave a direct width (15 ft) so width_source is direct_single_reading; face=left from photo 2's cardinal view; offset from dormer-window centers (14.9/20.6 ft) vs 37 ft wall centerline ≈ -0.8 ft.

_per_photo_readings.notes: clerestory/pop-up section carries the 180" (15 ft) WALL REF spanning its face — treated as a direct single reading of the right-slope dormer width
rejected — elevated, foreshortened right wall; did not resolve the raised section

_ai_dormers._reconciliation_note: Photo 7's clerestory (15 ft ref bar, ~40" siding band, slider window on its plane) is the right-slope counterpart of photo 2's left dormer; different faces so NOT collapsed with the left dormer; only one photo measured it → direct_single_reading, offset unknown → 0.

_per_photo_readings.notes: cardinal left elevation, dormer face square-on, 444" wall ref on same photo — kept for width and face
saw the set-back upper wall with the two slider windows; no width reading (foreshortened) — confirms existence only

_ai_dormer._reconciliation_note: Only photo 2 gave a direct width (15 ft) so width_source is direct_single_reading; face=left from photo 2's cardinal view; offset from dormer-window centers (14.9/20.6 ft) vs 37 ft wall centerline ≈ -0.8 ft.

_count_corner_audit.residual_note: Correlated-error residual (logged per 1c pre-registration): same-corner agreement cannot catch two photos fabricating the SAME wrong count — the enumerated tier is corner-verified, not tape-verified.

_gable_demotion_audit.residual_note: Oblique demotion residual (amended per 79j.89 ruling): lexical demotion catches admitted inflation only where the admission matches the pinned corpus; novel phrasings escape until added; fully silent inflation always passes. Primary defense is independent geometry (square-on preference, corner agreement); lexical demotion is a second net, not a wall.

raw_per_photo.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-RIGHT and the shot shows the garage-door front gable wall plus the long right wall receding to the right.

The WALL REF 48" line lives on the upper right wall plane and was not used cross-plane. The gable window height is anchored at 36" via WIN REF. Lower-wall window sizes were scaled off the 180" bar which lives on the clerestory plane — cross-plane, so treat lower-wall dimensions as ±10-15%. Left lower window is >50% hidden behind the evergreen shrub; its width is an estimate from visible frame edges. measurements._ai_reference_used: Contractor WALL REF bars (324" front/back, 444" left, 180" pop-up) plus 3.75" lap course counts; WIN REF bars for opening sizes
measurements._ai_story_count_reasoning: 5 of 8 photos read 1.5 stories; the two '2-story' reads (photos 1,3) mistook the shed-dormer/clerestory pop-up for a full second story.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows a full-frontal gable wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~32 lap courses (3.75" exposure) from the visible siding start line between the garage doors up to the rake/eave intersection at the front-right corner: 32 × 3.75 = 120 in ≈ 10 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-front wall — no horizontal eave on this face; reported eave height is at the side-wall eave line where the rakes terminate at the corners. Narrow lap exposure consistent with 3.75" contractor calibration. Small side roof returns from left/right walls visible at eave level. Concrete apron/driveway at grade; siding runs nearly to grade between garage doors. Yellow pin 2SL tag applied to gable window. No SHAKE accents detected on this elevation.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner says FRONT-LEFT; photo shows the long left wall receding left and the front wall with garage doors facing camera-right.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses at 3.75" exposure on the left wall near the front-left corner from the estimated siding start line to the main (lower) eave: ~28 courses x 3.75" = 105" = 8.75 ft; start line partly hidden behind woodpile/bins so it was projected from the visible course rhythm.
_ai_photos.notes: Upper level appears to be a raised second-story section (not a dormer) with a low-slope shed roof; its eave sits well above the main 8.75 ft left-wall eave. Left-wall first-floor windows are partially obscured by the woodpile and ladder — heights below sill level are estimated. The brick region at far right may belong to an attached brick section (consistent with brick noted on the back elevation) but a separate neighboring structure is also visible behind it — flagged with low certainty. Foreshortened along_wall_ft values on the left wall used the 444" (37 ft) left-wall length from the annotation set. The front-wall upper window's along_wall_ft is null due to severe foreshortening. No SHAKE accent regions independently observed in this photo.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and shows a single long eave-side wall with a shed dormer, matching Photo 3's annotation set (444" wall ref, 3 DH + 2 2SL pins).
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Courses not countable — backlit shadow makes individual laps indistinguishable at this resolution; measured via same-plane pixel scale from the 444" WALL REF (1300 px -> 0.342 in/px): siding start visible at the left corner (~y865) to eave line (~y588) = ~277 px ≈ 95 in ≈ 8.0 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: Main-wall DH windows sized with the same-plane 444" wall ref (WIN REF 36" bar sits on the dormer plane and per rule 6 only scales the two dormer sliders). Dormer 2SLs sized with the 36" win ref on their own plane. Course counting was impossible due to heavy backlighting; eave height is a pixel-scale read, so it does NOT qualify for the tape-provable tier. Brick visible at far right edge belongs to the adjacent front/rear plane, not this wall. Firewood pile hides the siding start across the center of the wall, but the start line is clearly visible at the left corner, which anchored the height read.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels this REAR-LEFT ELEVATION; a rear gable wall with patio door and the long left wall receding toward the street/front are both visible at a shared corner.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses at 3.75 in exposure on the back (gable) wall along the rear-left corner from the estimated siding start line (hidden behind shrubs, inferred from course rhythm) up to the eave line: ~32 courses x 3.75 in = 120 in = 10.0 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: This image carries WALL REF = 48" (vertical, on the upper-story wall band of the left wall) and WIN REF = 36" (on the upper left-wall window), matching the Photo-4 rear-left annotation rather than the Photo-3 left-elevation annotation; no yellow style pins are visible in this frame. Course count on the back gable wall used the patio-door plane; start line inferred from course rhythm behind shrubs/planters. A short dark foundation band is visible below the siding start near the patio door and was excluded from the count. The upper window (left-w2) sits on the elevated second-story wall plane — its WIN REF was used only for that window, not for lower-wall scale. Far-right lower window is heavily foreshortened and partially blocked by the firewood stack, so its along-wall position was left null.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads BACK ELEVATION and the photo shows a full gable-end wall with patio door facing the rear yard/fire pit.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses (3.75" exposure) from the estimated siding start line to the side eave along the photo-right (rear-left) corner: ~29 courses x 3.75" = ~108.75" = ~9.0 ft; start line partially hidden by plantings so position inferred from course rhythm.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-end rear wall, 324" (27 ft) wide per wall ref. WIN REF 36" (90 px) on the gable window is same-plane for this gable-end wall and corroborates opening sizes. Both contractor 2SL pins captured (gable window + lower-left window). Brick no-siding region on lower-left already contractor-annotated; excluded from siding coverage and not re-emitted as an accent. Course count anchored on photo-right corner; bottom courses inferred through hosta plantings (start_line_occluded).

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION; a gable-end wall with patio door faces the camera with the rear wing stepping back to the left.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Courses are visible but too fine to count reliably at this resolution (3.75" exposure); scaled on-plane using the patio door (~80" tall, ~160 px -> 2.0 px/in) and WIN REF 48" window on the same gable plane; eave line to siding start ~205 px ≈ 104" ≈ 8.7 ft. Same-plane references only.
_ai_photos.notes: MISMATCH: prompt says index 5 = BACK ELEVATION with WALL REF 324", but this photo's banner reads REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION with WALL REF = 84" and WIN REF = 48" (matching the photo-6 annotation set) — I anchored to the references VISIBLE in this photo (84" wall ref on rear wing plane, 48" win ref on the gable-plane window). A reddish stone/parged foundation band (~1-1.5 ft) is visible below the siding start near the corner and left of the patio door — EXCLUDED from eave height per rules. Siding start partially hidden by shrubs/furniture, so start line estimated from course rhythm (start_line_occluded=true). Gable window rr-w2 sits in the gable triangle above the eave. No SHAKE accent visible on this elevation despite matching-pattern hint — all visible cladding is narrow lap.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-RIGHT ELEVATION and the photo shows the front gable/garage wall meeting the right side wall at a corner.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~30 lap courses (3.75" exposure) up the front-right corner trim from the estimated siding start line to the right-wall eave; 30 x 3.75 = 112.5 in ≈ 9.4 ft. Start line is hidden behind shrubs so its position was inferred from course rhythm.
_ai_photos.notes: Photo shows the WIN REF = 36" blue bar on the front gable window (used for opening scale on the front plane) and the WALL REF = 48" red bar on the set-back upper-story wall — that ref is on a different, higher wall plane and was NOT used for the lower-wall eave (course count used instead). Upper-story section behind the garage roof indicates a 1.5-story massing; its window sizing is approximate due to steep foreshortening (along_wall_ft left null). Eave read is siding-start-to-eave on the right wall; foundation not visible under shrubs. No shake accents visible on these elevations despite the SHAKE annotation on photo #8 — front and right planes read as uniform ~4" lap.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Straight-on view of a wall with front walkway and entry landscaping matching the photo-sequence label; primarily one wall plane visible with a clerestory pop-up above the lower roof.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~23 lap courses at the visible left portion of the lower wall from the siding start line (visible near the left corner by the truck) up to the eave/frieze under the main roof; 23 x 3.75 in = 86.25 in = 7.2 ft. Center of the start line is bush-covered but the rhythm is continuous from the visible left run.
_ai_photos.notes: Contractor 180-in (15 ft) bar spans the clerestory pop-up wall (x 0.328-0.646) and the vertical WIN REF = 36 in locks the upper window height; upper window scaled off those refs (dormer/pop-up plane). Lower-wall window sizes and along-wall positions were scaled off the same bar as a last resort and are approximate (bar is on the pop-up plane, not the lower wall plane) — course counting was used for the eave height instead, which is plane-correct. Pop-up wall band is ~3.5 ft tall (its own mini-eave to lower roof). Right third of the wall is fully hidden by trees — any openings there are unreadable from this photo. No shake seen on this wall despite the photo-8 SHAKE annotation hint; the annotated shake region is not re-emitted here per double-count rule.

_ai_pin_gap_hints.message: Dormer on the left slope reports 15.0 ft wide but NO reference marker was in frame. Unanchored dormer widths drift 25-90% — re-shoot the left elevation with a WALL_REF or WIN_REF bar in frame before quoting.

saw the raised upper section and its two slider windows but foreshortened >45° — rejected for width

openings._reconciliation_note: Contractor-pinned gable window: photo 0 at 13.4 ft (48×35, WIN REF 48" anchored), photo 7 at 12 ft (40×30, self-flagged approximate wrong-plane scale), photo 6 at 9 ft (26×36, oblique/elevated) — direct frontal frontal saw only ONE front window, so all three collapse; pin values kept.

Photo 0 at 7.1 ft (100×71, 324" ref), photos 1/6 at 4 ft (108×84 nominal, oblique). Positions/sizes exceed strict tolerance but four garage doors cannot fit a 27 ft wall (417" > 324") — geometry forces merge; direct-frontal measurement kept.

_reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Confidence-weighted average of final per-wall heights (front 10.0 @75, left 8.75 @78, back 9.5 @80, right 9.4 @65) = 9.4 ft; photo 7's 7.2 ft front read and photo 2's/5's pixel reads were subordinated to course counts per rules 1b/1d.
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: Gable triangles seen face-on on front (photos 0,6) and back (photos 3,4) plus a right-wing gable (photo 5); dormers_observed_count > 0 in photos 2 and 7 upgrades gable → gable-shed-dormer.
_reconciliation_notes.dormers: Detected 2 dormers/pop-ups: a left-slope shed dormer (photo 2 direct, 15.0 ft, single reading → amber) and a front clerestory pop-up (photo 7 direct, taped 180" bar = 15.0 ft, single reading → amber); different faces so never collapsed; photos 1, 3, 6 contributed face/count evidence only (foreshortened, rejected for width).
_reconciliation_notes.story_count: Mode of per-photo reads is 1.5 (photos 0,2,5,6,7); photos 1 and 3 read 2 stories by mistaking the shed-dormer/pop-up massing for a full floor; photo 4's 1-story read saw only the rear gable.
_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: House is ~4" lap throughout; deducted contractor-marked brick on back (~60 sqft, photo 4/annotation) and stone band on right (~25 sqft, photo 5); photo 1's ~100 sqft brick was flagged possibly a neighboring structure and treated as the same rear brick zone → ~92% overall.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: Sunlit samples preferred (photos 3, 4, 5): siding = median of three sunlit reds (#A8453E), trim from photo 4, roof = median sunlit gray (#8B8E90), door from photo 5; backlit photo 2 sample discarded.
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: 40 raw openings across 8 photos → 23 after dedup: front garage doors/entry/gable window collapsed 12→7 using the 324" wall-width constraint and direct-frontal photo 0; left wall collapsed 13→6 anchored on photo 2's 5 contractor pins (3 DH + 2 dormer 2SL); back collapsed 7→4 anchored on photo 4's 2 pins; right kept all 6 (no ±2 ft position overlaps); 4 null-along openings kept un-deduped per rule 3c. NOTE: source extractions carried no bboxes, so all bbox fields are zeroed placeholders — no photo captured normalized boxes.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled FRONT ELEVATION and shows a full-frontal gable wall with two garage doors and an entry door.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~32 lap courses (3.75" exposure) from the visible siding start line between the garage doors up to the rake/eave intersection at the front-right corner: 32 × 3.75 = 120 in ≈ 10 ft.
photos.notes: Gable-front wall — no horizontal eave on this face; reported eave height is at the side-wall eave line where the rakes terminate at the corners. Narrow lap exposure consistent with 3.75" contractor calibration. Small side roof returns from left/right walls visible at eave level. Concrete apron/driveway at grade; siding runs nearly to grade between garage doors. Yellow pin 2SL tag applied to gable window. No SHAKE accents detected on this elevation.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner says FRONT-LEFT; photo shows the long left wall receding left and the front wall with garage doors facing camera-right.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses at 3.75" exposure on the left wall near the front-left corner from the estimated siding start line to the main (lower) eave: ~28 courses x 3.75" = 105" = 8.75 ft; start line partly hidden behind woodpile/bins so it was projected from the visible course rhythm.
photos.notes: Upper level appears to be a raised second-story section (not a dormer) with a low-slope shed roof; its eave sits well above the main 8.75 ft left-wall eave. Left-wall first-floor windows are partially obscured by the woodpile and ladder — heights below sill level are estimated. The brick region at far right may belong to an attached brick section (consistent with brick noted on the back elevation) but a separate neighboring structure is also visible behind it — flagged with low certainty. Foreshortened along_wall_ft values on the left wall used the 444" (37 ft) left-wall length from the annotation set. The front-wall upper window's along_wall_ft is null due to severe foreshortening. No SHAKE accent regions independently observed in this photo.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and shows a single long eave-side wall with a shed dormer, matching Photo 3's annotation set (444" wall ref, 3 DH + 2 2SL pins).
photos.eave_reasoning: Courses not countable — backlit shadow makes individual laps indistinguishable at this resolution; measured via same-plane pixel scale from the 444" WALL REF (1300 px -> 0.342 in/px): siding start visible at the left corner (~y865) to eave line (~y588) = ~277 px ≈ 95 in ≈ 8.0 ft.
photos.notes: Main-wall DH windows sized with the same-plane 444" wall ref (WIN REF 36" bar sits on the dormer plane and per rule 6 only scales the two dormer sliders). Dormer 2SLs sized with the 36" win ref on their own plane. Course counting was impossible due to heavy backlighting; eave height is a pixel-scale read, so it does NOT qualify for the tape-provable tier. Brick visible at far right edge belongs to the adjacent front/rear plane, not this wall. Firewood pile hides the siding start across the center of the wall, but the start line is clearly visible at the left corner, which anchored the height read.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels this REAR-LEFT ELEVATION; a rear gable wall with patio door and the long left wall receding toward the street/front are both visible at a shared corner.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses at 3.75 in exposure on the back (gable) wall along the rear-left corner from the estimated siding start line (hidden behind shrubs, inferred from course rhythm) up to the eave line: ~32 courses x 3.75 in = 120 in = 10.0 ft.
photos.notes: This image carries WALL REF = 48" (vertical, on the upper-story wall band of the left wall) and WIN REF = 36" (on the upper left-wall window), matching the Photo-4 rear-left annotation rather than the Photo-3 left-elevation annotation; no yellow style pins are visible in this frame. Course count on the back gable wall used the patio-door plane; start line inferred from course rhythm behind shrubs/planters. A short dark foundation band is visible below the siding start near the patio door and was excluded from the count. The upper window (left-w2) sits on the elevated second-story wall plane — its WIN REF was used only for that window, not for lower-wall scale. Far-right lower window is heavily foreshortened and partially blocked by the firewood stack, so its along-wall position was left null.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads BACK ELEVATION and the photo shows a full gable-end wall with patio door facing the rear yard/fire pit.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses (3.75" exposure) from the estimated siding start line to the side eave along the photo-right (rear-left) corner: ~29 courses x 3.75" = ~108.75" = ~9.0 ft; start line partially hidden by plantings so position inferred from course rhythm.
photos.notes: Gable-end rear wall, 324" (27 ft) wide per wall ref. WIN REF 36" (90 px) on the gable window is same-plane for this gable-end wall and corroborates opening sizes. Both contractor 2SL pins captured (gable window + lower-left window). Brick no-siding region on lower-left already contractor-annotated; excluded from siding coverage and not re-emitted as an accent. Course count anchored on photo-right corner; bottom courses inferred through hosta plantings (start_line_occluded).

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION; a gable-end wall with patio door faces the camera with the rear wing stepping back to the left.
photos.eave_reasoning: Courses are visible but too fine to count reliably at this resolution (3.75" exposure); scaled on-plane using the patio door (~80" tall, ~160 px -> 2.0 px/in) and WIN REF 48" window on the same gable plane; eave line to siding start ~205 px ≈ 104" ≈ 8.7 ft. Same-plane references only.
photos.notes: MISMATCH: prompt says index 5 = BACK ELEVATION with WALL REF 324", but this photo's banner reads REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION with WALL REF = 84" and WIN REF = 48" (matching the photo-6 annotation set) — I anchored to the references VISIBLE in this photo (84" wall ref on rear wing plane, 48" win ref on the gable-plane window). A reddish stone/parged foundation band (~1-1.5 ft) is visible below the siding start near the corner and left of the patio door — EXCLUDED from eave height per rules. Siding start partially hidden by shrubs/furniture, so start line estimated from course rhythm (start_line_occluded=true). Gable window rr-w2 sits in the gable triangle above the eave. No SHAKE accent visible on this elevation despite matching-pattern hint — all visible cladding is narrow lap.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-RIGHT ELEVATION and the photo shows the front gable/garage wall meeting the right side wall at a corner.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~30 lap courses (3.75" exposure) up the front-right corner trim from the estimated siding start line to the right-wall eave; 30 x 3.75 = 112.5 in ≈ 9.4 ft. Start line is hidden behind shrubs so its position was inferred from course rhythm.
photos.notes: Photo shows the WIN REF = 36" blue bar on the front gable window (used for opening scale on the front plane) and the WALL REF = 48" red bar on the set-back upper-story wall — that ref is on a different, higher wall plane and was NOT used for the lower-wall eave (course count used instead). Upper-story section behind the garage roof indicates a 1.5-story massing; its window sizing is approximate due to steep foreshortening (along_wall_ft left null). Eave read is siding-start-to-eave on the right wall; foundation not visible under shrubs. No shake accents visible on these elevations despite the SHAKE annotation on photo #8 — front and right planes read as uniform ~4" lap.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Straight-on view of a wall with front walkway and entry landscaping matching the photo-sequence label; primarily one wall plane visible with a clerestory pop-up above the lower roof.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~23 lap courses at the visible left portion of the lower wall from the siding start line (visible near the left corner by the truck) up to the eave/frieze under the main roof; 23 x 3.75 in = 86.25 in = 7.2 ft. Center of the start line is bush-covered but the rhythm is continuous from the visible left run.
photos.notes: Contractor 180-in (15 ft) bar spans the clerestory pop-up wall (x 0.328-0.646) and the vertical WIN REF = 36 in locks the upper window height; upper window scaled off those refs (dormer/pop-up plane). Lower-wall window sizes and along-wall positions were scaled off the same bar as a last resort and are approximate (bar is on the pop-up plane, not the lower wall plane) — course counting was used for the eave height instead, which is plane-correct. Pop-up wall band is ~3.5 ft tall (its own mini-eave to lower roof). Right third of the wall is fully hidden by trees — any openings there are unreadable from this photo. No shake seen on this wall despite the photo-8 SHAKE annotation hint; the annotated shake region is not re-emitted here per double-count rule.

_pin_gap_hints.message: Dormer on the left slope reports 15.0 ft wide but NO reference marker was in frame. Unanchored dormer widths drift 25-90% — re-shoot the left elevation with a WALL_REF or WIN_REF bar in frame before quoting.

_count_corner_audit.residual_note: Correlated-error residual (logged per 1c pre-registration): same-corner agreement cannot catch two photos fabricating the SAME wrong count — the enumerated tier is corner-verified, not tape-verified.

_gable_demotion_audit.residual_note: Oblique demotion residual (amended per 79j.89 ruling): lexical demotion catches admitted inflation only where the admission matches the pinned corpus; novel phrasings escape until added; fully silent inflation always passes. Primary defense is independent geometry (square-on preference, corner agreement); lexical demotion is a second net, not a wall.

reference_dim:
Pre-AI photo annotations:
Photo 1: FRONT ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 648"" is a known 648-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this. Blue line marked "WIN REF = 72"" is a known 72-inch span across a window edge — use this as a TIGHTER per-window scale for ALL window sizing on this photo (the wall ref handles whole-wall geometry, this one handles openings). Window measurements should be within ±5% of this anchor..
Photo 2: FRONT-LEFT ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 336"" is a known 336-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this.
Photo 3: LEFT ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 360"" is a known 360-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this. Blue line marked "WIN REF = 28"" is a known 28-inch span across a window edge — use this as a TIGHTER per-window scale for ALL window sizing on this photo (the wall ref handles whole-wall geometry, this one handles openings). Window measurements should be within ±5% of this anchor..
Photo 4: REAR-LEFT ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 360"" is a known 360-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this. Blue line marked "WIN REF = 36"" is a known 36-inch span across a window edge — use this as a TIGHTER per-window scale for ALL window sizing on this photo (the wall ref handles whole-wall geometry, this one handles openings). Window measurements should be within ±5% of this anchor..
Photo 5: BACK ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 648"" is a known 648-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this. Blue line marked "WIN REF = 36"" is a known 36-inch span across a window edge — use this as a TIGHTER per-window scale for ALL window sizing on this photo (the wall ref handles whole-wall geometry, this one handles openings). Window measurements should be within ±5% of this anchor..
Photo 6: REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 360"" is a known 360-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this. Blue line marked "WIN REF = 36"" is a known 36-inch span across a window edge — use this as a TIGHTER per-window scale for ALL window sizing on this photo (the wall ref handles whole-wall geometry, this one handles openings). _reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Confidence-weighted average of final per-wall heights (front 9.9@80, back 9.1@70, left 11.0@60, right 9.2@82) = 9.7 ft; note the left wall's disputed 11.0 pulls this up.
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: Side-gable: gable triangles reported on the left wall (photos 1,2,3) and right wall (photos 5,6,7); photo 4's head-on 'hip' impression outvoted; dormers_observed_count = 0 in all 8 photos so no dormer upgrade. Consensus pitch 7/12 (photos 2,3,6).

_reconciliation_notes.story_count: Unanimous: all 8 photos report a single story.
_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: Lap 4" siding on all walls, gables, and the siding-clad chimney chase; only deduction is photo 5's ~45 sqft brick region → 98%.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: Photo 3 used — the only 'mixed' sample_quality (all others 'shaded', no 'sunlit' available), per the sunlit>mixed>shaded preference.
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: 32 raw openings across 8 photos → 14 after dedup. Key merges: 3 front twins + door seen in photos 0/1/7 (photo 0 cardinal governs; photo 1/7 positions foreshortening-compressed, matched by order under photo 0's full-wall census); back wall 5 windows + door seen in photos 3/4/5 (photo 4 cardinal governs); single left-gable window seen in photos 1/2/3 (17/21/20 ft) collapsed to one. Front twins preserved as three separate 72" units at 5.8/25.9/47.5 ft. Phase A extractions carried no bboxes, so bbox fields are null — no photo captured pixel geometry for openings. NOTE: prompt photo-order annotations were offset by one from the in-frame banners (photo 2 is LEFT/360", photo 3 is REAR-LEFT, photo 4 is BACK/648", photo 6 is RIGHT); reconciliation trusted the in-frame refs each extractor actually anchored to.

_count_corner_audit.residual_note: Correlated-error residual (logged per 1c pre-registration): same-corner agreement cannot catch two photos fabricating the SAME wrong count — the enumerated tier is corner-verified, not tape-verified.

_gable_demotion_audit.residual_note: Oblique demotion residual (amended per 79j.89 ruling): lexical demotion catches admitted inflation only where the admission matches the pinned corpus; novel phrasings escape until added; fully silent inflation always passes. Primary defense is independent geometry (square-on preference, corner agreement); lexical demotion is a second net, not a wall.

reference_dim:
Pre-AI photo annotations:
Photo 1: FRONT ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 600"" is a known 600-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this. Blue line marked "WIN REF = 72"" is a known 72-inch span across a window edge — use this as a TIGHTER per-window scale for ALL window sizing on this photo (the wall ref handles whole-wall geometry, this one handles openings). Window measurements should be within ±5% of this anchor.. YELLOW PINS with brown style badges mark CONTRACTOR-TAGGED WINDOW STYLES — treat each tagged STYLE as GROUND TRUTH (overrides whatever your photo-inference would have guessed for that pin's style). Tagged styles in this photo: Twin Single Hung; Twin Single Hung; Twin Single Hung. Each yellow pin = ONE window — include it in your openings list with the contractor's exact style and style_confidence=100, BUT YOU still measure width_in and height_in from the photo using your normal scale reference. Don't size-tag a yellow pin as 0×0 — measure it like any other window..
Photo 2: FRONT-LEFT ELEVATION.
Photo 3: LEFT ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 360"" is a known 360-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this. Blue line marked "WIN REF = 28"" is a known 28-inch span across a window edge — use this as a TIGHTER per-window scale for ALL window sizing on this photo (the wall ref handles whole-wall geometry, this one handles openings). Window measurements should be within ±5% of this anchor..
Photo 4: REAR-LEFT ELEVATION.
Photo 5: BACK ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 600"" is a known 600-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this. Blue line marked "WIN REF = 36"" is a known 36-inch span across a window edge — use this as a TIGHTER per-window scale for ALL window sizing on this photo (the wall ref handles whole-wall geometry, this one handles openings). Window measurements should be within ±5% of this anchor.. YELLOW PINS with brown style badges mark CONTRACTOR-TAGGED WINDOW STYLES — treat each tagged STYLE as GROUND TRUTH (overrides whatever your photo-inference would have guessed for that pin's style). Tagged styles in this photo: Single Hung; Single Hung; Single Hung; Single Hung; Single Hung. Each yellow pin = ONE window — include it in your openings list with the contractor's exact style and style_confidence=100, BUT YOU still measure width_in and height_in from the photo using your normal scale reference. Don't size-tag a yellow pin as 0×0 — measure it like any other window..
Photo 6: REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION.
Photo 7: RIGHT ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 360"" is a known 360-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this.
Photo 8: FRONT-RIGHT ELEVATION.

measurements._ai_reference_used: WALL REF 600" on front (photo 0) and back (photo 4) elevations; WALL REF 360" on left (photo 2) and right (photo 6) elevations; WIN REFs 72"/28"/36" for opening sizes; 4.25" lap course counts for all vertical scale

No bboxes were provided in any Phase A extraction, so bbox fields are null.

_count_corner_audit.residual_note: Correlated-error residual (logged per 1c pre-registration): same-corner agreement cannot catch two photos fabricating the SAME wrong count — the enumerated tier is corner-verified, not tape-verified.

_gable_demotion_audit.residual_note: Oblique demotion residual (amended per 79j.89 ruling): lexical demotion catches admitted inflation only where the admission matches the pinned corpus; novel phrasings escape until added; fully silent inflation always passes. Primary defense is independent geometry (square-on preference, corner agreement); lexical demotion is a second net, not a wall.

_reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Confidence-weighted average of final per-wall eaves (front 8.6@90, back 8.6@85, left 9.0@75, right 8.3@75) = 8.62 → 8.6 ft; all values are course-count derived; both cardinal gable-end reads (photos 2 and 6, 10.5 ft) were rejected as 28-course outliers against five agreeing 22-24-course corner counts.
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: Gable triangles reported on the left wall (photos 1,2,3) and right wall (photos 5,6,7) with front (photo 0) and back (photo 4) as eave-only walls and ridge parallel to the 50-ft span → side-gable ranch; photo 4's stray 'hip' comment and photo 5's 'back gable' label (an orientation mix-up — its gable is the right wall) were both overruled; dormers_observed_count = 0 in all 8 photos so no dormer upgrade.
_reconciliation_notes.dormers: Detected 0 dormers — every photo, including both straight-on gable-end views, reported dormers_observed_count = 0; dormers[] is empty by observation, not omission.

_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: Every photo reports lap 4"-4.5" siding across wall bodies, gable triangles, and the chimney chase with no no_siding_regions → 100%; exposed foundation/waterproofing membrane bands were below the siding start line and excluded per the extraction rules.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: No sunlit samples exist; photo 0 (mixed quality, cardinal front) supplied siding/trim/roof; door hex is the median of the three shaded door samples (photos 1,3,4) since neither mixed-quality photo sampled a door.
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: 29 raw openings across 8 photos → 12 after dedup. Key merges: front door seen 3× (34/34/36 ft), front twins each confirmed by oblique partial-sash reads (photos 1,7 deferred to photo 0's 72" WIN REF); single left-wall window seen 3× (18/21/23 ft); back wall's photo 3 reads matched photo 4 by door anchor + preserved ordering under its stated ±3 ft foreshortening; photo 5's 4 'right-wall' windows were reassigned to the back wall (photo 6's cardinal right elevation shows ZERO openings on the right wall, proving photo 5's label swap) — its w1/w2 mirror-matched back-w5/back-w4 (50−4=46≈47.1, 50−11=39≈40.4), and its w3/w4 (confidence 35, heavily foreshortened, mirror positions landing on the already-counted door/w3 region) were rejected as unplaceable double-counts of existing back openings rather than kept as phantom right-wall units.

_gable_demotion_audit.residual_note: Oblique demotion residual (amended per 79j.89 ruling): lexical demotion catches admitted inflation only where the admission matches the pinned corpus; novel phrasings escape until added; fully silent inflation always passes. Primary defense is independent geometry (square-on preference, corner agreement); lexical demotion is a second net, not a wall.

reference_dim:
Pre-AI photo annotations:
Photo 1: FRONT ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 273"" is a known 273-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this. Blue line marked "WIN REF = 80"" is a known 80-inch span across a window edge — use this as a TIGHTER per-window scale for ALL window sizing on this photo (the wall ref handles whole-wall geometry, this one handles openings). Window measurements should be within ±5% of this anchor.. Red hatched areas marked NO SIDING are NOT siding (Brick) — exclude from siding_pct_this_wall.
Photo 2: FRONT-LEFT ELEVATION. Red line marked "WALL REF = 209"" is a known 209-inch span — anchor the WALL/elevation scale to this. Blue line marked "WIN REF = 110"" is a known 110-inch span across a window edge — use this as a TIGHTER per-window scale for ALL window sizing on this photo (the wall ref handles whole-wall geometry, this one handles openings). measurements._ai_reference_used: Per-photo taped WALL REF spans (photo 0: 273", photo 2: 351", photo 4: 360", photo 6: 351") for wall geometry; WIN REF bars (80"/110"/48"/31"/73"/60"/30") for opening sizes. House is pre-siding (Tyvek) so no course counts exist anywhere.
measurements._ai_story_count_reasoning: 6 of 8 photos read 2 stories (split-level with brick/CMU walkout lower level and cantilevered upper story); photo 1's single-story read reflects only the wing it framed.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is annotated FRONT ELEVATION and shows the entry door, driveway and garage door face-on.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: No siding installed (Tyvek only) so no course counting possible; measured siding-start (top of brick lower level, ~y745) to main eave (~y458) = ~287 px at 2.53 px/in from the same-plane WALL REF 273" = ~113 in ≈ 9.4 ft. This is the upper Tyvek band only; brick lower level excluded per rule 5.
_ai_photos.notes: New construction, no siding installed — house wrapped in Tyvek, so all profile/color siding fields are empty/null and no course count is possible. Vertical eave read used the same-plane WALL REF 273" (690 px, ~2.53 px/in). WIN REF 80" blue bar sits at the right window group; window dims cross-checked against it (~2.7 px/in) and agree with the wall scale within ~8%. Lower walkout level is brick (marked NO SIDING) with garage; upper Tyvek band is the sidable area, plus a raised clerestory band (~y360-460) above the main eave that will also take siding (~44 ft x ~2.5 ft plus box faces). Porta-potty occludes part of the garage door; overhanging branches partially obscure front-w1.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads FRONT-LEFT ELEVATION and the photo shows two walls meeting at a corner, with the entry/garage face (front) and a long wrapped wall (left) both visible.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: No siding installed (Tyvek wrap only) so no course counting possible; scaled on the front wall plane using WALL REF = 209" (~320 px => ~1.53 px/in), measuring from the siding start line (top of exposed concrete foundation, where WALL REF is drawn) up to the front eave fascia (~190 px => ~124 in ~ 10.3 ft). Shed-style roof means eave height varies along the wall; this is the lower front eave.
_ai_photos.notes: New construction — walls are Tyvek/OSB only, no siding installed, so all profile/siding-color fields are empty and no course counting is possible. Exposed poured-concrete foundation band (~3-4 ft, taller downhill) is visible below the siding start line and was excluded from eave height per rule 5. Roof is a low-slope shed/modern design with a raised clerestory monitor section; no true gables or dormers. fl-w3 sizing anchored to WIN REF = 110" (its width matches the blue reference span); its height read of ~96-129 in is uncertain due to vertical perspective — flagged for reconciliation with the straight-on front photo. fl-g1 is an uninstalled garage/lower-level opening partially blocked by a porta-potty; low confidence on its dimensions. Basement window fl-w6 sits in the concrete foundation, not in the sided wall area.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and shows a single gable-end wall square-on with the 351" WALL REF line.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: No siding installed (Tyvek wrap), so no courses to count; measured pixel scale from the on-plane 351" WALL REF line (~990 px = 351 in, ~2.82 px/in) and read wrap-bottom (siding start) to rake/eave intersection at the wall corners as ~335-340 px ≈ 119 in ≈ 10 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: New construction, Tyvek house wrap only — no siding, so course counting is impossible; eave read is a same-plane pixel-scale measurement anchored to the 351" WALL REF (rule 6 satisfied, on-plane). Wall span ≈ 351 in ≈ 29.3 ft. Main gable is one tall story; an upper second-story section with its own roof and a small window (left-w2) sits behind/above at the viewer's left, on a set-back plane — its window size is a rough estimate and along_wall_ft is null because it is not on the WALL REF plane. left-w1 is an angled unit under the gable (rectangular lite + trapezoid lite following the roofline); WIN REF = 48" appears to correspond to one lite span, whole unit ≈ 96 in wide. Exposed CMU/parged foundation band (~30-36 in tall) visible below the wrap start line — EXCLUDED from eave height per rule 5. Prompt says index 2 but this image matches the 'Photo 3: LEFT ELEVATION' annotation set (WALL REF 351, WIN REF 48) — index reported as instructed.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-LEFT ELEVATION and the photo shows a corner view of the back wall (patio slab, sliding doors) plus the left-side one-story gabled wing.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: No siding installed so no course counting; scaled from the same-plane WALL REF = 186" line at the base of the wing wall and the ~80" sliding door on that plane — eave sits roughly 25-30" above the door head, giving ~9 ft siding-start-to-eave for the one-story wing. Two-story section eave not directly measurable from this angle (null course data).
_ai_photos.notes: NOTE: annotation manifest for 'Photo 3' says LEFT ELEVATION with WALL REF=351", but THIS image's overlays read WALL REF=186" and WIN REF=31", matching the rear-left description — readings anchored to the on-image 186"/31" refs. House is pre-siding (Tyvek HomeWrap only), so no siding profile, no course counting, and siding color is unsampleable. Exposed foundation band (~18-24" of dark waterproofed foundation) visible below the wrap on the wing wall — excluded from eave height per rule 5. Clerestory band rl-w7 sits in the wing gable, sized via same-plane proportion only (WIN REF plane differs) so confidence on it is lower. Window positions along_wall_ft mostly null due to corner foreshortening.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is annotated 'BACK ELEVATION' and shows a full fronto-parallel rear wall with patio slab and rear yard.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: No siding installed (Tyvek wrap only) so course counting is impossible; measured with the in-plane WALL REF = 360" (~775 px, ~2.15 px/in): upper eave line to estimated wall base is ~530 px ≈ 246 in ≈ 20.5 ft for the two-story section; the one-story wing eave reads ~10 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: New construction, all walls in Tyvek house wrap — no siding profile or siding color exists yet, so course counting was impossible and eave height comes from the in-plane 360" wall reference. WIN REF = 73" matches the width of the upper-right window (back-w2) and was used to tighten window sizing (~2.33 px/in per-window). Main roof and wing roofs are low-slope standing-seam metal (shed style, no gables). Lower-level wall base at left bump-out sits close to grade; main-wall base behind patio slab estimated, so start_line_occluded set true and confidence reduced. Total rear span including one-story wing ≈ 48-49 ft; upper two-story wall itself spans the 360" (30 ft) reference.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION and the annotations (WALL REF = 171", WIN REF = 60") match the rear-right photo callout; the corner view shows the back wall and right wall of a wrapped new-construction house.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: No siding courses exist (house wrap only). Scaled from the WIN REF 60" bar near the rear entry door on the back-wall plane: door-top to upper shed-roof eave measures roughly 10 ft, door assembly ~7 ft, plus ~2 ft of wall below door sill to the apparent siding start line above the black waterproofing membrane — approx 19 ft to the two-story rear eave. Perspective compression makes this a moderate-confidence estimate only.
_ai_photos.notes: New construction: walls are Tyvek house wrap only, so no siding profile, siding color, or course counting is possible. Exposed foundation below the siding start (black waterproofing membrane + green Kingspan GreenGuard board, roughly 3-4 ft tall on the downhill side) is EXCLUDED from the eave height per rule 5. Roofs are shallow standing-seam metal sheds — no gables or dormers. The WALL REF 171" red line runs along the base of the rear glass bump-out and can anchor horizontal scale on that plane; the WIN REF 60" blue bar sits near the rear entry door and was used for vertical scale on the back-wall plane. Large Pella glass units on the bump-out may be fixed picture units rather than operable sliders — style confidence lowered. Neighboring brick house at right rear is not part of the subject and was excluded.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is annotated 'RIGHT ELEVATION' and shows a single wall plane straight-on with WALL REF = 351" matching the right-elevation annotation set.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: No siding installed (Tyvek wrap) so no course count possible; used the in-plane WALL REF = 351" (975 px ≈ 2.78 px/in) to measure siding-start line (red line, top of foundation) up to the lower main eave ≈ 305 px ≈ 110" ≈ 9.2 ft; the taller clerestory section eave measures ≈ 555 px ≈ 200" ≈ 16.6 ft (shed-roof design, multiple eave heights).
_ai_photos.notes: New construction — walls are Tyvek-wrapped with no siding installed, so no course counting and no siding color sample. Basement/foundation level below the WALL REF line is CMU block with black waterproofing membrane and green Kingspan insulation board on the right — excluded from siding area per rule 5. Right-w2 and right-w3 lie beyond the right end of the 351" wall ref arrow; their along_wall values are extrapolations (right-w3 left null). Roof design is modern shed style with two eave heights (~9.2 ft lower band, ~16.6 ft clerestory); no gable triangles. Windows sized using in-plane WIN REF = 30" on right-w1; other openings scaled from the wall ref. Heavy tree occlusion lowers confidence on the right half of the wall.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels this FRONT-RIGHT ELEVATION and both the front wall (garage door, window band) and the right wall (brick lower level, hatched NO SIDING zone) recede in a corner view.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: No siding installed (Tyvek housewrap) so no course counting possible; estimated grade-to-eave via pixel scale from the 30" WIN REF on the lower right-wall plane, projected up to the cantilevered upper-story eave which sits on a different (overhanging) plane — cross-plane, low confidence.
_ai_photos.notes: House is under construction: walls are Tyvek housewrap with no siding installed, so no lap courses to count and profile callouts are empty. Split-level/mid-century form with cantilevered upper story over a brick/CMU lower level; raised clerestory roof section with small windows (not a dormer). WIN REF = 30" is placed on the right-wall lower brick plane; upper-story eave estimate used it cross-plane and should be treated as low confidence. Portable toilet and trees partially occlude the front lower wall; sizes/positions of upper window band are rough due to foreshortening.

measurements._ai_roof_type_reasoning: Modern low-slope shed/clerestory roof with a true gable end visible only on the left wall (photos 1, 2, 3); no dormers observed on any photo, and 'hip' is wrong because a rake triangle exists, so the ternary forces 'gable' at low confidence.

raw_ai.reference_used: Per-photo taped WALL REF spans (photo 0: 273", photo 2: 351", photo 4: 360", photo 6: 351") for wall geometry; WIN REF bars (80"/110"/48"/31"/73"/60"/30") for opening sizes. House is pre-siding (Tyvek) so no course counts exist anywhere.
raw_ai.story_count_reasoning: 6 of 8 photos read 2 stories (split-level with brick/CMU walkout lower level and cantilevered upper story); photo 1's single-story read reflects only the wing it framed.
raw_ai.roof_type_reasoning: Modern low-slope shed/clerestory roof with a true gable end visible only on the left wall (photos 1, 2, 3); no dormers observed on any photo, and 'hip' is wrong because a rake triangle exists, so the ternary forces 'gable' at low confidence.

walls.label: front
walls.confidence_reasoning: Straight-on photo 0 with same-plane 273" WALL REF, corroborated within 1 ft by photo 1; brick lower level (NO SIDING) and clerestory band complicate area but not the eave read.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct front elevation, same-plane WALL REF 273", siding-start to main eave
kept — front-plane WALL REF 209" on-plane read from corner view; agrees within 0.9 ft

rejected — eave_scale_cross_plane=true (30" WIN REF on lower brick plane projected to cantilevered upper eave) AND it measured grade-to-eave including the excluded brick level; same-plane readings exist

walls._reconciliation_note: Two valid same-plane readings (9.4, 10.3) agree within ±1 ft; no count-derived reading exists (Tyvek only), so median 9.9 kept; photo 7's 17 ft cross-plane read discarded per 79j.67(c). Width ~41 ft from photo 0 opening extents (garage right edge @~48.5 ft-in span ≈ 40-41 ft); the 273" ref covered only part of the span.

walls.label: left
walls.confidence_reasoning: Square-on left elevation with full-span 351" WALL REF on-plane; only one direct eave reading exists so it cannot be cross-checked.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — square-on, same-plane 351" WALL REF
rejected for eave — its 10.3 ft reading was the FRONT plane; left wall strongly foreshortened; rake informed pitch only
rejected for eave — its 9.0 ft reading was the rear wing on the back plane; 3.5 ft triangle is the wing gable, not the main left gable

walls._reconciliation_note: Single valid direct reading (photo 2, 10.0 ft) kept as direct_single_reading; gable triangle 5.5 ft taken from the square-on gable-end photo 2 over photo 1/3's foreshortened 4.0/3.5 reads. Width = full-span 351" ref = 29.3 ft.

walls.label: back
walls.confidence_reasoning: Two-story rear eave read from in-plane 360" WALL REF but the wall base is occluded (start_line_occluded=true on both rear photos) and the two direct attempts disagree by 1.5 ft.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — fronto-parallel back elevation, in-plane 360" WALL REF; strongest reasoning despite occluded base
rejected — corner view with perspective compression, start line occluded, WIN-REF chained estimate; disagrees with photo 4 by 1.5 ft (>1 ft, no averaging)
not applicable to main back wall — measured the one-story wing eave only; retained as the wing's secondary eave height, not merged

walls._reconciliation_note: 20.5 vs 19.0 disagree by 1.5 ft so NOT averaged; photo 4 (direct elevation, in-plane wall ref) outranks photo 5 (corner compression) → 20.5 kept, flagged direct_disagreement. Multi-eave shed design: one-story wing eave ~9-10 ft (photos 3, 4). Width 48.5 ft = photo 4's stated total rear span; the 360" ref covered only the two-story portion, hence direct_single_reading.

walls.label: right
walls.confidence_reasoning: Straight-on right elevation with in-plane 351" WALL REF but heavy tree occlusion; only one valid eave reading and the shed design carries a second ~16.6 ft clerestory eave band.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — straight-on, same-plane 351" WALL REF, siding-start to lower main eave; clerestory band separately reads ~16.6 ft
rejected — eave_scale_cross_plane=true and same-plane reading exists (79j.67(c)); its 17 ft roughly corroborates the clerestory band, not the primary eave

walls._reconciliation_note: Photo 6's same-plane 9.2 ft kept as the primary (lower) eave, direct_single_reading; photo 7 cross-plane read discarded. Width: 351" ref = 29.3 ft but photo 6 notes openings extend beyond the ref's right end (right-w2 @30 ft) → ~33 ft, not assumed symmetric with left.

openings._reconciliation_note: photo 0 at 7.8 ft (85×64, branch-clipped left edge) and photo 1 at 4.6 ft (fl-w3, 110×96) physically overlap on the front wall → same unit; width 110 kept (anchored to photo 1's WIN REF=110"), height 64 kept from straight-on photo 0 since photo 1 flagged its own height as perspective-uncertain; photo 7's front-w5 twin (60×70 @9, foreshortened) folded in as a re-read.
photo 0 at 17.4 ft (36×84), photo 1 at 19.0 ft (36×80) → Δ1.6 ft ≤ 2 ft, same wall/type/width → same door; 4" height delta attributed to photo 1 corner perspective, straight-on 84" kept.
photo 1's fl-w5 (140"-wide '3-Lite Slider' @26 ft) and photo 7's foreshortened casement reads are corner views of this three-unit band @27.3-36.6 ft; band kept as three units per straight-on photo 0.
photo 1 fl-w4 (48×30 @15) and photo 7 front-w6-clerestory (48×24 @8, offset origin) are corner re-reads of the clerestory awning band; straight-on photo 0 sizes kept.
twin of front-w7 by size; kept both — photo 0 placed them at 26.8 and 32.2 ft, Δ5.4 ft > sum-of-widths ~3.3 ft.
NOT deduped against front-w10 (photo 0 at 5.6 ft, photo 1 at 3.5 ft — Δ2.1 ft > 2 ft AND width Δ22" > 3"); both kept per rule 3; foundation-level opening.

single garage opening: photo 0 straight-on WALL-REF read 192×96 @32.5 kept; photo 1 (108×84 @24) and photo 7 (108×84 @19) are corner reads whose foreshortening compresses width and whose origins are offset — rejected sizes, retained as confirmations.
projecting box/bay on the lower front seen only in photo 7; no photo-0 counterpart within size tolerance, kept as distinct; position uses photo 7's offset origin — treat as approximate.
twin pair with fl-w2 preserved; along_wall_ft null (foreshortened left wall) so no cross-photo dedup attempted per rule 3c.
twin of fl-w1 — both kept.
wing-gable clerestory band; along null, kept as-is per rule 3c.

only one large 3-lite patio unit exists on the rear: photo 4 (direct, 155×110 @13) kept; photo 5's back-pd1 (110×96 @22) is the same unit compressed by the corner angle with a right-biased origin — sizes rejected, confirmation retained.

photo 3 rl-pd1 (72×80 @4, origin at the wing's 186" WALL REF ≈15 ft right of the true left corner) and photo 4 (70×80 @19) match within ±3" both dims; the position delta is a coordinate-frame offset, not two doors → merged, photo 4 position kept.
width anchored to photo 4's WIN REF = 73".
along_wall_ft null and no photo-4 unit within ±3" (nearest back-d3 90×92) — kept per rule 3c.
width anchored to photo 3's WIN REF = 31"; along null → NOT collapsed against photo 5's 40×60 casement trio per rule 3c (width Δ9" also fails); possible overlap flagged in openings_dedup.
white rear service door seen only in photo 5; distinct from the patio sliders — kept.
trio at 22/26/29 ft — adjacent units sharing studs (Δ~3.5-4 ft ≈ sum of widths), all three preserved as separate units per twin rule 3e.
width anchored to photo 6's WIN REF = 30".
matched pair with right-w1 (same 30" width) at 17.5 and 30.0 ft — both preserved, Δ12.5 ft >> sum-of-widths.
bump-out right face; along null and photo 6's tree occlusion covers this area — kept per rule 3c.

_reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Confidence-weighted average of final per-wall eaves (front 9.9@75, left 10.0@78, back 20.5@60-weight, right 9.2@55) = 12.2 ft; contributing direct readings were photos 0,1 (front), 2 (left), 4 (back), 6 (right); rejected: photo 5 (corner compression, 19.0 disagreed >1 ft with photo 4's direct 20.5), photo 7 (cross-plane WIN-REF scale, 17.0), photo 3 (measured the one-story wing, not the main back wall). No course counts exist anywhere — house is pre-siding Tyvek.
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: 0 of 8 photos saw conventional gables on 2+ walls; the roof is a low-slope shed/clerestory design (photos 0,4,5,6,7) with one true gable end on the left wall (photos 1,2,3, pitch ~3-4/12), and dormers_observed_count = 0 on all 8 photos → forced ternary resolves to 'gable' at 0.45 confidence.
_reconciliation_notes.dormers: Detected 0 dormers — all 8 photos report dormers_observed_count = 0; the raised clerestory/monitor band (photos 0,1,6,7) is a full-width roof feature, not a dormer, so dormers[] is empty.
_reconciliation_notes.story_count: Photos 0,2,3,4,5,6,7 read 2 stories (split-level with brick/CMU walkout lower level); photo 1's '1' reflected only the wing it framed → 2.
_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: All walls are Tyvek (future siding) except excluded NO SIDING zones: front brick ~280 sqft (photo 0), right brick+CMU ~270 sqft (photos 6,7), left/rear CMU foundation bands (photos 1,3,5) → per-wall 60/92/90/70 → area-weighted ~78%.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: No siding or trim exists yet (pre-siding construction) → nulls, never confabulated; roof #7A7E82 is the median of four sunlit standing-seam samples (photos 1,3,4,5); door #5C6662 from photo 0 (sunlit straight-on front door) preferred over photo 7's mixed-light sample.
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: 61 raw openings across 8 photos → 48 after dedup (13 collapsed). Key merges: front entry door (photo 0 @17.4 ft vs photo 1 @19.0 ft, Δ1.6 ft); front twin casement (photo 0 @7.8 ft 85" vs photo 1 @4.6 ft 110" — spans overlap, WIN-REF-110 width kept); garage door (photos 0/1/7 → one, straight-on 192×96 kept over foreshortened 108×84 corner reads); photo 7's five foreshortened front reads folded into photo 0's straight-on band; rear 3-lite patio (photo 4 @13 vs photo 5 @22, right-biased origin); rear 70×80 slider (photo 3 rl-pd1 @4 on wing-offset origin ↔ photo 4 @19, sizes match ±3"). NOT deduped per rule 3c (null along_wall_ft): photo 1's left-wall 30×34 twins, photo 3's rear casement trio (31-36" wide, WIN-REF-31 anchored) vs photo 5's 40×60 trio — POSSIBLE overlap flagged but positions unverifiable so both sets retained (false duplicates cost less than lost twins); fl-w6 vs front-w10 kept separate (Δ2.1 ft and Δ22" width both fail tolerance). NOTE: Phase A extractions carried no bboxes, so all bbox fields are zeroed placeholders.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is annotated FRONT ELEVATION and shows the entry door, driveway and garage door face-on.
photos.eave_reasoning: No siding installed (Tyvek only) so no course counting possible; measured siding-start (top of brick lower level, ~y745) to main eave (~y458) = ~287 px at 2.53 px/in from the same-plane WALL REF 273" = ~113 in ≈ 9.4 ft. This is the upper Tyvek band only; brick lower level excluded per rule 5.
photos.notes: New construction, no siding installed — house wrapped in Tyvek, so all profile/color siding fields are empty/null and no course count is possible. Vertical eave read used the same-plane WALL REF 273" (690 px, ~2.53 px/in). WIN REF 80" blue bar sits at the right window group; window dims cross-checked against it (~2.7 px/in) and agree with the wall scale within ~8%. Lower walkout level is brick (marked NO SIDING) with garage; upper Tyvek band is the sidable area, plus a raised clerestory band (~y360-460) above the main eave that will also take siding (~44 ft x ~2.5 ft plus box faces). Porta-potty occludes part of the garage door; overhanging branches partially obscure front-w1.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads FRONT-LEFT ELEVATION and the photo shows two walls meeting at a corner, with the entry/garage face (front) and a long wrapped wall (left) both visible.
photos.eave_reasoning: No siding installed (Tyvek wrap only) so no course counting possible; scaled on the front wall plane using WALL REF = 209" (~320 px => ~1.53 px/in), measuring from the siding start line (top of exposed concrete foundation, where WALL REF is drawn) up to the front eave fascia (~190 px => ~124 in ~ 10.3 ft). Shed-style roof means eave height varies along the wall; this is the lower front eave.
photos.notes: New construction — walls are Tyvek/OSB only, no siding installed, so all profile/siding-color fields are empty and no course counting is possible. Exposed poured-concrete foundation band (~3-4 ft, taller downhill) is visible below the siding start line and was excluded from eave height per rule 5. Roof is a low-slope shed/modern design with a raised clerestory monitor section; no true gables or dormers. fl-w3 sizing anchored to WIN REF = 110" (its width matches the blue reference span); its height read of ~96-129 in is uncertain due to vertical perspective — flagged for reconciliation with the straight-on front photo. fl-g1 is an uninstalled garage/lower-level opening partially blocked by a porta-potty; low confidence on its dimensions. Basement window fl-w6 sits in the concrete foundation, not in the sided wall area.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is labeled LEFT ELEVATION and shows a single gable-end wall square-on with the 351" WALL REF line.
photos.eave_reasoning: No siding installed (Tyvek wrap), so no courses to count; measured pixel scale from the on-plane 351" WALL REF line (~990 px = 351 in, ~2.82 px/in) and read wrap-bottom (siding start) to rake/eave intersection at the wall corners as ~335-340 px ≈ 119 in ≈ 10 ft.
photos.notes: New construction, Tyvek house wrap only — no siding, so course counting is impossible; eave read is a same-plane pixel-scale measurement anchored to the 351" WALL REF (rule 6 satisfied, on-plane). Wall span ≈ 351 in ≈ 29.3 ft. Main gable is one tall story; an upper second-story section with its own roof and a small window (left-w2) sits behind/above at the viewer's left, on a set-back plane — its window size is a rough estimate and along_wall_ft is null because it is not on the WALL REF plane. left-w1 is an angled unit under the gable (rectangular lite + trapezoid lite following the roofline); WIN REF = 48" appears to correspond to one lite span, whole unit ≈ 96 in wide. Exposed CMU/parged foundation band (~30-36 in tall) visible below the wrap start line — EXCLUDED from eave height per rule 5. Prompt says index 2 but this image matches the 'Photo 3: LEFT ELEVATION' annotation set (WALL REF 351, WIN REF 48) — index reported as instructed.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-LEFT ELEVATION and the photo shows a corner view of the back wall (patio slab, sliding doors) plus the left-side one-story gabled wing.
photos.eave_reasoning: No siding installed so no course counting; scaled from the same-plane WALL REF = 186" line at the base of the wing wall and the ~80" sliding door on that plane — eave sits roughly 25-30" above the door head, giving ~9 ft siding-start-to-eave for the one-story wing. Two-story section eave not directly measurable from this angle (null course data).
photos.notes: NOTE: annotation manifest for 'Photo 3' says LEFT ELEVATION with WALL REF=351", but THIS image's overlays read WALL REF=186" and WIN REF=31", matching the rear-left description — readings anchored to the on-image 186"/31" refs. House is pre-siding (Tyvek HomeWrap only), so no siding profile, no course counting, and siding color is unsampleable. Exposed foundation band (~18-24" of dark waterproofed foundation) visible below the wrap on the wing wall — excluded from eave height per rule 5. Clerestory band rl-w7 sits in the wing gable, sized via same-plane proportion only (WIN REF plane differs) so confidence on it is lower. Window positions along_wall_ft mostly null due to corner foreshortening.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is annotated 'BACK ELEVATION' and shows a full fronto-parallel rear wall with patio slab and rear yard.
photos.eave_reasoning: No siding installed (Tyvek wrap only) so course counting is impossible; measured with the in-plane WALL REF = 360" (~775 px, ~2.15 px/in): upper eave line to estimated wall base is ~530 px ≈ 246 in ≈ 20.5 ft for the two-story section; the one-story wing eave reads ~10 ft.
photos.notes: New construction, all walls in Tyvek house wrap — no siding profile or siding color exists yet, so course counting was impossible and eave height comes from the in-plane 360" wall reference. WIN REF = 73" matches the width of the upper-right window (back-w2) and was used to tighten window sizing (~2.33 px/in per-window). Main roof and wing roofs are low-slope standing-seam metal (shed style, no gables). Lower-level wall base at left bump-out sits close to grade; main-wall base behind patio slab estimated, so start_line_occluded set true and confidence reduced. Total rear span including one-story wing ≈ 48-49 ft; upper two-story wall itself spans the 360" (30 ft) reference.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner reads REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION and the annotations (WALL REF = 171", WIN REF = 60") match the rear-right photo callout; the corner view shows the back wall and right wall of a wrapped new-construction house.
photos.eave_reasoning: No siding courses exist (house wrap only). Scaled from the WIN REF 60" bar near the rear entry door on the back-wall plane: door-top to upper shed-roof eave measures roughly 10 ft, door assembly ~7 ft, plus ~2 ft of wall below door sill to the apparent siding start line above the black waterproofing membrane — approx 19 ft to the two-story rear eave. Perspective compression makes this a moderate-confidence estimate only.
photos.notes: New construction: walls are Tyvek house wrap only, so no siding profile, siding color, or course counting is possible. Exposed foundation below the siding start (black waterproofing membrane + green Kingspan GreenGuard board, roughly 3-4 ft tall on the downhill side) is EXCLUDED from the eave height per rule 5. Roofs are shallow standing-seam metal sheds — no gables or dormers. The WALL REF 171" red line runs along the base of the rear glass bump-out and can anchor horizontal scale on that plane; the WIN REF 60" blue bar sits near the rear entry door and was used for vertical scale on the back-wall plane. Large Pella glass units on the bump-out may be fixed picture units rather than operable sliders — style confidence lowered. Neighboring brick house at right rear is not part of the subject and was excluded.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is annotated 'RIGHT ELEVATION' and shows a single wall plane straight-on with WALL REF = 351" matching the right-elevation annotation set.
photos.eave_reasoning: No siding installed (Tyvek wrap) so no course count possible; used the in-plane WALL REF = 351" (975 px ≈ 2.78 px/in) to measure siding-start line (red line, top of foundation) up to the lower main eave ≈ 305 px ≈ 110" ≈ 9.2 ft; the taller clerestory section eave measures ≈ 555 px ≈ 200" ≈ 16.6 ft (shed-roof design, multiple eave heights).
photos.notes: New construction — walls are Tyvek-wrapped with no siding installed, so no course counting and no siding color sample. Basement/foundation level below the WALL REF line is CMU block with black waterproofing membrane and green Kingspan insulation board on the right — excluded from siding area per rule 5. Right-w2 and right-w3 lie beyond the right end of the 351" wall ref arrow; their along_wall values are extrapolations (right-w3 left null). Roof design is modern shed style with two eave heights (~9.2 ft lower band, ~16.6 ft clerestory); no gable triangles. Windows sized using in-plane WIN REF = 30" on right-w1; other openings scaled from the wall ref. Heavy tree occlusion lowers confidence on the right half of the wall.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels this FRONT-RIGHT ELEVATION and both the front wall (garage door, window band) and the right wall (brick lower level, hatched NO SIDING zone) recede in a corner view.
photos.eave_reasoning: No siding installed (Tyvek housewrap) so no course counting possible; estimated grade-to-eave via pixel scale from the 30" WIN REF on the lower right-wall plane, projected up to the cantilevered upper-story eave which sits on a different (overhanging) plane — cross-plane, low confidence.
photos.notes: House is under construction: walls are Tyvek housewrap with no siding installed, so no lap courses to count and profile callouts are empty. Split-level/mid-century form with cantilevered upper story over a brick/CMU lower level; raised clerestory roof section with small windows (not a dormer). WIN REF = 30" is placed on the right-wall lower brick plane; upper-story eave estimate used it cross-plane and should be treated as low confidence. Portable toilet and trees partially occlude the front lower wall; sizes/positions of upper window band are rough due to foreshortening.

_corner_location_audit.residual_note: Corner-location residual (79j.91): presence guarantee means phantoms are flagged for field check, never auto-deleted; omission by the model remains the primary risk and is only caught against a field-counted key.

warnings.detail: Rakes 31 LF · Eaves 122 LF · a low ratio often means HOVER missed a rake run
