measurements._ai_reference_used: Taped WALL REFs (324" front/back, 444" left, 180" right pop-up) cross-checked against 3.75" lap-course counts on photos 0, 1, 3, 4, 7
measurements._ai_story_count_reasoning: Six of eight photos reported 1.5 stories (shed-dormer half-story over a 1-story main body); only photo 4's face-on gable view read 1, and it noted attic space at the peak.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is annotated 'FRONT ELEVATION' and shows a full-face gable wall with garage doors and entry door.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses at the front-left corner from the siding start line (visible at grade beside the left garage door) up to the side-eave/soffit return: ~32 courses × 3.75" exposure = 120" ≈ 10.0 ft. Wall-ref pixel scale (324" = ~1105 px) gives a consistent ~121".
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-front garage wall, 324" (27 ft) wide per wall ref. Upper 2SL window contractor-tagged; sized via 48" WIN REF on same plane. Left garage door possibly a 9x7 nominal (pixel read 97x73" of the opening face). Stone steps/retaining wall at right edge abut but do not cover the front wall siding. Roof plane not visible from this face — roof_hex null. Side wings/lower roofs visible flanking the gable belong to left/right elevations.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-LEFT and the shot shows the long left wall receding with garage doors on the front wall at right.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses at 3.75" exposure on the left wall from the siding start line (visible at the rear end near the downspout/AC unit) up to the main eave: ~29 courses x 3.75 in = ~109 in = 9.1 ft. Woodpile hides the start line mid-wall but it is exposed at the rear end, so count anchored there.
_ai_photos.notes: Photo's on-image refs (WALL REF = 48", WIN REF = 36") match the Photo 2 annotation set despite index 1 label; WIN REF sits on a dormer window so it was used only for dormer-plane window sizing per cross-plane rule. Eave height taken by course counting, not pixel scale. Front wall heavily foreshortened and backlit — garage door widths are approximate. Brick section at far right may be a chimney chase or attached structure at the front-right; along_wall_ft for front-upper-w1 left null due to foreshortening. Firewood stack prevents verifying any additional low openings on the left wall.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads LEFT ELEVATION and the photo matches the described 444" wall ref with 3 DH + 2 2SL pins.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Lap courses not countable in the deeply shaded/backlit dark-red siding, so measured pixel-scale using the same-plane 444" WALL REF (~2.93 px/in): siding start visible at the front-left corner (~y865) to eave line (~y595) ≈ 270 px ≈ 92 in ≈ 7.7 ft. Not measurable by course count because courses are indistinct in shadow.
_ai_photos.notes: 36" WIN REF sits on the shed-dormer window plane, so it was used ONLY for the two dormer 2SL windows; main-wall DH windows and eave height were scaled from the same-plane 444" WALL REF. Brick visible at photo right belongs to the rear/back wall (per Photo 5 NO SIDING note), not this left wall, so it is excluded here. Siding start line is occluded mid-wall by the firewood pile but clearly visible at the front-left corner, which anchored the eave measurement. Backlighting makes the sampled siding color darker than true; prefer a sunlit sample from another photo.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Corner shot showing the gabled back wall (patio door) and the long left wall receding right, matching the REAR-LEFT banner and the 48"/36" refs assigned to photo 4.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~30 lap courses at 3.75" exposure from the visible siding start line (above the dark foundation band) up to the side eave at the rear-left corner: 30 × 3.75 = 112.5 in ≈ 9.4 ft. Foundation band below siding start excluded per rule 5.
_ai_photos.notes: Sunlit rear-left corner. Dark foundation band (~1-2 ft) visible below siding start at the patio door area — excluded from eave height per rule 5. Upper left-wall section reads as a large shed-dormer/half-story with its own eave; WALL REF 48" red bar sits at that upper eave (cross-plane to main wall, so not used — course count used instead). WIN REF 36" applies to the upper dormer window plane only. Along-wall positions for the far-forward left-wall window left null due to woodpile/truck occlusion and heavy foreshortening.

_ai_photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is banner-labeled BACK ELEVATION and shows a full gable-end wall with the 324" wall reference matching the back-elevation annotation.
_ai_photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses at 3.75" exposure from the visible siding start line up to the eave/soffit intersection at the photo-left (rear-right) house corner: ~30 courses × 3.75" = 112.5" ≈ 9.4 ft.
_ai_photos.notes: Gable-end wall; gable triangle is coplanar with the main back wall, so the WIN REF 36" on the gable window is same-plane for opening sizing. Gable window value confirmed against WIN REF (95 px = 36"). Peak window suggests attic space but no clear second story. Roof surface not visible from this angle (only rake/fascia). Bottom-left siding start partly hidden by furniture/plants but start line was traceable near the patio door where the count was anchored.

The WIN REF 36" sits on the pop-up (clerestory/oversized shed-dormer) window plane and was used ONLY for that window; lower-wall openings were sized via course-count/proportion since the ref is cross-plane to the main wall. raw_ai.reference_used: Taped WALL REFs (324" front/back, 444" left, 180" right pop-up) cross-checked against 3.75" lap-course counts on photos 0, 1, 3, 4, 7
raw_ai.story_count_reasoning: Six of eight photos reported 1.5 stories (shed-dormer half-story over a 1-story main body); only photo 4's face-on gable view read 1, and it noted attic space at the peak.
raw_ai.roof_type_reasoning: Gable triangles confirmed on front (photos 0, 6) and back (photos 3, 4, 5) with ridge running front-to-back, and shed dormers observed on the left slope (photos 1, 2, 3) and right slope (photo 7 pop-up, corroborated by photo 6's elevated right-wall corner).

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct LEFT elevation, dormer face parallel to camera, posts located at 0.29/0.70 of the 444" wall ref
rejected for width — front-left corner shot, left wall receding/foreshortened; corroborates face and count
rejected for width — rear-left corner shot, left wall strongly foreshortened; corroborates face and count

dormers._reconciliation_note: Only photo 2 viewed this dormer face-on, so its 15 ft read (anchored to the same-plane 444" WALL REF) is kept as a single direct reading; corner-shot reads of 14 ft from photos 1 and 3 corroborate but were rejected for width per the >45° foreshortening rule. Posts at 0.29-0.70 of the 37 ft wall center the dormer, so offset 0.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — taped 180" WALL REF spans the pop-up face directly (15.0 ft); photo reassigned from mislabeled 'front' to right wall (see note)
corroborates existence only — elevated 'raised upper wall above garage roof' corner at 0.45 of right wall; no width read

dormers._reconciliation_note: Photo 7 self-labeled 'front' at only 40% confidence, but the confirmed front (photo 0) is a gable-end with no camera-facing roof slope, while photo 7 shows a slope facing the camera with a shed pop-up — geometrically this must be the right slope (left slope already documented with different scenery in photos 1-3); width 15 ft comes from the taped 180" ref, single direct reading.

walls.label: front
walls.confidence_reasoning: Full-span 324" taped ref plus a 32-course count anchored at a visible start line; pixel scale independently agreed within 1 inch.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — direct front view, 32-course count corroborated by 324" ref pixel scale
eave kept as corroboration (within 1 ft); gable 11.5 ft / 10:12 pitch REJECTED — oblique corner shot exaggerates rise vs photo 0's face-on 7/12
rejected for this wall — photo reassigned to the RIGHT wall (self-label confidence 40, scene and roof geometry inconsistent with the gable-end front)

walls._reconciliation_note: Photos 0 (10.0, 32-course count) and 6 (9.5, door-scaled pixel) agree within 1 ft; count-derived value outranks the pixel read per rule 1d, so 10.0 kept.

walls.label: back
walls.confidence_reasoning: Full-span 324" taped ref with a 30-course count at the rear-right corner, corroborated within 0.9 ft by photo 5's same-plane patio-door scale.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — 30-course count at rear-right corner; face-on gable measured against 324" ref (6/12)
corroboration — same-plane patio-door scale, within 1 ft agreement band but start line occluded; count outranks it
photo 3's eave count belongs to the LEFT wall; its 9.0 ft / 8:12 gable read rejected — oblique corner exaggeration vs photo 4's face-on 7.0 ft

walls._reconciliation_note: Two direct readings (9.4 count, 8.5 pixel) agree within 1 ft; count-derived 9.4 taken per rule 1d. Gable triangle 7.0 from photo 4's face-on ref-scaled read, corroborated by photo 5 (7.5); photo 3's 9.0 rejected as oblique. Brick regions (~41-60 sqft, contractor-hatched NO SIDING) reduce siding to ~85%.

walls.label: left
walls.confidence_reasoning: Full-span 444" taped ref fixes width; two independent course counts (29 and 30) agree, but a conflicting backlit pixel read had to be discarded.

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — 29-course count anchored at rear-left corner where start line is exposed
kept — 30-course count at rear-left corner, foundation band correctly excluded
rejected — backlit pixel-scale read; extractor itself noted courses indistinct in shadow; disagrees >1 ft with both course counts

walls._reconciliation_note: Photo 2's 7.7 ft pixel read disagrees >1 ft with the two course counts (9.1, 9.4) which agree within 1 ft; per rule 1b/1d the count-derived readings win — median of the two counts = 9.3, source flagged direct_disagreement so the discarded 7.7 stays traceable.

walls.label: right

_per_photo_readings.notes: kept — 26-course count, plane-correct, but start line inferred under plantings; only direct reading; photo reassigned here from mislabeled 'front' (self-confidence 40, roof slope facing camera impossible on the gable-end front)
rejected — right wall nearly edge-on in this corner shot, not measurable; corroborates windows and elevated pop-up corner only

walls._reconciliation_note: Single direct course count of 8.1 ft (photo 7, reassigned to this wall); nothing cross-checks it and the start line was inferred, so flagged direct_single_reading. Width 37 ft assumed symmetric with the left wall — no ref covered this span; capture a direct right-side shot before quoting.

openings._reconciliation_note: Contractor-pinned 2SL sized off photo 0's 48" WIN REF at 13.5 ft; photo 6's 22×36 @11 ft and photo 1's 28×44 @null are the same centered gable window shrunk by foreshortening — photo 0's direct elevation saw exactly one front window, so merged despite size deltas.

Photo 0 at 7.1 ft (ref-scaled) matched photo 1 at 8 ft; photo 6's 4 ft position rejected as foreshortened. Photo 1/6's 108×84 reads discarded — both extractors flagged garage widths approximate; photo 0's direct 324" ref read kept. Only 2 doors fit the 27 ft front, forcing this merge.
Photo 0 at 18.2 ft matched photo 1 at 18 ft (Δ0.2 ft); photo 6 at 12 ft rejected as foreshortened; sizes taken from photo 0's direct ref-scaled read.

Photo 0 at 24.4 ft vs photo 6 at 19 ft exceeds the ±2 ft gate, but photo 0's direct elevation shows a single entry door right of the garages; photo 6's position/size (36×80) distorted by the oblique angle — merged with photo 0 authoritative.
Photo 2 at 8.5 ft, photo 3 at 8 ft on left wall → same window (Δ0.5 ft); contractor pin fixes style DH; photo 3's 28×54 read discarded for photo 2's same-plane 444"-ref sizing.
Photo 2 at 17.6 ft, photo 1 at 20 ft (Δ2.4 ft, foreshortened corner view) → same window; contractor pin count of exactly 3 DH on this wall confirms the merge.

Photo 2 at 26.9 ft, photo 1 at 28 ft → same window (Δ1.1 ft); photo 1's 12 ft read and photo 3's null-position rl-left-w3 folded in under the contractor-pinned count of 3 DH — no 4th main-wall window exists.
Dormer twin #1: photo 2 at 14.9 ft, photo 1 at 17 ft, photo 3 at 14 ft → same window; sized off photo 2's dormer-plane 36" WIN REF; contractor pin = 2SL.
Dormer twin #2: photo 2 at 20.7 ft, photo 1 at 22 ft → same window. Twins at 14.9 and 20.7 ft NOT collapsed — separation 5.8 ft > sum-of-widths ~8.2 ft/2; both preserved.
Contractor-pinned; photo 4 at 13.2 ft, photo 3 at 13 ft (read as DH gable window, style overridden by pin), photo 5 at 11.5 ft → same centered upper window; photo 4's same-plane WIN REF sizing kept.
Contractor-pinned; photo 4 at 7.0 ft, photo 3 at 7 ft, photo 5 at 6 ft → same window; oblique size reads (36×36, 48×44) discarded for photo 4's direct read.
All three rear photos report exactly one 72×80 patio door on the back wall (photo 4 at 20.1 ft, photos 3/5 at 16 ft — corner-view position skew); merged, photo 4 direct read authoritative.
along_wall_ft null (foreshortened, shrub-occluded) — kept separate from photo 7's right-wall openings per rule 3c; may overlap, needs a cleaner right-side shot.
Null position — not dedup-eligible; kept.

_reconciliation_notes.avg_wall_height_ft: Confidence-weighted average of final per-wall eaves (front 10.0 @88, back 9.4 @80, left 9.3 @75, right 8.1 @50) = 9.3 ft; left wall's 7.7 ft backlit pixel read (photo 2) and photo 7's 8.1 ft were excluded from front/left math per the disagreement and wall-reassignment rules.
_reconciliation_notes.roof_type: Gable triangles on front (photos 0, 6) and back (photos 3, 4, 5) with eave-side left/right walls establish a front-back ridge gable; dormers_observed_count > 0 in photos 1, 2, 3, 7 upgrades to gable-shed-dormer.
_reconciliation_notes.dormers: Detected 2 shed dormers — one per roof slope. LEFT dormer: face from photo 2's cardinal left view, width 15 ft (single direct reading; photos 1/3 corner reads of 14 ft rejected for width but confirm face/count), centered on the 37 ft wall. RIGHT dormer: photo 7 reassigned from its 40%-confidence 'front' label to the right slope (the confirmed front is a gable-end with no camera-facing slope; left slope scenery doesn't match), width 15 ft from the taped 180" ref (single direct reading), corroborated by photo 6's elevated right-wall corner post. Different-face dormers never collapsed.
_reconciliation_notes.story_count: Median/mode of per-photo reads: 1.5 in photos 0,1,2,3,5,6,7; photo 4's face-on '1' outvoted (its own notes mention peak attic space).
_reconciliation_notes.siding_coverage_pct: All walls lap 4" siding except contractor-hatched brick on/near the back wall (~41-60 sqft across photos 1, 4, 5) against ~1,530 sqft total wall area → ~95%.
_reconciliation_notes.dominant_colors: Photo 5 (sample_quality=sunlit) supplied all four fields, consistent with photos 3 and 4 (also sunlit); photo 2's backlit #6E2A24 siding sample discarded as its own extractor recommended.
_reconciliation_notes.openings_dedup: 40 raw openings across 8 photos → 19 after dedup. Key merges: 2 garage doors + entry door + gable window on front (photo 0 direct elevation authoritative, photos 1/6 foreshortened confirmations); left wall collapsed to the contractor-pinned 3 DH + 2 dormer 2SL twins (twins at 14.9/20.7 ft preserved, not collapsed); back wall collapsed to pinned 2 sliders + 1 patio door seen in photos 3/4/5. Right-wall openings from photos 6 and 7 NOT cross-deduped (photo 6 positions null per rule 3c) — possible over-count there, flagged for a follow-up right-side photo. Phase A extractions carried no bboxes, so bbox fields are zeroed placeholders.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is annotated 'FRONT ELEVATION' and shows a full-face gable wall with garage doors and entry door.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses at the front-left corner from the siding start line (visible at grade beside the left garage door) up to the side-eave/soffit return: ~32 courses × 3.75" exposure = 120" ≈ 10.0 ft. Wall-ref pixel scale (324" = ~1105 px) gives a consistent ~121".
photos.notes: Gable-front garage wall, 324" (27 ft) wide per wall ref. Upper 2SL window contractor-tagged; sized via 48" WIN REF on same plane. Left garage door possibly a 9x7 nominal (pixel read 97x73" of the opening face). Stone steps/retaining wall at right edge abut but do not cover the front wall siding. Roof plane not visible from this face — roof_hex null. Side wings/lower roofs visible flanking the gable belong to left/right elevations.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Banner labels FRONT-LEFT and the shot shows the long left wall receding with garage doors on the front wall at right.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses at 3.75" exposure on the left wall from the siding start line (visible at the rear end near the downspout/AC unit) up to the main eave: ~29 courses x 3.75 in = ~109 in = 9.1 ft. Woodpile hides the start line mid-wall but it is exposed at the rear end, so count anchored there.
photos.notes: Photo's on-image refs (WALL REF = 48", WIN REF = 36") match the Photo 2 annotation set despite index 1 label; WIN REF sits on a dormer window so it was used only for dormer-plane window sizing per cross-plane rule. Eave height taken by course counting, not pixel scale. Front wall heavily foreshortened and backlit — garage door widths are approximate. Brick section at far right may be a chimney chase or attached structure at the front-right; along_wall_ft for front-upper-w1 left null due to foreshortening. Firewood stack prevents verifying any additional low openings on the left wall.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads LEFT ELEVATION and the photo matches the described 444" wall ref with 3 DH + 2 2SL pins.
photos.eave_reasoning: Lap courses not countable in the deeply shaded/backlit dark-red siding, so measured pixel-scale using the same-plane 444" WALL REF (~2.93 px/in): siding start visible at the front-left corner (~y865) to eave line (~y595) ≈ 270 px ≈ 92 in ≈ 7.7 ft. Not measurable by course count because courses are indistinct in shadow.
photos.notes: 36" WIN REF sits on the shed-dormer window plane, so it was used ONLY for the two dormer 2SL windows; main-wall DH windows and eave height were scaled from the same-plane 444" WALL REF. Brick visible at photo right belongs to the rear/back wall (per Photo 5 NO SIDING note), not this left wall, so it is excluded here. Siding start line is occluded mid-wall by the firewood pile but clearly visible at the front-left corner, which anchored the eave measurement. Backlighting makes the sampled siding color darker than true; prefer a sunlit sample from another photo.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Corner shot showing the gabled back wall (patio door) and the long left wall receding right, matching the REAR-LEFT banner and the 48"/36" refs assigned to photo 4.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted ~30 lap courses at 3.75" exposure from the visible siding start line (above the dark foundation band) up to the side eave at the rear-left corner: 30 × 3.75 = 112.5 in ≈ 9.4 ft. Foundation band below siding start excluded per rule 5.
photos.notes: Sunlit rear-left corner. Dark foundation band (~1-2 ft) visible below siding start at the patio door area — excluded from eave height per rule 5. Upper left-wall section reads as a large shed-dormer/half-story with its own eave; WALL REF 48" red bar sits at that upper eave (cross-plane to main wall, so not used — course count used instead). WIN REF 36" applies to the upper dormer window plane only. Along-wall positions for the far-forward left-wall window left null due to woodpile/truck occlusion and heavy foreshortening.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Photo is banner-labeled BACK ELEVATION and shows a full gable-end wall with the 324" wall reference matching the back-elevation annotation.
photos.eave_reasoning: Counted lap courses at 3.75" exposure from the visible siding start line up to the eave/soffit intersection at the photo-left (rear-right) house corner: ~30 courses × 3.75" = 112.5" ≈ 9.4 ft.
photos.notes: Gable-end wall; gable triangle is coplanar with the main back wall, so the WIN REF 36" on the gable window is same-plane for opening sizing. Gable window value confirmed against WIN REF (95 px = 36"). Peak window suggests attic space but no clear second story. Roof surface not visible from this angle (only rake/fascia). Bottom-left siding start partly hidden by furniture/plants but start line was traceable near the patio door where the count was anchored.

photos.elevation_reasoning: Annotation banner reads REAR-RIGHT ELEVATION and the view shows the back gable wall with the right wall wrapping away at the photo-right corner.
photos.eave_reasoning: Individual 3.75" lap courses are not resolvable at this compression for a reliable count; used the patio door (~80" glass/frame, same wall plane) as vertical scale — siding start to eave on the back gable wall ≈ 210 px at ~1.94 px/in ≈ 102-108" (~8.5-9 ft). The 84" WALL REF at the photo-left corner is a different, lower roof plane.
photos.notes: In-photo refs are WALL REF = 84" and WIN REF = 48" (matches the REAR-RIGHT banner, not the prompt's index-5 BACK/324" description — used what is visible in THIS photo). A dark red brick/parged foundation band (~5-8" exposed, more at the corner) is visible below the siding start and was EXCLUDED from eave height per rule 5. Siding start line is partly hidden by shrubs/planter, so start_line_occluded=true. rr-w3 on the tree-obscured left plane is a low-confidence read. Numerous small signs/decorations on the wall are decor, not openings.

_gable_demotion_audit.residual_note: Oblique demotion residual (amended per 79j.89 ruling): lexical demotion catches admitted inflation only where the admission matches the pinned corpus; novel phrasings escape until added; fully silent inflation always passes. Primary defense is independent geometry (square-on preference, corner agreement); lexical demotion is a second net, not a wall.
